name: Good Voyage
reviews: ["Unbelievable gem nestled in the South Park neighborhood. Serving French fare with their own flair, everything we had was delicious, thoughtful, and unlike the normal PNW fare. The staff was knowledgeable, very approachable, and eager to help you make decisions. I'll be back!","Stopped in for brunch on a Sunday around 8:45am. There were a few other patrons at the time. Super welcoming staff. You can’t go wrong with anything on the menu.\n\nWe had the breakfast plate, the gigante beans, and the jambon beurre sandwich. All delicious. We got the lentil salad and yogurt parfait to go. The lentil salad is so good! I’m still thinking about it!!","My new favorite place for brunch, lunch, coffee, happy hour and dinner.  Love this place so much. Everything is delicious, the service is impeccable and the atmosphere is wonderful.","What a warm, bright little gem to happen upon on a drizzly Sunday morning. I expected to grab a coffee & maybe dry off for a few minutes but ended up eating a lovely, very tasty brunch in a warm atmosphere full of happy people. The staff treated everyone like old friends. Highly recommend.","I ordered a bacon, egg, and cheese sandwich and a black coffee—both were solid. The sandwich was fresh and flavorful, and the coffee was smooth. The girl at the front counter was friendly. The overall vibe of the place was nice too—cozy interior with a relaxed atmosphere. Would definitely come back and try it again."]

name: Volunteer Park Cafe & Pantry
reviews: ["Amazing pasties! The breakfast sandwich is skippable (not much flavor), but any of their sweets are great. The chocolate babka muffin was so good!! A bit tough to find seating.","Super cute spot. Came here before hitting the Asian art museum.  Coffee was so good... perfect micro foam! Toast was amazing! It was pretty small inside but there is outside seating as well. Definitely worth a visit. Cookies are super good too!","Stepping inside felt really comforting and homey — the kind of place that instantly gives you that neighborhood vibe. Everything about it felt familiar and welcoming, like somewhere locals actually love to hang out. The staff was friendly and seemed genuinely happy to be there, even while keeping busy with all the orders. It made the whole experience feel effortless and relaxed, like you could take your time and enjoy your meal without feeling rushed. There’s something about the combination of a cozy interior, a friendly team, and that lived-in neighborhood energy that just makes you want to come back again and again. It’s one of those spots where you immediately feel at ease and can enjoy good food or drinks without any fuss.","I’m visiting from England and stopped by for tea – honestly one of the best cups I’ve had in a long time. The flavour was spot on and brewed perfectly. I also tried one of their pastries and it was fresh, warm, and delicious.\nThe cafe has a really cosy atmosphere, friendly service, and a peaceful vibe that makes it a great place to relax. Definitely worth a visit if you’re in the area!","Found this gem while completing a neighborhood stroll.  Such a quaint place.  In the middle of a residential area, tree lined streets.  Street parking.  Limited tables.  Coffee, pastry’s, etc.  pet and kid friendly.  Lines might be long and tables go fast."]

name: Lune Cafe
reviews:
["Very nice cafe! The food was delicious, the atmosphere was cozy, and Jasim provided excellent service. He was very friendly and attentive. I really enjoyed my time here and will definitely come back again. Highly recommend!","Dubai Chocolate Strawberry is so delicious! It's crazy. And the staff is so nice and the service is amazing. The chairs are comfortable and I love this cafe because I'm enjoying the rainy Seattle.","We had such a great experience at Lune Cafe, thanks to our waiter Jasim. He provided excellent service and made the experience feel welcoming and fun. He crafted us amazing soda drinks — I had the strawberry flavor, and it was incredibly flavorful and paired perfectly with the four types of buffet waffles.

This stop truly lifted my boyfriend’s spirits and was the perfect dessert after our dinner at Pike Place. We’re so glad we decided to come here. We also loved being able to play Jenga and Uno while we waited — such a fun and relaxed touch.

Big shoutout to Jasim — we will definitely be back because of him. Highly recommend Lune Cafe for both locals and visitors alike!","My ultimate comfort spot! ♥️ Best drinks, best desserts, and the friendliest staff ever. Prices are super reasonable too! I could come here every day and still not get enough. Truly love everything about this place—10/10, highly recommend! ☕🍰✨","I found this little gem because I was on Instagram and I saw they had this amazing Dubai chocolate spread! I saw it and I had to have it! Some of my best friend and I drove out there and we got the strawberries covered in the Dubai chocolate with other chocolate on top I don’t even know what it is. I just know it was amazing and we went back again and again the staff our students I believe and they are really sweet! I will be back for more!"]

name: Rita’s Cafe
address: Located in, 808 3rd Ave, Seattle, WA 98104, USA
reviews: ["It is super cozy in here (honestly one of the coziest cafes I've been to in awhile), coffee is really good and the owner/staff are so friendly and kind. I'm excited to come back and try their many yummy seasonal drinks and food selections as well. Try Rita's out, you won't be disappointed!","An absolutely enchanting cafe with a warm welcoming vibe and absolutely fantastic goodies! Their Chocolate Orange winter special is a must try!!!","adorable new little spot. love that they have a Lotus menu as well as a coffee menu! barista was so sweet - very helpful and conversational - plus gave me a lollipop and a loyalty card with my drink (which was delicious). i work near here and was just looking for somewhere to kill an hour of time but now i will definitely be going back! it’s a cute space with a few tables and chairs but great that you can sit in the lobby of the central building if the tables fill up inside, too.","Wonderful cafe! Super cute and cozy interior, excellent drink options and preparation. My latte was one of the best I’ve ever had.\n\nThe best part is Rita! So sweet and accommodating. Make this place your regular stop if you work in downtown Seattle!!","Very cute cafe. Service was fantastic and the holiday lattes were incredible. We got the Chocolate Orange and Hazelnut Dream lattes and loved them a ton. Highly recommend."]

name: Stone Way Cafe
address: 3525 Stone Way N, Seattle, WA 98103, USA
reviews:
["This breakfast burrito is well known for both its size and quality, and I can confirm it mostly lives up to those.

You just have to start with how large this is. Apparently it's gotten smaller - which if it has - thank god, because I don't know how I could've finished anything
bigger than this. It's a true behemoth, having heft to that's largely carried by its fillings.

The choice of protein is left to you - and I went with the chorizo. I did think this was probably the weakest part unfortunately. It felt underspiced, a little dry, and
didn't have as much flavor as I was expected. I would go with another meat option if I were to come again.
However, every other part of this burrito was just fantastic

The particular standouts were the combination of the eggs and home fries. Both filled up the insides - with the eggs being a soft scramble that bit through easily, while the home fries being fluffy, seasoned well, and again just super soft. It felt like pure breakfast comfort food.
The cheese was also a great addition - not overly cheesy, but being there just enough to add extra fat to each bite.

I loved the chipotle sauce added, but my minor issue with that was that it was all off to the side of the burrito - it wasn't distributed evenly. What this meant was that if you took a bit of one specific side, you got a ton of the chipotle sauce, but for the other 90% of the burrito, it just wasn't there.

For around $15, this is a pretty amazing price for something you could honestly split between two people. It's one of the best lunch deals I've seen in Seattle.

Overall, this is a huge burrito at a cheap price that tastes very good as well. 8.5/10","wonderful wonderful spot!!! the food is absolutely delicious and well-priced and well- portioned.
I got the cajun benedict and my partner got the waffles. both amazing!
the staff was kind and I love the open air feel in the cafe.","They’ve got a pretty solid brunch here! Seems to be Mexican-inspired. Their breakfast burrito is HUGE! And it’s pretty tasty!! You can find generous portions and delicious bites here. Very PNW vibes and lots of friendly faces. Ordering can be kind of hectic with people up front either standing to order or waiting or putting things away (you buss your own table). It’s a bit chaotic, but once you sit down, it makes for a cool brunch spot. Indoor and outdoor seating available. Finding street parking can be a little tough, especially on the weekends. There is a tiny parking lot out front.","Superrrr tasty! Walked here but there's plenty of parking. Nice big open space with inside and outside seating. Simple menu. Food was amazing 👏🏾 they serve breakfast food all day. I got a breakfast sandwich with chorizo and my wife got the Cuban. Very impressed. The savory muffin was amazing as well! It was toasted.. so yummy. Will need to go back!","Wow and wow. I liked the food. All was excellent. I was full and actually shouldn’t have eaten it all but I did. Didn’t need lunch for sure. Coffee was excellent. We stayed a while. It was pleasant. The bathrooms are clean.  Price is excellent and that’s why this place has a line of customers. Worth your wait.  Did manage to snag a table for four with no wait."]

name: Lost Lake Cafe & Lounge
reviews:
["Tried Lost Lake Cafe & Lounge and really loved the vibe. It’s cozy, lively, and has that classic diner-meets-lounge feel. The service was great and the staff were super friendly.

We ordered the crispy chicken sandwich with fries and a grilled cheese with onion rings. The chicken sandwich was crispy on the outside, juicy on the inside. The fries were well-seasoned too. The grilled cheese was comforting with gooey melted cheese, and the onion rings were crispy but a bit on the greasy side.

Overall, it’s a fun spot, though it does get a little loud during busy hours.","I’ve only had their happy hour meals because they have some of the cheapest meal prices in Capitol Hill, and the vibes are so cozy and fun. Outside of happy hour it’s all regular Seattle prices but probably better food. The staff is kind, and they played Shrek on the tv last time I went. I recommend the quesadillas from the happy hour meals.","I met up with some friends for brunch. I ordered a classic breakfast. It is simple, two eggs over easy, hash browns and biscuit. Eggs cooked perfectly, hash browns crispy on top and bottom, and biscuit baked well. I really like the biscuit, break it in half and heat steam out, crispy top layer and bottom layer, moist in the middle. Service was good and there is 5% service charge. Inside, is classic diner atmosphere.","Host was a little rude. Not sure I understand the 5% percent service charge when she couldn’t bring us paper menus 😭 food was ok cheaper for the area but not with hidden service fee. Mimosa wasn’t even used with fresh orange juice 😩
Also they don’t except cash..

Will I visit again?

We will see. If they get a different host then maybe.

Denver foodie ❤️","Excellent late night food option on Capitol Hill! Open until 3am on weekends! The fried chicken is among the best I’ve ever had, especially with the mashed potatoes and gravy that would make my late grandmother weep with joy (as others have said!). Combined with the GIANT “side salad,” this $21 plate is TWO full meals! Pro tip: take the salad and save some chicken to go and have a chicken salad for lunch the next day!! Service was great, atmosphere was fairly quiet considering the cacophony of Sat night in the Hill! Highly recommended!!"]

name: Bang Bang Cafe
reviews:
["Breakfast burrito made vegan, made beautifully! Supper tasty with vegan egg replacement.

The staff were super kind and helpful.
I would for sure come back on my next visit to town.","My husband and I went here for breakfast yesterday morning for the first time. The place itself is super cute and the staff was friendly. My husband had a breakfast burrito and I had the posole and the focaccia breakfast sandwich. Everything was good, but OMG, the breakfast sandwich was one of the best breakfast sandwiches I've ever had and it'll be living rent-free in my head from now on. We don't live here, but Bang Bang will be a must-visit on future Seattle trips. Also, the pop tart was delicious- get one!","I liked the pozole. It was a nice warming dish with complementary textures, exactly what's needed on yet another rainy day. The place felt cozy, with a variety of pillows to support the back when sitting in the wall area. This is sort of a counter serve, they'll bring the food to your table and you should return the empty dish yourself. The atmosphere could have been better if this was a full service restaurant. It doesn't seem to be that much more of a stretch to remove dishes or fill up a glass of water once. As the food is fine, elevating the experience could be a good strategy to make it more of a destination.","🔥🌮 Bang Bang in Othello — A Hidden Spot with Room to Rise

I love supporting new local businesses hidden in our communities, and I was excited to try Bang Bang in Othello after a friend’s recommendation. The space itself is comfortable, warm, and inviting, with a full bar that makes it feel like a true neighborhood hangout.

The highlight of my visit was the tequila experience—smooth, memorable, and introduced with genuine excitement. That moment showed me the spark of what this place can be when passion and presentation come together.

What I hope for is that the same level of excitement is carried throughout the entire dining experience. The servers have the power to guide guests with joy and confidence, turning a meal into something unforgettable. While I left a little unsure about the menu, I also left believing in the potential.

I truly hope Bang Bang will fire into a destination—because our city needs more local fun, more hidden gems, and more places that light up the soul. I’ll be back, ready to experience the next level.","This cafe has the best vegan options in Seattle. Also they have amazing coffee. The vegan Mac and cheese is insanely good, I’ve had it probably 100 times no exaggeration. Also we had a vegan torta that was so delicious. Cannot recommend this place enough."]

name: Portage Bay Cafe - South Lake Union
address: 391 Terry Ave N, Seattle, WA 98109, USA
reviews:
["This was one of the recommendations of walking distance restaurants for breakfast that my hotel offered. I was one of the first ones to be seated when they opened and the restaurant filled up pretty quickly.

I had the crab Benedict and lemon ricotta pancakes along with a lavender London fog.

The meal was scrumptious. I barely left anything and the portions were hefty. They offer a fruit and nut bar to accompany your meal as well.
I had to walk it off for about an hour after but it was so worth it.

The service is also good and the server was very nice.

The pricing is reasonable. I ate for two people so my bill was a bit high but expected.

When I’m back in Seattle, I will visit this restaurant again and try something different.","This was my third visit to Portage Bay Cafe and my first time sitting at the bar. As on previous visits, the food itself was excellent. I had a grapefruit mimosa and the Apple Cobbler French Toast with gluten-free challah. It arrived hot, beautifully plated, and absolutely delicious.

However, sitting at the bar also means you have a full view of the kitchen, and sometimes that transparency works against the experience. Watching the BOH staff made it clear that glove-changing practices are not great. I realize this is a widespread issue in many restaurants, but it’s still not something diners want to see while eating.

The food and drinks remain great, which is why I’ve come back multiple times. But seeing what happens when the curtain is pulled back changed the experience this time. Had I been seated somewhere without such a clear view behind the scenes, this easily could have been a five-star visit.","Went on a weekend morning and, as expected, it was busy. This location is huge with plenty of tables inside and a few outside with heaters. It definitely helps to have a reservation. For food, we got the French toast, which gives you access to their famous berry bar. I got the eggs Benedict and it was great. Service was pretty quick and attentive considering how packed it was. Parking is limited since you’re in downtown, so keep that in mind.","Service was prompt and friendly. Waiter recommended a seasonal Benedict. Interesting flavors with a little spice. The English muffin was a little tough but the taste made up for it. The cheddar biscuit was delicious! A side of sausage gravy went with and I could have eaten it like soup. Yummy! One of us is gluten free so the lemon ricotta pans were a perfect choice. The restaurant is a little loud that’s why 4 stars on ambiance.","Good location, and atmosphere. Make sure to reserve table in advance for weekends. This place has decent options for vegetarian/vegan food. I ordered Vegan banana pancakes. I liked the fruit bar with all options for toppings like fruits, cream, and nuts, but pancakes were little undercooked and doughy. I had been to this location before and have tried their dish The Farmers Daughter and remember it being good. Might give this cafe another try."]

name: Hangar Cafe
reviews:
["I had brunch here with a girlfriend today (a Sunday), fully knowing that there would be a wait because this is a converted HOUSE with a 1 person kitchen. It is well worth it!

I'm beyond impressed at the quality of food the chef put out, giving full attention to every dish, even under the stress of a busy day. The staff were so patient and kind, even when dealing with disgruntled customers who don't understand that they have a tiny kitchen with 1 chef. I had no place to be and was content waiting but was still offered a drink and coffee cake as an apology for the wait. So sweet.

I had The Market, a savory crepe, which they modified for me with no questions asked. It was delicious. The coffee cake was PERFECT, the mimosa was HUGE and my coffee cup never went empty. I love the outdoor patio. It's so cozy that I could sit out there all day.

The Hangar Cafe is an institution and I can't wait to return!","We stopped by by chance, and I absolutely loved the cozy and warm atmosphere.
The food was delicious, and the service was wonderful.
A perfect spot for a lunch date with my son! 💛
Highly recommended for a relaxing and happy afternoon in Seattle.","Fabulous food and Service! Toni was so nice to every single person walking in and communicated any wait times very effectively. It’s a busy morning and the chef was killing it - we could see from our seat that they were knocking out orders like a pro. Haven’t been to Seattle in over 10 years and I would 100% recommend this place for a great breakfast!","This spot is amazing. I felt like I was on a movie set—the design is absolutely great and wonderfully immersive. I especially enjoyed the crepes; they have an authentic, Eastern European style that was a delightful surprise. The price is affordable and the food is consistently good. Highly recommend checking out the atmosphere alone.","Pretty good brunch and breakfast. I liked the patio setup. Very pretty and it was cool to have the little finches scrambling around having breakfast with us."]

name: Piedmont Café
address: 1215 Seneca St Suite 100, Seattle, WA 98101, USA
reviews: ["This cafe does a beautiful job at faithfully breathing life into the historic lobby of the Piedmont Hotel. The vaulted ceilings, arched windows, furniture and plants make it feel really authentic and homey. I ordered the berry chocolate muffin which was fantastic and the drip coffee was good. WiFi can be a little spotty but besides that it’s a nice place to hang out for a bit.","The staff was super welcoming. I had the Apple turnover and a drip coffee. The man behind the counter said it was a good choice and even offered to heat it up. The coffee cup was very pleasant with a ceramic cup on a saucer. The environment quite homely. That's what we really need in a coffee shop. Homely  super welcoming like a living room much compliments to the staff.","Piedmont Cafe in First Hill is a hidden gem! Loved the coffee (cappuccino was great) and the old school charm vibe. Cozy spot with lush plants and plenty of seating - perfect for work or chill. Slightly less noise would be nice, but overall great experience. Tucked away in Tuscany apartments, it's a fave for creatives - was busy even on a weekend evening. Will return to try the food!","This is a really great place to come if you’re trying to just pass time, relax, chat, or get some work done. There is a ton of seating, it’s fairly quiet, and the drinks are really nice. It’s a pretty popular photo spot, so there were about 3 groups (including me) taking photos when I came, but the staff is really friendly.\n\nThe hot cocoa was really nice and I had a gingerbread latte, the coffee part was great but I wasn’t a huge fan of the gingerbread flavor because it was very spicy. I’d love to try some food next time I go there.","Very pleasantly surprised with this place. The  building and decor were on point. Perfect play to get some work done, people watch, or chat with a friend. The food and coffee were outstanding. I had an almond croissant wheel and it was absolutely perfect. The texture was crunch, crispy, flanky, and soft all at the same time very happy with this choice. I also had the cold brew which was fantastic and served quickly. I’d definitely be back when I’m in the neighborhood"]

name: The 5 Point Cafe
reviews:
["Overall we enjoyed the vibe and the food, but we were disappointed by the service.

The buffalo chicken was spicy and pretty tasty and our burgers had a real authentic homemade taste to them. Delicious!

There was a good selection of alt rock being played which I enjoyed, but it was a little noisy.

Our server wasn’t so great though. Left us waiting to order for a long time (we were almost about the leave when he finally came over) and we also had to wait for what seemed like another eternity to pay the bill.
Don’t know why as the place wasn’t particularly busy.","First time here, loved this place. Super affordable. The food was fresh and came out in speedy manner. I had bourbon grill chicken sandwich. It hit the spot, I was famished. The service was exceptional, we got waiter, comedian and city ambassador all in one. Thank you for the recommendations. There was a lot happening at the venue, good loud music and decor.","Read good reviews but was super disappointed. Food came out cold. The meat on the French dip was over cooked and super hard and when it came out it was soggy on the bottom and super cold. Steak was super fatty and cold as well. Service was great loved the waiter he was awesome. Joked around a bunch and made us feel comfortable. Atmosphere was pretty good. Very much like an old bar. I liked the vulgar statements on the shirts and the posters those were fun. Price was crazy. 154 dollars for 3 peoples food and drinks. This is not a high class place. Should not of costed that much. Liked that it was one to our venu and parking garage. But overall not a very good impression. I would not eat here again. Maybe just drinks.","We were there at 4:45a bc our bodies are on east coast time - so good! Great coffee, pancakes, biscuits & gravy. Really nice service. Entertaining atmosphere, for sure. Worth the time and money - enjoy!","We searched reviews looking for a fantastic hamburger & decided to give 5 point cafe a try. One of  our party members had a valid out of state vertical drivers  license so a 10 minute wait quickly turned into a 30 minute wait. I’m guessing the establishment had trouble with under age dinking previously. The restaurant had an automatic 5 percent add on too , we experienced this during co-vid but it was 25 percent then.my table all ordered hamburger & cheeseburgers but were disappointed. I recommend changing out the fryer grease because the fries were heavily saturated in oil. The hamburger patties had a frozen box feel not a fresh in house grind. The restaurant was freezing due to staff leaving door open to serve patrons on the patio.The restrooms had a might catch a disease vibe some call it grunge, the restaurant overall cleanliness was bad. We won’t be returning to this establishment again…."]

name: Tailwind Café at Good Weather
address: In the Alley, 1424 11th Ave, Seattle, WA 98122, USA
reviews:
["Lovely coffee shop, plenty of seating with some outlets for working on your laptop and great breakfast options. The cardamom Oatmilk cappuccino I ordered was fantastic. They have lots of local event posters for biking, crafting, drag and also have a sister bike shop down the road.","This is a really cozy spot! Everyone’s always super nice and the food is always awesome. I’ve been two or three times in the last year and that’s been consistent too.

It’s a little hidden if you’re new to the area, but there’s plenty of signage once you’re out of the car and on the sidewalk. Fairly accessible, though the doors are a little heavy and there’s no push buttons. Doors open outwards.

On my most recent trip I got the mint tea and a croque madam (fried egg on smoked ham with Parmesan and creamy bechamel sauce and Gruyere on sourdough) with arugula salad and a pickle. Really good. I paid about $26 for my food and drink, and this is a place that doesn’t have a tipping system, it’s all in their wages (stated by sign on menu).

They did remove the space heaters that used to be above the outdoor table, but I’m not sure if that’s a permanent removal or if it’s because it hasn’t been as cold this week. There’s a cute public firepit right next door though, and once I finished my food/returned the dishes I moved there to relax.

There’s a bathroom down a hallway that’s shared with other businesses that has a door code, so make sure to ask if you need it!

Glad to visit once again, and glad I remembered to review this time. 😂","Such a lovely space to grab coffee, a drink, or just hangout! An amazing patio for the summers, and such a cozy vibe for rainy days. I came first time in 2020 and got a coffee and a flat fixed and everyone was so nice. It’s only gotten cuter since then and such a beautiful space. From what I could tell the space is mostly utilized for the cafe but I’m not a local anymore so I’m sure they still provide bike services at good weather as well!","Tailwind Cafe is a nice, low-key spot. The coffee was okay for my taste—but the space itself is the highlight. It’s cozy, comfortable, and a great place to sit and relax for a bit.","The barista was kind, attentive, and able to track the line even when customers arriving didn’t stand in one. He made me an excellent, smooth oatmilk matcha latte and the milk was rich enough that he could shape the foam into a leaf decoration on top. The one type of vegan pastry available was fresh and tasty. The place has a bicycling theme and is massive inside with loads of seating options. Worth walking down the alley (enter from 11th) to reach it. There’s a slight foul smell seeming to emanate from the back of the alley but that’s the only atmospheric ding I noticed."]

name: Biscuit Bitch Pike Place
reviews:
["The food was fantastic! Fresh and homemade 😋.  I had the spicy links bichwich and it is the best breakfast sandwich I've ever had.  Definitely give it a try.
Just an FYI this is not a sit down restaurant. You ordered like you would from a food truck.  They have a few tables along the sidewalk.","Oh my god. This place slaps! I really enjoyed! I was raised in the South in Texas, but let me tell you.. This place is a new stop as a Washington Local now. The biscuits are so soft and warm, they melt in your mouth good. The gravy was just phenomenal and the atmosphere is everything. Try it for yourself!","Good but not great IMO! (Sorry.) The biscuits are definitely the highlight—I’d eat a tray—but the filling was meh. (Sorryyyy.) The London Fog is EXCELLENT though. Compliments to the chef/barista.

Recommend ordering ahead online.","Recommended to come here and try some biscuits. I ordered the biscuits with the gravy and scrambled eggs on top and cheese. It was good and filling. Definitely something different. Also ordered a pumpkin coffee drink but I didn't really like it, tasted watery. Girl at front was very friendly, great service!","Wow!  What a breakfast biscuit!  Arrived at 9 am, so during the breakfast rush and a one hour wait.  But Pike Market is around the corner.  Despite the “name calling” and wide us of “vulgarity”, due to the business name,  these guys were super helpful and friendly. There are a few small tables along the street if you’re able to snag one.  Pictures don’t know this biscuit justice!"]

name: Jollidaze Cafe
reviews: ["The decoration is out of this world it is beautiful definitely come here, not many options for any christmas drinks considering it’s decorated like Christmas blew up here.. as for the service it wasn’t good sorry but for me visiting here for the first time out of state and seeing this on TikTok definitely the service or greeting disappointed me. The drinks I got was Nutella the latte in hot and it was delicious! My mom got the Americano and she loves it 🩷 come here for the drinks and decoration to take pictures for sure","Fun atmosphere and well priced menu items. I had the Pandan Palms and Eggs in Purgatory. Both delicious! Definitely coming back here.","Stopped by this shop because they had good drinks and they did not disappoint! Their interior was lovely and had a lot of seating. They have a wide variety of Asian inspired drinks. Loved their ube latte, and their matcha latte without having the majority of the drink with ice, it’s a perfect amount! Their cheese corn tots were so good and the egg ham and cheese croissant, all within reasonable prices. Would come back!","We came during the non festive time and were glad to find the cafe empty at opening 9:30 which was great.\n\nI didn’t really like anything we ordered but I think it’s more of my palate not being used to the Asia version of foods.","One of the best Vietnamese coffees I’ve had. The coffee here is pretty strong too. The tots were really good but the eggs in purgatory tomato sauce was a bit too sweet."]

name: Bacco Cafe
reviews:
["The food was super tasty and the service was lovely. They refilled our coffee more than we expected which is always nice. They have the favorite French toast and waffles. Along with some non traditional yummy foods like salmon or crab Benedict.

Would definitely give it a try.","All three - food, service and ambience top the list. The cafe is along the main street near Pike place market serving great food. It has two - three areas catering to the public, so don't be fooled in case you do not find a table once you enter from the main door of the cafe.
Negative points- although the menu has typical with added exuberance of crab or salmon etc. the overall receipt is steep high. 100+ (you can pay by cash to avoid taxes)
Good food, heavier portions.
Do indulge in their French toast, Irish coffee and freshly squeezed juice, refreshing to the core.
Better visit the cafe when on foot at the Pike place, searching for a free street parking spot is difficult.","This is the perfect breakfast spot for fresh, locally sourced cuisine and clean ingredients. The brioche french toast was incredibly fluffy and flavorful, and the lox + bagel featured fresh and palatable flavors that hits the spot. The juice was something to come back for - orange juice and the Modena were killer. Highly recommend to come early in the morning, beat the crowds at Pike Place, and enjoy a hearty and healthy breakfast!","I walked in alone. The place was already passed at 8 o’clock in the morning. I start at the bar. Could have waited for a table The weight would’ve been about 15 minutes. Sitting at the bar was a pleasant experience. The wait staff checked on me several times offering more coffee and check to see if everything that I ordered was up to my expectations. Yes  definitely.","Quickly seated on a Saturday morning at 930. Food was phenomenal. A little on the small side but I left full. No left overs. We ordered and got food within 5-10 mins. Quick service and fabulous food."]

name: Storyville Coffee Pike Place
address: 94 Pike St Top floor Suite 34, Seattle, WA 98101, USA
reviews:
["Saw this place as highly recommended on many different platforms so I had to check it out for myself.
Was greeted by two lovely young men who took our orders verbally and passed along the info - nothing written or put into a computer. I found that to be quite impressive because we even made some modifications to our coffees. I had to try their cinnamon roll and my friend got their almond croissant. So 3 coffees and 2 pastries and it totaled to …$55 😯 and so I spent $66 after tipping. I must say that was a lot steeper than I expected but it was a very famous place and it was very busy.
We managed to find a spot to sit with the 4 of us and another employee came by and offered us water which was so kind. We also had a nice view out the window so that was a plus for ambiance.
The lattes were solid, I mean for $12 it had to be lol
But I have to say, my first bite into the cinnamon roll launched me into heaven. I’ve never had anything like it before. You get a small pourer on the side of the warm frosting, the cinnamon roll was so perfectly crispy on the outside and had a dense chew on the inside. It was almost like a glaze that gave it this orgasmic mouth feel. INCREDIBLE. The croissant was also very delicious but I will come back for that cinnamon roll any day. We enjoyed our time there and my only qualm is that it was quite pricey but they do a really good job there. The quality was top notch! A must go to if you’re in Pikes place market!","This Coffee Shop is a cozy haven for coffee enthusiasts. The moment you walk in, the aroma of freshly ground coffee beans envelops you, inviting you to explore the menu. I ordered a cold brew, which was smooth and refreshing, perfectly balanced with just the right amount of sweetness. The baristas are knowledgeable and passionate about their craft, offering recommendations based on your preferences. The seating is comfortable, with both cozy nooks and communal tables, making it an ideal spot for both solo work and socializing. I also indulged in a slice of homemade carrot cake that was moist and flavorful—an excellent pairing with my coffee. Brewed Awakening is not just a coffee shop; it’s a community hub where creativity and connection thrive.","Cozy atmosphere with an efficient, attentive staff that made the visit very welcoming. I especially enjoyed sipping a cortado by the fireplace overlooking the Seattle waterfront. It’s a perfect place to recharge after walking around Pike Place Market.

Service and workflow were impressively smooth, but the coffee itself didn’t quite match the setting. My espresso tasted slightly over-extracted and lacked the depth and balance I was hoping for. A small adjustment to grind size, extraction time, or dialing in shots during busy periods could really elevate the experience for those coming looking for a well pulled espresso. This being said, I didn’t get the feeling that the baristas lacked ability to make this happen.

I’d absolutely return for the atmosphere and hospitality, and with a bit more attention to espresso calibration, this could easily become a standout coffee destination.","Unfortunately they were sold out of breakfast sandwiches. I got an almond croissant it was pretty good. The drinks were higher than Starbucks about $8 for a small. There are cozy seats available . It’s not fair for me to judge this place .","Drinks are pricy compare to Starbucks (about $8). Americano was a bit mild to me. Latte was good. They bring a cup of water to everyone. Free mug for QR event. Clean washroom. Hard to find as it's located on the 2nd floor. Go around O mart and enter the building, and take the elevater to upstairs."]

name: Oddfellows Café + Bar
reviews:
["Awesome spot for brunch! Great Ham and Eggs Benedict and good coffee too (I got an Americano with Oatmilk). It does get busy on the weekends so expect at least a 45 minute wait around noon on a Saturday.","Got together with our family while they were in town (5 adults and 7 kids) and emailed their large party and events folks just to see if they might accommodate us. Liz, their managing partner got back to us in just a few hours for an early dinner that night, which was perfect because we also got to order things from the “fancy hour” (happy hour) menu.

I enjoy coming here because we can get coffee AND cocktails; even hot cocoa for the kids. Emily, our server was delightful and all the staff were lovely.","I came in for my 21st birthday brunch with my fiancée and had mixed feelings about the experience. When we arrived, our server seemed snappy and was chewing gum very aggressively, which felt unprofessional and immediately set an uncomfortable tone. She asked what we wanted to drink, and when I asked about a latte, she abruptly said they don’t have that anymore and then immediately asked what we wanted for food, which felt rushed and dismissive.

We ordered and the food itself was decent. I didn’t love my dish, but it was okay and I still told her it was good out of respect. My fiancée absolutely loved his meal — the biscuit and gravy was delicious, and the fries with garlic aioli were amazing. The coffee was also really good.

Toward the end, things felt awkward and disappointing. There was a surcharge added to our bill, which I thought might have included the tip, so we didn’t leave an additional one. As we were leaving, our server gave us a death stare, which made the situation uncomfortable. She also brought me a to-go cup for my coffee, but when I started to pour it myself she quickly took over and seemed annoyed. Throughout the meal, I also noticed her looking over and talking to her coworker, which made me feel self-conscious, especially on my birthday.

I truly hope we didn’t offend her in any way — we were kind, polite, and tried to make small talk. Unfortunately, the service made what should have been a special birthday brunch feel awkward and disappointing. The food had some great highlights, but the service really affected the overall experience.

I have noticed others saying the same in their reviews so just be aware if you wanna visit.","Great place to meet and catch up, or discuss business, or just sit and eat. It's cozy and comfy and spacious at the same time. I recommend it and will be back. The building is old Seattle brick with a great updated build out, so the charm is everywhere. Very cool spot.","Delicious brunch, great array of dishes,  wonderful customer service, gendered bathrooms (with code).

Arrived Friday at 2:30pm, with a party of 3, and we’re sat immediately. Friendly, attentive, and quick service. Dishes were tasty."]

name: The Outer Rim
address: 2821 Thorndyke Ave W, Seattle, WA 98119, USA
reviews:
["A Must-Visit for Coffee Lovers and Star Wars Fans!
I absolutely love the vibe of this cafe. The Star Wars-inspired interior is unique and stylish without being overwhelming. It’s the perfect spot if you’re looking for a quiet, peaceful place to get some work done. The atmosphere is incredibly friendly and calm.
A special thank you to the barista, Sofia! She is wonderful—so welcoming and makes a truly delicious cup of coffee. Highly recommend for anyone looking for a cozy corner and a great caffeine fix!","I’m a huge Star Wars fan, so I’m super biased with this place. I love the atmosphere of the cafe. It’s definitely sci-fi inspired but mostly seems influenced by Star Wars. The staff were really patient and friendly with us while we took a while to figure out what we wanted to drink. They have a large selection of syrups, so there are plenty of options for sweetened drinks. As for their black coffee, I had the cold brew which was okay. Definitely recommend this to anyone, especially those who are fans of Star Wars, Star Trek and Sci-fi stuff!","Love this place! Barista is always super friendly, and I love the Star Wars themed drinks My favorite is the "Chewbacca" - but I also like the "Jedi Mind Trick", which is pictured.

Every time I come here, I check out the little library out front (I've dropped off a few! Hopefully people enjoy them). Love that sense of community, I'm really happy that they added that~","This is a very unique coffee shop! If you love Star Wars and Star Trek, you will find yourself in galactic heaven. And coffee is not secondary, they go to great lengths to create the perfect coffee beverage. You should beam yourself over there right away.","This is such a nice little coffee shop. The theme is on point. Very clean and simple while still showing off the theme without being too busy. My chai was great, my partner got a tracker beam and the barista was friendly, engaged, and genuine."]

name: Storyville Coffee
reviews:
["This place is beautiful, the food and coffee are exceptional, even the service from the staff is top notch.  Run away from other coffee places and come here.  You may even score a new mug!  I tried the chocolate croissant and a cinnamon roll, both were AMAZING!  A real treat! I have been telling everyone at work to go try something.  I also had a decaf mocha and it was also very good, hard to find good decaf sometimes!  Don't hesitate, GO!","cute coffee shop with dim, warm lighting! lots of seating, though it was hard to find a table on a busy saturday afternoon. vanilla latte was 7/10 and chocolate croissant was average, but the cinnamon roll is 10/10. they give free Storyville branded coffee mugs if you sign up with your email. if you’re at pikes, definitely recommend stopping by!","WOW! Incredible coffee! Coffee is superb, so rich and full of flavour. Staff are super lovely, nice atmosphere inside - great decor too. Must do for coffee lovers","Emmanuel was so polite and kind.  He offered me a free coffee mug for signing up for their newsletter.

We got to talk briefly about the trip that I just got to go on.

He seemed genuinely interested and was a pleasure to talk with.

The barista when I came in was so nice and the environment is very relaxing.

Highly recommend","Best coffee ever!  Nice ambience and freindly staff. But sorry about missing a free mug for a first time customer. Hope going back and get one later."]

name: Hood Famous Bakeshop (Chinatown-ID)
reviews:
["Best vibes! Met so many cool people and their pets! Drinks were delicious, my favorite was the iced ube latte.
I haven’t tried their cakes yet but I heard good things about it.
The cafe is in Chinatown though so it is hard to find parking and has to paid for parking.
Will come back for sure!","Hood Famous Bakeshop offers some of the best Filipino dishes and desserts we have experienced! We especially recommend the ube cheesecake and the chicken arroz caldo (rice porridge). The service was warm and attentive, and the space is cozy, colorful, and thoughtfully decorated. Please note that only paid street parking is available in the area.","This place is awesome. I rolled in a little after opening on a Thursday and it was pretty quiet. I ordered at the kiosk, grabbed a number, and sat at a table with a convenient outlet for my laptop charger. I easily connected to their free Wi-Fi (which is apparently not available on weekends — be warned).

I started with a chicken arroz caldo with crispy chicken skin and a poached egg plus a pandan Italian soda. The arroz caldo is fantastic! Warm, hearty, and texturally interesting, it's comfort in a bowl. The add-ons are non-negotiable, in my opinion. The pandan Italian soda is also tasty and unique, though a coffee lover would probably just prefer a pandan latte.

I enjoyed my food and sipped on my drink for a couple hours while I got some work done. The cafe picked up a little as the morning went on but it stayed relatively quiet and pleasant.

Around lunchtime, I ordered a longsilog (basically a breakfast plate with Filipino pork sausage patties). I also added a mung bean patty to give it a try. When it arrived, I topped it off with some chili crisp and went to town. Wow! Explosion of flavor. The pork sausage is unbelievably juicy and tender. It's perfect with some runny egg yolk and a bit of rice. The greens and pickled veggies add a nice crunch and kick of acidity. The mung bean patty is a little less exciting — a bit dry and without a ton of flavor. But if you're not a meat eater, it's a decent alternative!

I finished my meal and then ordered an assortment of pastries to go. They have a huge variety of unique flavor combinations! Several of them are classic Filipino sweets that you'd be hard pressed to find elsewhere in Seattle, so it was really cool to try them.

The cheesecake is one of their specialities, so I got several varieties to try. Pro tip: you should really enjoy these cold. Once they warm up to room temperature, their flavors dull significantly. My favorites are the Vietnamese coffee and white chocolate guava, but the ube is also rich and unique.

A few other standouts for me are the turon cream puff, the black sesame roll, the mango peach empanada, the bibingka corn muffin, and the beef mechado pocket. If you're a fan of less sweet desserts, you'll probably really enjoy the ube pianono cake slice, the cassava cake, and the biko.

Overall, I can't recommend this place highly enough. The hot dine-in foods, hot or iced drinks, savory pastries, and sweet desserts are all flawlessly prepared in their own ways.","Although we like cooking at home we always visit  and support Filipino restaurants when out of town. Hood Famous delivers on taste, flavor and presentation! The chicken arroz caldo is luscious and comforting, with the toppings of crispy fried garlic and the thin chicken skin added an interesting and great contrast to the creaminess of the rice. The tapsilog and longsilog (beef tapa and longganisa patties were perfectly cooked and cooked. They were tender and flavorful. The desserts and drinks were equally delicious and provided a satisfying way to end our meal. The crew is amazing and friendly, and the chefs and cooks obviously know what they are doing and serving. The quality of the food and service is worthy of more stars than this platform can offer. The space is spotless and  the vibe very upbeat without being loud. Great place to bring family and friends to!

Pro tip: Visit the sauce bar they have next to the counter to make your own dipping sauce with the variety of vinegars and sauces they are offering for free. The Lakan hot sauce and banana ketchup will make your palate dance!","Perfect location for brunch on a Sunday! My colleague and I stopped here as we had heard good reviews about this Filipino cafe! It did not disappoint!

I tried their Ube Ice Latte, and it was the perfect blend of sweetness and expresso! I would highly recommend this drink!

For brunch, I ordered the Pork Menudo (which is a tomato based pork dish with a side of rice). It was my first time trying it, and it was decent. The tomato taste was strong, which was not something I was used to. My colleague ordered the Longsilog, which was different from the normal sausage Longsilog he was used to.

Service was amazing, and they were quick. The waitress accidentally spilled a bit of my latte but cleaned it up right away.

I'd recommend anyone trying out this cafe if they want to try a Filipino cafe!"]

name: Yard House
reviews:
["How am I just hearing that Seattle has a yardhouse? Home of the last good happy hour. You can take the light rail and get off at Westlake center and walk across the street, no need to hassle with parking in downtown.
I'm pleased with everything I've tried here. Delicious food. Good service. Clean restrooms. Good prices.","There was a bit of a wait when we arrived, but with homecoming going on and the Mariners game playing on the TVs, it was completely understandable. Once we were seated, we were greeted by Nile, who was friendly, funny, and really knew the menu well enough to help answer any questions we had. We shared a few laughs with her throughout our visit, which made the experience even better.

The atmosphere was cozy and surprisingly romantic, especially considering how many TVs were around. We were seated in a great spot, quiet enough to talk comfortably but close enough for me to catch glimpses of the game.

We started with the firecracker shrimp, which came with a rice cake on the bottom, something I’d never tried before but really enjoyed. My wife went with the Shiitake garlic noodles, and I ordered the Nashville hot chicken. Both were delicious, though I would’ve liked mine a little spicier. It would have been nice if they offered different heat levels.

Overall, we had a great time. The food was solid, the vibe was perfect, and Nile’s great service really stood out. We’ll definitely be back the next time we’re in town.","We had dinner here two nights in a row because we loved it so much!! 🥲 The food was amazing, we tried the Whiskey Glazed Salmon, Mediterranean Salmon, and the Beer-Battered Fish & Chips, all delicious! The service was great, and the cozy friendly atmosphere made our nights even better ✨ Such good memories.. thank you Yard House Seattle! 💛✨","The staff here is really nice. We were visiting out of town and our daughter turned 2 the day before and when we went the host was very generous to offer her a dessert. The water was very friendly and attentive. They made our experience very pleasant.","The food is great! My family and I ate the fish and chips, burgers, Nashville hot, macaroni, pizza and chicken strips. We enjoyed all the food. We bought the nachos too. Delicious! Cash served us and he was great! He kept our drink full the whole time and had great customer service too. Thank you Yard House, in Seattle Washington, for a wonderful experience!"]

name: Alder & Ash
reviews:
["This was my first time ever coming to my significant others job and visiting this luxurious destination. The music and atmosphere give you a Hollywood like, fine dining experience. The food here was phenomenal. I ordered the seafood risotto and the salmon dip with kettle chips. My service was enhanced by the following individuals: Isaac, Vaughn, Anna and Emma. The servers frequently visited our table to check to see how everything was going. The food was delivered by Vaughn, the head chef to elevate the experience. The food has a flavor like no other. It was a carnival of textiles, bursting with flavor and had high visual merchandising appeal. My partner had a great birthday experience. Thank you to everyone who contributed to our excellent first time.","The ambiance and menu selection were great.
Our server was amazing.
However, the ahi tuna poke had an off smell, so I did not eat the protein, the pickled cucumber that came with the poke was amazing. I was also disappointed with the dessert. The salted chocolate ganache was overdone with salt. Each bite of the ice cream tasted like salt bits. However the pecan flavor was great.","My girlfriend and I enjoyed the three-course Thanksgiving meal. The squash soup and seasonal salad were fresh and delicious, while the main course of turkey and mashed potatoes was flawless. The pumpkin pie tasted homemade and was a sweet ending to a great Thanksgiving. We also enjoyed a seasonal espresso martini and old fashioned, both great. The service was excellent and wait time was minimal. Can’t wait to try the normal menu.","Outstanding across the board. Food, service, atmosphere. Four of us went for Thanksgiving. Two ordered the fixed turkey menu, two ordered off the normal menu. We had oysters, meatballs, salad, pork shoulder, mashed potatoes, etc. Everything was perfectly seasoned and well balanced. I am not a fan of squash but ordered the squash soup and it was phenomenal, not overly sweet like most, lots of layers. The chef here knows what they are doing. Will definitely be back.","Sat at the bar, nice place. Convenient for a quick but yummy meal.

The Adler and Ash burger was tasty. I wouldn’t say it’s special but decent. No blue cheese stuffed olive for my dirty martini but the server made up for it by being so attentive.
Happy Hour was pretty good. $10 menu list was pleasant surprise.  I had flatbread, watermelon salad (?)."]

name: Ludi's Restaurant
reviews:
["Had an amazing experience at Ludi’s Restaurant! From the moment we walked in, the owners made us feel so welcome. They were incredibly kind and genuine, and the staff was super nice, attentive, and helpful throughout our visit.

We ordered the Filipino favorites—shrimp rice, fish rice, veggie cheddar omelette, and the ube pancake—and every single dish was flavorful and well prepared. The food tasted fresh, comforting, and full of authentic flavor. The ube pancake was especially memorable—beautifully purple, soft, and delicious!

Everything we tried was really good. The only small suggestion would be about the shrimp being fully shelled—it made eating a bit messy and cumbersome. That said, it didn’t take away from how great the food tasted.

Overall, this is a wonderful place with great food, warm hospitality, and a welcoming atmosphere. You can tell the owners truly care about their customers and their craft. Highly recommend stopping by if you’re in the area—you won’t be disappointed!","Just in town for a long weekend and this place was recommended by our hotel staff!  What a find!  We arrived at 8 am on a brisk 39° Sunday morning and found a line outside.  It was a hour-long wait which was worth it.  An elderly gentleman was out handing some hot chocolate for those in line... once seated, he came around to talk to guest and turns out he is the owner.  He genuinely loves to interact with guests and provide everyone one with Filipino hospitality.
We order the stack of Ube pancakes and a combo Silog breakfast.  Pancakes were bouncy and healthy covered with generous ube sauce... beautifully topped with whipped cream and am orchid flower.  The combo Silog had a nice breaded pork chop, a long lumpia, 2 types of filipino breakfast sausages over a generous portion of Garlic fried rice.  When the combo showed up, it smelled so nostalgic that I quickly tore into it and forgot to take a pick for the review.  Great food!  We picked up a couple of turons for a later afternoon snack.
You order at the start before upu get seated and food came out promptly. A nice find and instant favorite!","Food was yummy. Ube pancakes looked and tasted delicious. However they want everyone to order a separate dish which was weird and difficult esp with children. We ordered big plates & wanted to share it but had to order extra food/ drinks just to satisfy this rule.","Worth the wait!  Hearty breakfasts and awesome people. Filipino breakfasts with sausages, pork chops and other delicious dishes with "standard" breakfast options like pancakes, French toast, etc.  we will definitely be back to try more of the menu!

Line moves relatively fast and as others mention you order and pay before seating (once a table opens up).  Drinks and food come pretty quickly and there's no pressure to eat fast even though there's a line outside.  So enjoy your delicious breakfast, and then let someone else enjoy the goodness!","Uncle Greg is the best. You can tell he loves his restaurant. The line is worth it!! I promise. Because once you order (cafe style, preorder and then sit), your food comes to you fast. It’s authentic Filipino breakfast/brunch food. The ube pancake is so rich, so delicious. I always get the combo silog with the garlic rice, it comes with eggs, longanisa (Filipino sausage), pork chops and a lumpia (authentic lumpia, I’m sure it’s house made). You’ll definitely feel like you’re at your cousin’s house for breakfast, because Greg always has a smile and will come to your table to say hi."]

name: Toulouse Petit Kitchen & Lounge
address: 601 Queen Anne Ave N, Seattle, WA 98109, USA
reviews:
["Toulouse was such a pleasant surprise. The ceviche was bright, fresh, and perfectly balanced, and the Cajun-infused dishes added a bold, flavorful twist without overpowering anything. The Delmonico steak was cooked to absolute perfection,tender, juicy, and seasoned just right. We also loved the burrata, which was creamy and fresh and paired beautifully with the accompaniments. Every dish felt thoughtfully prepared, and you can really taste the quality. A must-visit if you’re looking for elevated comfort food with a Cajun flair. Kids menu comes in huge adult size portions!!!","Wife and I went here for our birthday dinner on a Saturday night. We had reservations at 6:30 so upon arrival we were seated promptly. The menu here is vast so prepare yourself. I had never tried oven roasted oysters before so I immediately ordered 4 of the Venezia oven roasted oysters. For entrees my wife ordered the Snake River Farms Wagyu Baseball Cut Sirloin and added the Fried Green Tomatoes with Tarragon Ravigote. I tried the Toulouse Seafood Gumbo with added Dungeness crab and scallop coins. First off, kudos to the kitchen for getting our food out so fast. The Venezia oysters were DELICIOUS, very sweet tasting with all the different toppings. I think I've discovered my new favorite way of devouring oysters. My wife's steak was juicy and tender, cooked exactly medium well as she requested; pairing well also with the chimichuru side sauce that came with it. The Fried green tomatoes were a nice addition, crispy and nicely cooked. My seafood gumbo was simply delicious, packed fulled of seafood and house made andouille sausage. It was a very flavorful and filling dish although the roux was not as thick as I'm accustomed to having, nevertheless the dish was very satisfying. For dessert we sampled the buttermilk beignets with vanilla ice cream. The beignets were very tasty, we could definitely taste the buttermilk component to them. Along with the vanilla ice cream it was the perfect ending to a very delightful meal (and the restaurant discounted the dessert because it was our birthdays). I highly recommend this establishment for any special dining occasion.","We went on an Thursday evening at 8 PM. The service is amazing; we were seated and orders taken quickly, food came out piping hot and our glasses were always filled. I recommend looking at the menu ahead of time, it's a very big menu. Beautiful ambience.

We ordered:
• green goddess salad: small portions but very refreshing ane herby, good accompaniment with other heavier dishes.
• fries: MUST ORDER, perfectly crispy and garnished with parsley, garlic and parmesan.
• blackened rockfish w/ creole crawfish tasso sauce with corn grits: amazing, although a little indulgent because of the grits
• petrale sole herb crusted with prawns, mashed potatoes, and asparagus. i can’t find it on the menu they have on their website, it must be a seasonal thing or a special, but absolutely AMAZING.
• fried chicken gumbo: chicken was a little dry, but the gumbo was out of this world
• non-alcoholic monday mezcal mule: delicious, tangy and spicy aftertaste
• beignets: good, but not exceptional, but we've been spoiled with new orleans beignets","Great experience! This restaurant was fantastic and so much fun as a newcomer who’s never been! The restaurant itself has a beautiful interior where pictures will never do it justice. It’s kept clean, is spacious and staff are friendly and attentive!
Menu is huge and offered a ton of variety for people to choose from.
What I loved about this restaurant is that it’s definitely not like one you’ll experience everyday! The food was fantastic, rich and provided a homey comforting taste that was flavorful but also inspiring.

Highly recommend this restaurant and the opportunity to have a wonderful experience enjoying a night out, holiday event, etc - this is the place!","This place needs real ratings. First, the welcome was excellent entering the facility. Next the beignets were dry overly fried and lacked powered sugar. My family placed their order 1st because their menu was so large I was undecided.  Yet when I placed my order, my food came out with my family's food. Rushed and cold. The biscuits was warm and the sauce on top was cold. So I sent it back to the kitchen. I never ordered again. Overall, this place seems like they rushed in the kitchen or their food is not prepared fresh to order. The atmosphere was ok. Dark w/candles lit.

However, i don't recommend.  This place is rated high, yet their food lacks freshness and love."]

name: The Fisherman's Restaurant Seattle
reviews:
["Amazing place, fun and lively.  We went in for lunch on a beautiful fall day.  Wanted to eat outside.  We were told 20 minutes and invited to have a "day drink" in the bar - yup, that works.
20 minutes later we were invited to a table looking out at the water.  The service was brilliant and entertaining.   The cliental  was international. All of us tourists eating and laughing - fun!
Food was serviced in a timely manor and was amazing!","My family and I were visiting Seattle before a cruise and were hungry when visiting Seattle’s waterfront.

Naturally, you would think to yourself this must be the place to eat seafood (hence the name and location next to the water).

I normally don’t write bad reviews but my first impressions wasn’t a good one.

First the dishes weren’t cleaned properly. I saw food particles still on glasses and cutlery. Second, we asked for an outside table. But they put my dad (who is in a wheelchair) directly in the sun. When we asked to switch, it wasn’t still in a good spot. Our server wasn’t also very helpful. We needed refills on this hot day and we saw only two tables in his section. But we saw other parties (particularly of a certain privileged race) getting served properly and quickly than our table.

To top it off, the food wasn’t what you would expect next to a waterfront with such a high price tag.

So to sum it up;
If you’re a tourist. This is a tourist trap.

You’re better off finding quality food at pikes Market.

To the managers please check your staff. I didn’t want to complain since i was already tired and the heat would’ve not made my suggestions received properly.","Obviously this is a tourist trap, therefore don't complain when its expensive, you know what the crack is!
The viewing deck outdoors is great. We were lucky enough to be there on a beautiful February day with no clouds. We didn't eat but what I saw looked pretty good.","It was a very last minute lunch. It was quiet in the off season. Beautiful view, great location overlooking puget sound. I had the prawns and chips $20 and my husband had the halibut and chips $26. Everything came fast and hot. Both were very good. The marmalade dip with the prawns was superb. Chips were nice and crispy and fresh. Really great meal.","Good views on the water next to the Ferris wheel. Sit outside if the weather is nice. Foods ok, overpriced for lunch I thought. Online menus I saw aren't current, looks like they raised prices again recently. Fish was a little dry. Oysters were small. Portions aren't huge, a lot of breading and fries. Paying for location."]

name: Lola
reviews:
["Lola presents itself as a modern Greek-influenced restaurant, but it unfortunately misses much of what makes Greek cuisine so appealing. Greek food should be fresh, vibrant, and full of flavor, yet the dishes here felt flat and slightly Americanized. The meal lacked both the freshness and the love that great Greek cooking usually delivers. Nothing was terrible, but nothing impressed either. In the end, it felt like a very average restaurant loosely inspired by Greece rather than a place that truly celebrates its flavors. If you’re expecting vibrant Greek or Mediterranean food, this probably isn’t the place you’re looking for.","We had dinner at Lola, and the whole experience was truly wonderful. The food was flavorful, the ambiance relaxing, and our server was the sweetest person—so warm and attentive throughout the evening.

We started with the Lola spreads, opting for the smoky eggplant and roasted sweet red pepper. Both were incredibly flavorful and set a great tone for the meal. We also enjoyed the beet salad, chicken kabab, and lamb kabab, all of which were very tasty.

A couple of dishes fell a bit short for us: the roast chicken lacked some flavor, and the seafood tagine had a broth that leaned a little too sour. However, these minor misses didn’t overshadow the rest of the experience.

For dessert, the apple & date butter crostata was a delightful finish—warm, comforting, and very nicely done.

Overall, we had a great time at Lola and would love to return.","Absolutely incredible. Smaller breakfast and lunch menu than what they have for dinner but everything was made to perfection. High quality ingredients & full of flavor. I forgot to take pictures of our other dishes because we were too busy devouring the food. We got the lamb and pork kebabs with the roasted potatoes and french fries and both were delicious! Don’t think twice!","Service was great. Food was delicious overall, and atmosphere is pretty warm and cozy. I came for a dinner here with family. Shoutout to the hostess and server who took care of us tonight. Loved the pita, spanakopita, and side of greek pasta. Lamb kebabs were a bit dry, side of potatoes were also dry and too thick to enjoy, and serving sizes are a bit small. We got the coconut cream and apple fig dessert they were pretty safe and good.
Fries were okay nothing special. Overall this is a good place to hit for dinner if you are in the area.","Loved everything. I had a 'winter pearing' cocktail to start which was lovely. We started my sharing the beet salad which was yummy and the kalamata olive fig spread. We also shared the Anderson valley lamb and halloumi-fig kebabs. We topped it all off with cappuccinos. It was a great dinner spot for a lovely date night"]

name: The Hart and the Hunter
reviews:
["Went later on a Friday night before Valentines day, it was decorated with cute flowers and candles, nice theme and the drinks were good. But the food was amazing. Braised beef is a must! Service was good! I would most definitely come back to eat again.","We actually came here for dinner, but I would be happy to return for brunch, as it seems that is what they specialize in.

- Biscuits were heavenly. It pairs well with their delicious homemade butter and apricot jam.
- No need to go to Starbucks. The PSL here is flavorful and smooth.
- I’m usually not a French fry person, but the fries were really good here!
- I got the Palicob salad. I think this was the weakest part of the meal, as the chicken was kind of tasteless. Everything else about the salad (cheddar cheese and bacon) were well-cut and presented and satisfying to mix together though. I was also happy they didn’t use too much dressing.

Staff is really kind and attentive. The restaurant is a little hidden in plain sight, but it’s pretty charming and has old-school vibes. It also connects to the cute hotel lobby area, so definitely check that out too.","The Hart and The Hunter is one of those places that understands comfort without dumbing it down. The biscuits arrived warm, dense yet flaky, with butter and preserves that made you slow down and pay attention. We also had the brisket with eggs and pickled vegetables, rich, savory, and cut with just enough acidity to keep it honest. Relaxed, welcoming, and perfectly placed near Pike Place, it’s the kind of spot that quietly earns your loyalt","The biscuits are amazing and the espresso martini are divine. I would come back here just for those 2 items. We went for dinner and also ordered the burger and the chicken pot pie which were both very tasty. The service was fantastic. They offer a brunch menu so we are definitely coming back to try that. Would definitely recommend this place if you are downtown and looking for elevated home-cooked food.","Stopped in to grab a quick bite after spending the night in Seattle. Nice chill atmosphere and attentive staff. The biscuits were amazing and the eggs Benedict definitely won’t disappoint.  Top of the list the next time we are in town"]

name: How to Cook a Wolf
address: 2208 Queen Anne Ave N, Seattle, WA 98109, USA
reviews:
["Let me start by saying that their outdoor patio is so charming and cozy.  Their cocktails are carefully prepared and their cocktail menu is out of this world.  Yes, we have tried every single cocktail in their menu.  The food is simple but super tasty and full of local/seasonal produce.  Meg, the manager, is all over this restaurant.  She makes the service better and the servers more attentive.","Check in: I thought this restaurant entrance/check in would be a little bit fancier since when I made the reservation I was asked to upload a picture so they knew who we were when we entered but there isn’t even a host stand which I found odd.

Whoever the manager or lead was that night was top tier! We had a reservation for 7pm and the previous party was still at our table. The lead sat us at an outdoor table with a heater and brought us a sample of the house champagne! 20 mins later when we finally got to our table, he also brought us polenta fritters! He definitely heightened our dining experience!

Service was fantastic. Our waitress knew the menu and was attentive throughout our time dining.

Atmosphere: cozy, dark, calm a little loud but we could talk normal and still hear just fine. There’s a disco ball on the ceiling which was a little odd but funny.

Food:
-Polenta fritters : not my favorite. Could use a spicier additive for more flavor.
-Sourdough: great with that amazing butter and cloves of garlic
🌟 Burrata: this is a FAVORITE! Absolutely 10/10 recommend and love the addition of pomegranate seeds
-Steak: not worth $60 in my opinion. Not the best cut, could be seasoned more but the squash with it was amazing.
-Porkchop: this was fantastic and I rarely eat pork! Perfectly cooked, cut easily, and good flavor.
🌟 Pappardelle: this is the best dish on the menu! We ordered two of these and took one home because it was SO good! The meat is perfectly seasoned and good and the noodles are perfectly cooked as well.
-drinks: great flavor, perfectly blended, and a great selection of spirit free drinks.
-cannolis: I wish the ricotta was colder and a little more flavorful. But the shell is perfect.","We had an incredible first experience at How to Cook a Wolf, and it instantly became one of our favorite spots since moving to Seattle. The food was outstanding, and we love that the menu changes with the seasons. It makes us even more excited to come back and see what’s next.

Since it was our first visit, we ordered a lot so we could really explore the menu and find our favorites. We started with the olives, and wow, easily some of the best olives we’ve ever had. Perfectly marinated, super flavorful, and honestly blew us away. We also ordered the sourdough, which was delicious with the butter, garlic, olive oil, and balsamic. This might be something you could skip if you’re being price-conscious, but if you’re a bread lover, it’s absolutely amazing. Bonus: both the olives and sourdough are always on the menu, so you can count on them every visit.

Next was the burrata, and let me just say, it was the best burrata we’ve ever tasted. The combination of flavors was unreal. Pomegranate seeds, nuts, and other elements blended together perfectly. We ate it with the sourdough, and it was a must-have dish. I would order this every single time before it goes out of season, I believe around the end of March.

For drinks, we started with The Professor and Cream of the Crop, both bourbon-based and very well balanced. Bourbon can be strong, but these were smooth and flavorful. The Professor is always on the menu and clearly a favorite. With our meal, we ordered the Mai Tai and Arrivederci, which were sweeter and incredibly smooth. Between the two, the Mai Tai was our favorite. It was less alcohol-forward and topped with a foam that made it so fun and delicious to drink.

For our main course, we both chose the Paccheri, and it did not disappoint. It had a slight spice, breadcrumbs, olives, and a red sauce. There were probably even more flavors, but those stood out most. It tasted truly unique, like something you couldn’t get just anywhere, which is exactly why we loved it. You can absolutely tell the noodles are made from scratch, and they tied the whole dish together beautifully.

To finish, we ordered an espresso martini and the pistachio cake for dessert, best decision ever. The pistachio cake was incredible and seasonal. If you love pistachio, do not skip this. We’re already considering ordering one for an upcoming birthday. The espresso martini was also exceptional. You can tell they have an espresso machine in-house because it tasted fresh and smooth, not like coffee concentrate mixed with liquor. Perfectly balanced.

Beyond the food and drinks, the experience itself was top-notch. The service was incredibly attentive, and even though we were the last ones in the restaurant, we never felt rushed. We even offered to leave so they could close, but they insisted it was totally fine and encouraged us to stay for dessert. We also had the chance to meet the chef, who was super cool and genuinely sweet. The gratuity is included in the meal but it was how much we would’ve tipped anyways, they 100% deserve it.

Overall, this was a 10/10 experience across the board. From the food and drinks to the service, ambiance, lighting, and overall vibe, everything was amazing. We will absolutely be back soon to try more dishes.

Highly recommend ✨🤩","We were 3 people and got to sit outside. If you really wanna sit inside I recommend reserving in advance. The service was absolutely phenomenal. Everyone was super nice and greeted us with smiles and took very good care of us. The host even got us an extra heater for our legs so we won’t have to endure the cold outside. The food was okay.. I don’t think it’s worth the price tag but you’re paying for the experience more than for the food. Would totally go back.","I have been wanting to eat here for years and was finally able to. We celebrated our 1 year anniversary here and we would highly recommend.

They gave us two glasses of champagne to celebrate and we also ordered the Watermelon Sugar High and the Fire and Ice. Both were Unique and well made drinks.

We ordered the Sea Wolf Sourdough which was my wife’s favorite. Perfectly infused garlic in the bread. We also ordered the Buccatni, sauce and the pasta came al dente.  Perfectly cooked and well made.

We also had the Hamachi Crudo which was fresh and the Lamb Shank which filled us. All were flavor full and well prepared. Super happy with all the dishes.

Would highly recommend and will definitely be back!"]

name: Marination Ma Kai
address: 1660 Harbor Ave SW, Seattle, WA 98126, USA
reviews:
["I placed my order and it was up within 5 minutes even tho the place was very busy. I got the Miso ginger chicken taco's. They were yummy, the chicken was tender and flavorful and the corn tortillas soft and delicious. This Lil establishment sits right on the water. Great place to hang out or catch the water taxi to downtown Seattle.","Overall a good place. It is self service. I got fish and chips. The looks of fries were not that appetizing but it makes for that with crunch and taste. The kimchi fried rice was decent.

The biggest thing going for this place is the location. The location is really great with Seattle downtown view.

I wouldn't recommend their mango lemonade. It taste good but they need to cut down sugar content.","The food was excellent!! I had the fish sandwich and my brother had the pork katsu sandwich…very very good. The fries are not good, they are the fresh ones like in n out, so if you like those then I guess you will like these. Mac salad was very very very good and we also had a malasada. The malasada was not great, I would skip it.
The views are maybe the best of the Seattle skyline you will get…there is also a water ferry dock that will take you to downtown I believe. There is also a place to rent kayaks and paddle boards.","We always make a point to stop at Marination Ma Kai whenever we visit friends in Seattle. It’s the perfect spot if you’re out walking your dog or just want a casual bite with a view. The cocktail window makes grabbing a quick drink easy, the food comes out fast, and the outdoor seating usually doesn’t have too long of a wait. The waterfront views alone are worth five stars, but paired with the food and drinks, the experience is even better. No complaints at all… and the lychee margarita is an absolute must try!","Great tacos, really enjoyed the variety and flavor. Wonderful outdoor patio and spectacular view of the Seattle skyline. One of the best places to enjoy the sunset while eating dinner. Very reasonably priced and ample parking."]

name: The George
reviews:
["Was staying at a nearby hotel and came for breakfast.

It goes without saying the interior is absolutely stunning. The colors,  the furniture,  art just blends so well together.

The quality of the decor is only matched by the food.  Everything was fresh,  seasoned and delicious. Would recommend the Dungeness crab omlette or the salmon lox for a lighter meal. There is plenty of meat incorporated in each dish so you don't feel skipped out on. For kids, I think the best wasn't the pancakes but the fresh vanilla yogurt and berries.

The service was excellent as well. Each team member was in sync making sure our drinks were always full and refreshed. They would often check in to ensure everything was up to their standards and I appreciate that greatly.

Will certainly be back,  hopefully for dinner next time.","This restaurant came up a few times in my search. So on our last night in Seattle we decided to try it. Such a pretty location. Our server was very attentive and made suggestions for us. Our Oysters were fresh and tasty. Our drinks well made and our server even made us a version of a green tea shot. Our meat was well cooked and flavorful. Overall an excellent experience here. The only draw back, bring a sweater it was a bit chilly. It was a great way to say goodbye to Seattle.","This last Christmas I had the most spectacular meal at the George with my girlfriend. The service was excellent. They were more than thrilled to make our evening special. Food was excellent. I had the half duck and she had a beef and polenta dish that was excellent. The cocktails were phenomenal. We're never going to forget this place because it is where I decided to ask her to marry me! Thank you, George and Fairmont staff.","We came to Fairmont for a date night: got our massages, relaxed in the spa zone and then went for a dinner. The George features unique interior, attentive service and interesting tastes. We ordered a couple of appetizers, 2 mains and a desert and everything tasted fresh and balanced.
