My husband loved the sirloin stake the best and I enjoyed my wagyu burger. The tiramisu for 2 was a nice touch as the end of the dinner.","A True Seattle Gem

This review has been long overdue, but it’s finally time to sing the praises of my favorite restaurant in Seattle — George, located in the elegant Fairmont Olympic Hotel. Every visit here reminds me why this place stands out as one of the city’s finest dining experiences.

The atmosphere is warm, bright, and inviting, striking that perfect balance between upscale and comfortable. Whether you’re there for a special occasion or a casual evening out, the ambiance makes you feel instantly welcome.

The service deserves special mention — friendly, fast, and efficient. The staff is attentive without being intrusive, and every interaction feels genuinely warm.

Now, onto the food, which is consistently delicious and beautifully presented. We started with the Organic Skagit Sourdough Loaf served with thyme whipped butter and Maldon sea salt — warm, soft, and the perfect start to the meal. The Burrata Salad was wonderfully fresh and flavorful. The Warm Dungeness Crab Dip with artichoke, crab claws, and garlic breadcrumbs was rich, creamy, and absolutely delightful. The Spanish Octopus was good but could have been slightly better in texture. However, the Scallops more than made up for it — perfectly cooked, tender, and full of flavor.

The desserts were nothing short of exceptional. The Chocolate Fudge Cake was indulgent, the Pumpkin Panna Cotta was smooth and beautifully spiced, and the Chocolate Dome with Cherry Cordial was as stunning as it was delicious.

Overall, George delivers a near-perfect dining experience — exquisite food, top-notch service, and a welcoming atmosphere that keeps you coming back. Highly recommend this place for anyone looking to enjoy a refined yet approachable meal in the heart of Seattle."]

name: Pasta Casalinga
reviews:
["This is a small, counter-serve spot that's located in the corner of Pike Place. It's a little difficult to find via Google Maps, but thankfully there are signs directing you to the place. From what I saw, most people eating there seemed to be locals on their lunch break (like myself)

I had the squid ink spaghetti alla chitarra, which came with salmon added on.
After having it, I see why of all the options around Pike Place, people still religiously come here. This was amazing.

If I had to start somewhere, it would be with the noodles itself. These were in the exact style that I found when I went to Italy - they were thick, flour-like, and very starchy, but most noticeably, they were cooked much less than what you usually expect. 'Al dente' means very different things in the US and Europe - in the US, the pasta ends up a lot softer after cooking. However, the pasta here matches that European style of being on the hardier and chewier side.
This worked really well at this place because they make their pasta fresh from the dough every single morning. It's also not overdone - still enjoyable to eat without being hard as a rock.

The squid ink fit so well here. It's somewhat rare that you see a place that offers it, but it works immensely well as a pasta sauce. Firstly, it coats the entire thing so well - giving it that deep black color - but it's also light enough that without the color you wouldn't really notice it visually. The extra flavor it imparts is subtle but ever-present.
Possibly the only nitpick I had was with the salmon; it was a bit too cooked for me. It seemed to be cooked on the medium-well side, and was a bit chewy as a result. Given that we're in Pike Place, there are a lot better salmon spots around.

The thing that really shines, though, is the toppings/add-ons. I got burrata on top, which increased the price a good bit, but it was so worth it as this was so soft and creamy. The mustard seeds, garlic, breadcrumbs, and butter combine to make an incredible flavor mix which encompasses every single bite of the pasta. The way each noodle is coated with deliciousness is truly extraordinary. The seasoning with herbs and spices is also perfect.

The price ended up being around $30 for the full pasta, which is on the expensive side, but honestly - I got the quality of a full sit-down Italian restaurant meal (honestly even better) for what ended up being a lower price than what I would've spent. The portion size was also incredibly large.

Overall, this is the best restaurant I've had in Pike Place, and is very reminiscent of places I've been in Italy. 9.25/10","This place was on Kenji Lopez-Alt’s “must eat” list and I was so happy I got to visit. Very close to the Pike place market but not at all crowded, quiet, located on the second floor of a small shopping mall. They serve wine and just a few kinds of fresh homemade pasta. I had to get the one “from the sea” with octopus. It’s funny they had “no chicken” written in big letters on the menu. Apparently people ask a lot. The pasta was cooked perfectly. Then, the octopus was yummy, olives, and capers added so much zing that my mouth is still watering when I think about it.","Stopped in for a pasta dish. I was on my own. The line moved quickly, and there is a sense to be prepared. They have high volume and for good reason, The pasta is just exceptional. They only have a few menu items but you can’t get it wrong.

I had the ocean pasta. Everything was cooked to perfection. It really hit the spot and cleaned my plate.

The service was great. Despite the volume and pressure everyone is under they were super helpful and friendly.

I found a seat at the bar table. Because I was alone this wasn’t an issue but the place was packed at 2pm on a Saturday. If my group was lager seating might be a problem but the turn over was quite high. It’s not a place to hang out.

No notes. Will definitely be back. Believe the hype.","We went here twice for lunch. Both times, we shopped around the restaurant picking up dips, meats, cheeses, bread olives/picked onions. Then we sat at a table upstairs and ate it while enjoying a bottle of wine that we bought, too. Loved the experience of picking up exactly what we wanted and how much of it at the deli counter and enjoying a lunch spread.

Great for foodies and picky eaters alike! They do have a menu if you'd like, but we liked having options.

Looking forward to visiting the next time we're in town!","By the time I post this review, the menu will probably be different. They make fresh pasta every day (we were at Pike Place around 8:30 am one morning and saw all the prep work). There was a line when we went on a Thursday afternoon, and it's a bit of a scramble for counter seats. You order, try to find a spot, and listen for your name.
When I went, I got the seafood pasta (they also have meat, veggie, lasagna, and pasta with their housemade rossodoro sauce). Mine had anchovies, escarole, olives, and breadcrumbs. It was super umami and tasty, and a good-sized portion too. I'd recommend stopping by if you're at Pike Place. It's a little less touristy than other places."]

name: Musubi Kai Seattle
reviews:
["We tried the eel musubi and the fried shrimp musubi, and both were great. I personally loved the fried shrimp one for its texture. The prices were reasonable, about $5 more or less for any musubi on the menu. Definitely worth checking out if you're looking for hawaiian food.","Was on my way to get a sandwich and found this gem.
Large specialty musubi portions for $6!
Ordered 4 types of musubi… wide variety of ingredients included eel, egg, gyro meat, avocado, cucumber and of course spam!

Delicious, will be back!","Stopped in for breakfast. It was nice visiting places that have breakfast musubis. The moco loco was also good. Small, restaurant with a polite and efficient staff. With stopping by if you've never tried musubi. Don't be afraid of spam.","Service was friendly and when I asked about recommendations they provided them. Got 2 regular spam musubi, one with eel and one with avocado. All were very good and worth the price. Picked up for take out. Would go back again and try new things or get the same.","After spending time in Hawaii and trying musubi I’ve been wanting to try it again. There aren’t any places on the east coast that sells this dish so to our surprise when we stumbled upon this, we had to stop in. A simple musubi  with just the nori, rice and spam was $5. Thankfully they were served warm. They taste just like they did in Hawaii but yes, very salty. Glad we got to have it again."]

name: Von's 1000Spirits
reviews:
["Ate here for the first time a couple weeks back and came back a few days later for NYE that’s how much we liked it. The food is awesome, I was skeptical at first since the majority of their main food is sour dough but I was definitely caught off guard. The first time we went, I We both ordered pasta, which were fantastic. The second go round I ordered the pepperoni pizza. I am more of a pizza guy so I I’ll order the a pizza next time but the pastas do not disappoint.

The staff there are really nice. Even with a line out of the door they are super friendly and did not seem stressed or annoyed both times. They do their best to accommodate you and get you seated as fast as they can.  There is a reason this place has 6k reviews.

There aren’t very many places I would recommend within the city of Seattle due to weird/ hipster vibe most places offer but if  you’re a local or visiting town I would make sure you visit Von’s you will not regret it. All around this place is awesome. From their chefs to the hosts they have it dialed in. And for it being in Seattle it’s not super pricey, it’s what you would expect to pay to dine in a Seattle restaurant. So go check it out especially since sour dough seems to be the new fad right now. Experience the hype of sour dough made correctly.","I went here with group of friends and we had a good time. The restaurant was pretty loud, lots of hard surfaces did little in the way of deadening the sound of everyone around us. The restaurant was almost entirely full when we arrived, and our table was missing a chair despite our reservation. The food came out pretty quick once we got our orders taken, but it took quite a while for the waiter to take our food order. The food itself was really good, honestly the almond pesto was probably some of the best pesto I've ever had. We had the pork confit fries, the crispy calamari, and the burrata baguette for appetizers. All three were good with the burrata being the best, and the calamari coming in last, though still delicious. My entree was the basil almond pesto pasta and it was amazing. It was super creamy and all of the ingredients went so well together. I'd come back, but I'd probably try to find a less busy time.","Tried a bunch of food and drinks here and honestly, everything hit 😋

The Double Smashed American Burger was super tender and juicy.
The Nashville Hot Chicken Sandwich wasn’t very spicy, but the chicken was perfectly juicy and the sauce + pickle combo was outstanding.
Loved the Chicken Cutlet Grinder as well.

The Seattle Scratch Sourdough Pizza (Craft Bacon, Washington Apple & Rogue Blue) was a standout. Fig jam with bacon and blue cheese was an amazing combo.

Dessert (Smoky Campfire Rocky Road Pie) was perfect, not too sweet and served with marshmallow and vanilla crème anglaise.

Drinks were fun and solid, especially the Cotton Candy cocktail with the tableside pour and color-changing light cube.

The staff is super friendly and sweet, which made the experience even better. Definitely going back 💯","Can’t go wrong with sourdough pizza and pasta. We had both, plus a few drinks, and everything hit. The pizza was perfectly chewy with that signature sourdough flavor, the pasta was comforting and well done, and the drinks were really good (wish I remembered their names). Great spot, great vibes- would absolutely come back all the way from San Diego.","Food was great, drinks were good, and the atmosphere is as awesome as I remembered it from a few years ago. Visiting from Las Vegas means that I'm used to high quality F&B, and Von's has not disappointed over the years. From the host stand to the bar service, the staff's hospitality is a highlight! Cecilia, our server, had a fantastic attitude throughout and made us feel comfortable and like we belonged.

The King salmon pasta is well-balanced between the creamy, cheesy sauce and well-cooked salmon. I probably would have preferred the addition of fresh basil leaves and roasted tomatoes to liven up the dish, but the heartiness was great regardless!"]

name: Tilikum Place Cafe
reviews:
["Supreme customer service, delicious dishes, great view of the space needle, clean and comfy vibe, gendered bathrooms.

Arrived at 1:00pm on a Friday with a group of 3. They had some cancellations and we were sat within 2 minutes of walking in. Our server let us know the Dutch babies are delicious but take 25-35 minutes. We ordered eggs Benedict, scrabbled eggs, and French onion soup to enjoy while we waited (which took less than 10 minutes). Dutch babies took maybe 25 minutes. Everything was really delicious and service was top notch👌🏽","The food was great at a reasonable price, the Dutch babies were the highlight. Service felt busy and rushed and we almost felt like we had to rush out for others to get a table. But otherwise we enjoyed the place and would probably come back on a regular day.","Delicious Dutch baby pancakes, totally worth the hype! The ambience is nice and cozy and the window seat gets a good view of the space needle. The staff is warm and friendly and the coffee and side dishes are a must try!","Small, intimate cafe.  It is small so recommend reservations, especially for Brunch.  They do have a small bar that you can sit at but it is first come and must be 21.  Really recommend planning ahead and making reservations.
With all that said, definitely worth it!  The service is top notch and the food is delicious!!  You will want to get a Dutch baby and they take 30 min. So get that order in right way. We got a sweet and a savory for the table to share. We got the baked eggs, biscuits and chicken sausage gravy, omelet, and Tilikum Fry up.  Menus vary so your options may be different but pretty sure they are all delicious!
The service is friendly, helpful, and on top of it!!  We never lacked for anything, and had great recommendations!
Atmosphere is cozy, with a European feel. Large window so feels a little bigger than it is.","It's a pretty rare thing to see a Dutch Baby on a menu - so when you see a place that pretty much specializes in them, you need to get one. So that's what I did coming over here. They're pretty much fully booked on weekends, so I would recommend only coming on a Thursday or Friday when they serve brunch

I got the classic dutch baby, with a hot chocolate.
The hot chocolate was weirdly bad? It came out lukewarm, which is just odd. I've never had a hot chocolate come out anything but piping hot before. It also just tasted pretty flat. not sure what was up with that

I will say that the dutch baby made up for it though.
This was really good. The cast iron pan was not large - about 5 inches across, but the crust overflowed the side on all directions, appearing like a blooming flower of dough.

Every part of this was great. The outer crust had a perfectly crispy when touching but soft when yielding-type texture, kind of similar to the best croissants but without the flakiness. The inside was so soft, so custardy, so eggy, it was just phenomenal. It reminded me a little bit of French toast but like only the inside if that makes sense. The crust at the bottom also managed to be hard without being burnt.
The lemon, powdered sugar, and maple syrup combined to make this taste so good

The price of an individual dutch baby is actually pretty reasonable. It's not very big at 5 inches across roughly, but only being $15 (menu price) is relatively cheap. It does take a while to come out though - mine took over 30 minutes

Overall, no other brunch place does this unique dish quite like here, which makes it absolutely worth a visit. 8.25/10"]

name: familyfriend
address: 3315 Beacon Ave S, Seattle, WA 98144, USA
reviews:
["We came because we heard about the burger - which after trying, is overhyped. The single patty burger to bun ratio was too much bread we could barely taste anything else. The corn soup was probably the best thing we tried - flavor was complex. Would suggest trying that though the chicken in the soup did taste like the pre packaged cubed chicken from Costco. The seafood tostada was fine, it tasted like chopped up ingredients that were put on a tostada. Not like a cohesive dish where the ingredients complement each other. The cocktails were super good though - propofol ice & Lola - well balanced and a unique twist on some classic cocktails! Service was great & food came out fast! Prices were steep though for what we got but I guess that’s Seattle…and the suggested gratuity STARTED at 22%,27%, and 32%….","This place was hailed as having one of the best burgers in town, and it did not disappoint. I loved the charred, crispy edges of the smash burgers 🍔 and the umami-rich flavor of the sauce and toppings they added to it. It also came with thin truffle fries 🍟 which were extra crispy and elevated — did not even need ketchup. We also shared the bulgogi red rice and I could eat this every day. The beef was tender and well-seasoned with that delicious bulgogi flavor. Ot came with an assortment of pickled vegetables and the rice was perfectly cooked and seasoned. I can’t wait to go back for more and try their chicken adobo tacos! We got there just as they opened at 5 PM and got a table immediately but by 5:30 it was packed!","Absolutely obsessed with Family Friend. The smash burger is unreal and the truffle fries were amazing. The corn chowder was also great and comforting.

The music is a whole vibe, the service is quick and friendly, and it truly feels like an IYKYK kind of spot. Everything about the experience feels easy and intentional.

They also have a great patio space that’s perfect for private parties, which I will definitely be utilizing in the future. If you know, you know.","Everything we had was incredibly tasty! I loved the vibes and the service. Although it was slightly pricey for the portion size, I absolutely loved the fusion dishes they created here and so I found it worth the price!","Family friend is such a fitting name. Awesome staff, awesome food, awesome environment.

We got the Seattle cider, followed by pineapple wheat. Then the propofol cocktail. All were really good. Food wise we all got the corn soup which is a MUST! Then a seafood tostada and the fried chicken. All of which was fantastic. Zero complaints. 100% coming back."]

name: Ba Bar Capitol Hill
reviews:
["Came here during a visit to Seattle for a conference and dined at the bar. The food was excellent (I tried a dish based on the bartender’s suggestion).  i wanted something spicy as had been eating bland food at the conference and he had the kitchen add some side sauces (siracha, red chillies etc that fit the bill)

The decor is great as was the service. Almost went back the following day as I was in the neighborhood but went to Carmello’s instead

Highly recommended!","The food was plated well and looked appealing. However, everything we ordered (Bun Cha Hanoi Bowl, Mi Bo Vien, Bun Cha Gio, and Crispy Imperial Rolls) was mid. I’m not exactly sure how to describe it. It just felt like it was missing the flavors that make it authentic. Maybe for someone who has never had Vietnamese food before, this is a good entry point.","We are at the tail end of our trip to Seattle and we’re SO happy we decided to stop by Ba Bar before we left to return back to Hawaii. A friend recommended Ba Bar to us  and my family and I were craving Vietnamese food, so we decided to give it a try. We stopped in for lunch and had the best dining experience we’ve ever experienced in Seattle. The food was seriously top-notch; my family and I devoured our entire 3 course meal. There wasn’t a drop left of our pho bowls, we ended up ordering another serving of the Saigon wings (MUST TRY!) and I even bought a bag full of their macarons to bring home to the island as gifts.
Our food was delicious, but the incredible service we received from Michelle is what made it all the more special. Michelle was so attentive, she gave us great recommendations, and she was super sweet. I don’t remember the last time my dad was so impressed with customer service, but he said that it felt like we were at a fancy steakhouse with the 5 star treatment we received from Michelle. My family left feeling happily full, our cravings satisfied, but also a little sad that we don’t have more time to pay Ba Bar another visit before we leave (although, I’m trying to convince my dad we need to stop there before the airport for one more round of the Pandan Vietnamese coffee lattes, which were ridiculously bomb) We honestly can’t wait to come back! Thank you Michelle and Ba Bar for the hospitality and treating us like ohana. Aloha until next time!","⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ This is one of our favorite local spots. The food is amazing, good service and fun atmosphere with a slappin’ vibe. The crispy imperial rolls were generously stuffed, the beef/lamb rolls were perfectly seasoned, the combo platter was huge and delicious, and the braised beef soup special was a delicious and delightful way to stay warm on a cold, rainy Friday night.

We love BaBar for the best Vietnamese food in the city. Also, when you compare prices to other Cap Hill restaurants, their prices are much better for the food and service you get.

Also their playlist is fantastic! A good mixture of pop music classics and current Vietnamese pop!","I ordered through Uber Eats. The food is below average by Vietnamese standards—at best about 30% authentic—with some odd modifications. The house meat patty has an unusual, off flavor. The sauce, which is the soul of this dish, is a complete miss: underseasoned and lacking depth. I honestly can’t understand why this place has so many good ratings; perhaps they’re from people who haven’t tried proper Vietnamese food. There was also no tamper-proof seal on my delivery, which is disappointing for a takeout order. That said, the restaurant looks nice, which may explain some of the hype."]

name: Saigon Drip Cafe
address: 161 S Washington St, Seattle, WA 98104, USA
reviews:
["Happy hour prices for 3 different appetizers was a great deal especially with the portion sizes. The flavor of vegetable broth in the pho was underwhelming but for the most part we found everything quite tasty. There ain't a ton of seating and the bar seats are especially uncomfortable. Very clean bathroom.","So so so good! The dumplings and whatever sauce they come with is one of my new favs. They have beer and other alcoholic drinks, tea, boba, Thai tea, pho, spring rolls! Lots of great stuff. We got the dumplings, spring rolls with tofu, tsigtao beer, and thai green tea. It was happy hour so it's totally affordable. Clean bathroom with a mouthwash dispenser lol. I've never seen that before and it works!!! Baby changing table in there too. It's a great little Seattle spot and we'll definitely be back again.","Forewarning for folks that might be sensitive to it, they add a good amount of paté in the banh mi which can have a very strong flavor, especially when warm. It's good because it adds a nice creaminess and savoriness. But if you aren't ready for it (like I was on my first bite) it can be a bit of a shock. I actually preferred the chicken and pork banh mis after they had a chance to cool down in the fridge versus eating them hot with all of that strong flavor. Absolutely devoured them then.

Otherwise the service was fast and friendly.

The flavor of everything was amazing and they put an absolute ton of meat in their banh mi and pho which makes them very filling, which is great.

Only cringe I had was the price. The pho had double the meat as another places but was also double the price which hurt the wallet. We were in the area on a trip all week and our lunch here was the most expensive meal of the entire trip. I get the downtown location means high rent but wowza lol.

I'd still go back for a filling bowl of pho or a hefty good banh mi every once in a while though.","Bánh mì drip - brisket is very rich, good but too rich. The group of us struggled to finish half of it. First few bites were fantastic. Not too expensive, would come back to try other dishes.","Driving through Pioneer Square we decided to grab a bite before going home, and Saigon Drip was on my list of places to try

I went for the brisket Banh Mi Drip.

Overall it was good, the bread seemed fresh, and the brisket was tender and soft. However it was seasoned with a very sweet sauce which I did not care for. I'm certain that most people would love the sweet sauce, but I don't like sweet sauce on meat.

For this reason I do not imagine that I'll return. I'm also certain that others will love it."]

name: 5 Spot
address: 1502 Queen Anne Ave N, Seattle, WA 98109, USA
reviews:
["I was visiting from London and happened upon this place having been to the nearby Kerry park for some sightseeing.

Nikki delivered an efficient and attentive service, the portion size of the food was big (but good!), coffee was fresh and tasty. Plenty of condiments on the table too.

I highly recommend getting the cinnamon roll with the breakfast too. Looks big but I promise it’s so fluffy and fresh!

This was an authentic American experience - one of the best breakfasts I’ve had in the USA so far!","Even on Valentine's Day the wait wasn't terrible. Either way it was WORTH IT!!! Our server Kenny was wonderful, service was incredibly quick. And the food was spot on, good portions, delicious flavors, and very attentive staff. A super warm and cozy environment, could have stayed there all day. Will be going back soon!","Been coming here for years now and the food is absolutely delicious. There are a ton of options but their French toast and egg dishes are just to die for.

The service is warm especially Kenny. We always have amazing time here 💖

This is also a kid friendly place with lots of food options and some coloring activities to keep your kiddos busy.","We visited 5 Spot Café on November 21, 2025, around 11 AM, and the experience ended up being a mix of great food and disappointing service. The place itself is lovely, very clean, bright, and welcoming, with that classic Queen Anne charm. It was also quite busy, which shows how popular it is.
We ordered a Build Your Own Omelette, Avocado Toast and Farmhouse Scramble and we also ordered a coffee. The food itself was very good fresh, well seasoned, and nicely presented. Honestly, the kitchen did a great job.

Where things fell apart was the service. For a place this busy, I expected a certain level of attentiveness, but our server seemed checked out from the moment we sat down. Zero attention, no real interaction, and extremely short responses. At one point, we were literally mid-order when one of us was still deciding, and the server simply walked away abruptly without saying anything. It felt dismissive and unprofessional, especially during a brunch rush when clear communication matters.

Despite the great food and clean environment, the service brought the overall experience down.","Delmer was amazing !! Great service and made our morning. The food was good and a homey local vibe with this place. We had a group of 5 and were sat right away although it was very busy. We ordered the spicy bloody marys, french toast chicken sandwich, 5 spot breakfast meal, and sonoran scrambled! All great and speedy with HUGE portions."]

name: Sound View Cafe
reviews:
["Excellent clam chowder and salmon sandwich.

Very friendly and helpful staff.

Reasonable price considered strong food quality, tourist location and spectacular harbour view.

A bit of wait time on a sunny warm Wednesday lunch slot.","Good place with a nice view. Great food options. Okay coffee and no WiFi or outlets. Recommend for a quick food while near Pike Place, but not ideal for sitting in and getting some work done.","We were up early walking and built up an appetite. Stopped in for a bite. The person at the counter was polite and welcoming. We each had pancakes and some drinks/coffee. Over all tasty, but no butter makes for a pretty dry pancake. Real maple syrup was good as was the fruit. Very expensive for pancakes. Great view!","Enjoyed a very tasty clam chowder bread bowl for an early lunch. Make sure you have a little time to digest afterwards -- it almost put me to sleep! Beautiful view, friendly staff, little to no wait around 11am on a Monday.","Decided to check this spot out while walking through the farmers market.

I ordered a mimosa pitcher, pancake breakfast, and a cup of clam chowder.

The clam chowder was awesome and probably one of the best I've ever tasted. The pancakes were fluffy, the eggs were solid, and the turkey bacon was as well. They do not serve any pork in this establishment.

Mimosa was fresh and spot on.

Friendly staff, quick service, and solid views.

If in the area, give them a try."]

name: Portage Bay Cafe
address: 4130 Roosevelt Way NE, Seattle, WA 98105, USA
reviews:
["The food was amazing. Just like always me and wife went on a quest for a good breakfast/brunch. We been craving some good benny and either pancakes or French toast. We tried Reuben Benedict(February special) and Farmer’s breakfast with a side of French toast. We enjoyed every bit of the food, a perfect brunch for Valentine’s day. We drove from across the town for this and totally worth it.","Great service with absolutely delicious food and drinks!!! The food comes out fast and the lines move quick. This is another excellent Portage Bay","Used to live across the street from this u district location

I had never been

Ordered the brisket dish

Wow, the brisket was harder than clam shells

Didn’t really enjoy this experience","At first I was a little put off by the need to have a reservation at a cafe for breakfast.  Then WOW now I understand.  The atmosphere is a mix between industrial and boho.  The staff is friendly and fast.  The food is outstanding.","Portage Bay is a chain of cafes in Seattle, the one in U District is pretty solid. The quality of the food is clearly very high, there menu ranges from sandwiches to breakfast stables to seasonal dishes like crepes and such. I personally have been here around 3 times, the environment is very very busy on the weekends, however the service is fast and attentive to whatever your needs are. I dont have a photo of it, but they have a toppings bar with some of the best whipped cream I've had - it also has a plethora of fresh berries. Overall great spot for brunch with the parents, I would recommend getting a reservation before you go for sure."]

name: Pete's Eggnest
address: 7717 Greenwood Ave N, Seattle, WA 98103, USA
reviews:
["This was my second time to Pete's Eggnest recently. The food was pretty good, and it server was friendly. I had the Mexican breakfast to which I added onions and asked for a side of pico de gallo. The meal was tasty and the atmosphere is cozy and welcoming.

However when we got our bill, I was a bit surprised at the up charges. The Mexican breakfast was rung up at $18.25 instead of the $16 on the menu. The very small pico de gallo side was $2.75. I understand an add-on charge for these items, but $2.25 for adding onions to a scramble and $2.75 for pico de gallo I feel is outrageous. There was also no mention about a fee for either the onions or pico when ordering them.  The onions weren't listed as a line item so it just looked like I was overcharged. I asked our server about it before paying, and she confirmed what the charges were for and apologized.

This crazy charge of $5 for onions and pico are unfortunately enough to make me not want to come back to Pete's Eggnest again. :(","Visited this little restaurant to get some breakfast before my flight out of Seattle. They have a small dining area, very welcoming. The waitresses greeted us as we came in. Looked at the menu which they did have a lot of pork products but they did have turkey sausage, beef sausage too. I ordered the corned beef hash, and I have to say,  The best part of that dish was the hashbrowns and oh my gosh their biscuits are yummy. Really enjoyed the biscuits. The eggs were cooked the way I wanted them. I really didn’t enjoy the corned beef hash, I’ve had better. Their menu is a little too pricey to me, corn beef hash breakfast that was like $20. I spent a little over $20 and my husband was thinking about getting a cheeseburger but the cheeseburger, I can’t remember it was either like between $15-$16. Like $16 for a cheeseburger was diabolical, so he declined. I really wanted some eggs so we decided to eat here.","I really wanted to love this place but our experience here wouldn’t let us. The staff were friendly and it felt like a cozy, family owned restaurant. I will note that we went on Saturday during prime brunch time, so they were busy. The food generally looked really good too. HOWEVER, our food was served cold/room temp (at best). Moreover, we had a long wait between standard events (sit down, taking our order, getting the food, getting the check, and paying).

It looked like there were not enough staff for the restaurant. Moreover, it also didn’t look like tasks were being delegated appropriately. It might seem like I’m focusing on small events, but there were many things like this that all added up to a generally chaotic and disorganized environment.

Also, the food was overpriced. Yes, this is Seattle (and almost everything here is overpriced). But this place was DEFINITELY over priced. I ordered a waffle (which was a good size but not that big) with two eggs and four sausage links. Total for that was $23 (before tax/tip).

Overall, the experience was disorganized/chaotic with food served cold, and prices that were too high.","Pete's Eggneat is a breakfast restaurant in Phinney. It's easy to get to but parking around here is awful so be prepared to walk. Inside they probably have less than 20 tables total, and it's crammed most of the time. They can accommodate more than 4 people but it's tough for them. There's usually a wait, where you put your name at the front and then come back. Their menu is a good size, with lots of breakfast options plus a few lunch ones as well. Their food is delicious, with portions being huge for the price. The staff are so nice, and always around, but it's always busy here so they're constantly running all over. Their hashbrowns are great, biscuits and gravy are heavy, and their bacon is nice and crispy. Overall a really good brunch spot with a local vibe.","Migas and lemon ricotta pancakes were very tasty and flavorful. Pro tip: you don't need syrup with the pancakes - they taste like you are digging into cake batter. The prices seem quite reasonable (considering this place is located within Seattle).

The wait staff was attentive and spoke to us very nicely. Try going in early to avoid the morning rush during weekends. We'll be back to try more stuff on the menu.

You can skip the coffee - it tasted like hot water."]

name: Wild Mountain Cafe
reviews:
["I ordered the Persian Sun, sounded delightful. Imagine the disappointment to be served dry, thin slices of wheat bread, nary an eggwash to actually deem it "French toast" in sight, or taste. It seemed straight from a grocery store loaf covered in chunks of tough canned pineapple, despite claiming "fresh chopped" - there's a discernible difference between the two. A slab of equally dry and tough ham dared to call itself breakfast.

The menu stated "cardamom-infused sesame tahini, topped with lemon honey syrup" though again, the flavors didn't come through. At all. Seemed run-of-the-mill jarred tahini with a scant portion of runny, watered down honey on top. Womp-womp. A lack of cardamom and lemon in full.

Squishing into the crowded, stuffy place to wait and wait was no fun either. Save your money for something more satisfying, worth the wait that often accompanies the brunch crowd. Another review mentioned a "bad homemade" quality, and that's exactly the summation of the meal I experienced, especially for the price.

Hmm, a look at a post on Yelp shows the Persian Sun as I expected it to be- thicker eggwashed French toast slices, a proper portion of honey syrup visible on the plate, and varied chunks of pineapple that actually look to be fresh. I'll upload it for a side-by-side comparison. Having barely touched my meal, I expected the server to say something, but alas, no. Le sigh. On to the next!","We went to Wild Mountain Cafe for a brunch on a Saturday morning. It was crowded and we had to wait for 15 mins before getting a table. The vibe of this restaurant is very cozy. We ordered a Veggie Bennie and Buddha's Happiness. Both were delicious! The service was quick. The staff were incredibly warm and friendly. Absolutely loved everything about this place. Will definitely be coming back to try more stuff soon!","I would give a 100 stars if I could! This place is phenomenal! Fantastic service, coffee, cinnamon rolls, cold brew with Ameretto, and Irish cream! Grace knew ALL about the menu, and explained how the home was made into a really cool restaurant/Cafe. They bake, and prep downstairs.  My daughter got Jeremy jubilie, garlic potato cakes that melt in your mouth! With apple sausage, garlic, and zuke scramble. I got a Hawaii- 5 oh. Ham, Swiss, pinepple sandwich, and salad! BEST sandwich! I want to take everyone here, and show this place off! Great atmosphere! A great experience!","delicious garlic potato cakes! Got the Purdy Girl scramble. It was good but very wet and missing some flavors - the garden scramble was definitely missing flavor. Needs some herbs and spices in there. Was hoping the maple syrup would be the real stuff too...

Very good service and very sweet ambiance. I’d come back to give them another try. I really loved the charm of this place but I’m not sure what I’d order next time. Maybe black coffee and more garlic potatoes!","I’m on a business trip in Seattle and stopped by Wild Mountain Cafe for brunch. The atmosphere was cozy and inviting, and the Eggs Benedict was fantastic. Emma, our server, was incredibly friendly and welcoming. I’ll definitely come back next time I’m in Seattle!"]

name: Seattle Children's Museum
reviews:
["Awesome place, well designed, with many interactive rooms (including a vet room, a theatre, a construction site, a doctors office, a farmers market, a fire station etc) and activities galore (a hiking trails, a boat, a ball drop, Lego walls etc) Best for kids up to 6-7 years old IMO. Tickets are for the whole day and cover multiple entries if you want to take your little one out for a nap or lunch and bring them in later.","Avoid. Rip off place.  So this place charges for parents and kids. $16 -$18 each. Despite parents doing nothing but watching and standing around and looking at their phones. They claim that you will be interacting with them guaranteed. All I see is parents on phones. Kids are fine by themselves. This place isn't as cool as interactive as the one that Olympia.  Just be upfront with the pricing, charge more for just the kids. Parents don't go and play around all these little kids toys. Sounds like a dishonest business practice.","The Seattle Children’s Museum is a great spot for little kids, but getting there the first time is a bit of a maze. It’s on the bottom floor of the Armory building — make sure you take the main elevator in the center of the food court, not the side elevators, or you’ll end up wandering around confused.

Once you’re inside, though, it’s fantastic. There are tons of activities and play areas, and plenty of seating scattered throughout so parents can actually sit down while the kids explore. My 18-month-old had an absolute blast, and even two hours wasn’t enough time to cover everything at her pace before nap time. The changing rooms and stations are clean and well-maintained, and there are designated snacking areas too.

My biggest complaint — and the reason I don’t get a membership — is the parking situation. It’s a mess. There are lots of different parking lots around Seattle Center, but they’re expensive, and most involve a long walk if you’re hauling strollers and kids. If they ever fix that, this place would be perfect.

It does get busy, especially on rainy days but it's so big and spacious that it didn't feel too crowded on a Tuesday morning.","So disappointing.  Their website says”designed for kids 10 and under.”  I would say more like 4 and under.
I took a newly turned 6-year-old.  He was bored in no time.  And he was definitely the oldest child there.  It was primarily toddlers.  When I mentioned this to the front desk, I was told, “yeah, we’ve been talking about adding something for older kids.”  I really wish their website had been more honest.
Go to the Pacific Science Center instead.  It is large, well-done, very fun, and educational.","Honestly, this place does not deserve the name “Seattle Children’s Museum.”

The space is extremely small, with very limited exhibits and almost nothing engaging. The overall experience is worse than a small indoor play area in a shopping mall, let alone a museum.

There is little to no meaningful interactive content or educational value.
The name sets expectations far too high, and the reality is very disappointing.

Not worth a special trip."]

name: Pacific Science Center
address: 200 Sue Bird Ct N, Seattle, WA 98109, USA
reviews:
["Went here for the Pink Floyd- Dark Side of the Moon lazer light show. It was awesome! You can pre purchase tickets or buy them at the door, $17 for adults. They have an assortment of snacks & beverages for sale as well. I recommend bringing a pillow & blankets to lay on because u get the best view that way. Otherwise , if laying on the floor is uncomfortable for you , they also have seats around the edges for you to sit on. The whole length of the show was about an hour or a little more. The bathrooms are located outside right before the entrance, which were very cool. They were called the Grossology Restrooms with bathroom science facts & pictures. Cool for adults or kids and funny. My 9 year old niece really enjoyed it.
Also if you are handicapped it may be difficult accessing certain areas due to the construction going on. We had to travel down a few sets of stairs, not long ones tho only 1-4 steps max to get to where the lazer dome was.","This place would be really cool with kids but was a bit underwhelming (or maybe overwhelming) for adults. The spider exhibit was awesome, a good mix of interactive displays and displays for looking at/reading about. The light show was also a must-see. But all of the main space/physics exhibits were really just interactive items geared toward kids without much museum/non-interactive items to learn more from as adults. I think I was expecting a bit more museum-type exhibits in the mix. There were not nearly enough hand sanitizer stations out in the main exhibit area for us to want to interact with all the hands-on exhibits, especially with it being the time of year for sharing illnesses.","This is a must go to if you’re visiting Seattle with kiddos! My family & I really enjoyed all the exhibits offered. There is a lot of hands on and what I really appreciated was that there was a little sensory room for any kids that may get overstimulated easily. Also, if you can make a reservation for the laser show, do it! We enjoyed a laser show full of kpop demon hunter songs. Highly recommend coming here and make sure to bring snacks!","This place is great for kids and adults too! I learned so much about space and the brain.  I loved the toddler preschool age planetarium show for my 16 month old even if it's best for 2-5. It was a very slow day with only two babies and their moms today. Usually it's chaos with a little dancing part, the guide said, but today it was quiet and relaxing! The babes were loving sitting back on mom and watching the show.

I loved the interactive exhibits.  Baby's favorites were the bubble station, the gears, the Bernoulli effect balls, and the twist button where you see what color a star turns the hotter it gets.  My favorites were the butterfly house (by far!!) and the show in the planetarium.","The Pacific Science Center is currently being renovated as of February 2026. The courtyard used to be available to paying customers only, but is now open to thr general public and has a beautiful fountain area with large sculptures and a few interactive water features.

We came and visited for the spider and insect exhibit and our toddler absolutely loved it. There was plenty of kid friendly material and larger than life sculptures to look at.  We particularly loved the Butterfly house.

My husband and I are looking forward to coming back again in the future for a laser dome date."]

name: PACCAR IMAX Theater at Pacific Science Center
address: 200 Sue Bird Ct N, Seattle, WA 98109, USA
reviews:
["Visited the Boeing IMAX theater and had an impressive experience. The screen is massive, and the picture quality is extremely sharp, making it perfect for documentaries and aviation-related films. The sound system is powerful and immersive without being overwhelming.

The seating was comfortable, and the overall environment felt well-maintained and organized. It’s a great place to learn and enjoy larger-than-life visuals, especially if you’re interested in aerospace or science. Definitely worth a visit.","Good for one time experience. The theater itself is fine and the exhibits are great. But it runs on fcfs basis, so if you’re not willing to stay in line up to an hour or late you’ll get the left over seats which are not gonna bring you the best impression about the show. Around 400 seats, so pretty crowded if fully booked + if it’s an expected premiere the tickets are going to be sold out weeks before the show(We went for 10 year anniversary Interstellar) The screen is big and high quality. The parking is another disaster, will take you at least 30 mins and all paid spots, except it’s Sunday and you’re lucky to find roadside parking for free. Again, exhibits were worth much more than a film for me, a lot of experimental and cognitive content around, 100% worth of the time you’ll spend walking around especially with kids is a MUST","Boeing side of IMAX is amazing. Love it to the moon and back. ….. BUT THE PACCAR SIDE seats are horrible for a 2.5 hour long Demon Slayer movie.

Who decided to put in such horrible seats to then show movies at? Am I the only one who thinks this? Am I just the angry old man yelling at the moon?","The only imax in Washington state. Large screen thunderous sound system, nice seatings. There’s no assigned seating - aka first come first served. So come early if you want to get the best view. The area around the theater is also nice to check out.","How in the world does this theater not have the ability to show an Imax film?  Digital is cheaper but not as in​volved.  Seattle deserves a top shelf Imax theater.  Right now, your only options are a three hour drive north or 10 hours south."]

name: Science Center Outdoor Water Area
reviews: ["Its got stone dinosaurs  🦕  its a cool feature ,  this outside water area  most of it has no water due to environmental reasons","Fun place to visit for adults and children.","Always fun to too visit the water park","This place is not well maintained. Half the exhibits are broken. Half the fountains are empty. The butterfly exhibit is the only saving grace. Not worth the price of admission at $36 per person.","The trip backwards into 1962..."]

name: Museum of Illusions - Seattle
reviews:
["My family really enjoyed the museum. I have three boys age 19,15 and 11. We did this because we had a day before the football game and it was well worth it.
If you have teenagers make sure they eat before so they don't give you an attitude. Do all the activities and the staff are very engaged and helpful throughout the entire tour.

Again do all the activities otherwise you're in and out in 30 minutes and for the price you wanna get your money worth.","Awsome, interesting, fun museum.....its location is good in downtown Seattle  nearby to lots of public transportation, hotels etc......the vortex here is alot of fun ...this museum is also ideal for picture   taking of yourself, friends, family  and staff also offer to take pictures for you too  ,similar to selfie 🤳 museum type of idea......love that this museum is open till late at nights 🌙  i wish most museums In Seattle were like this open till late nights 🌙......they got small gift shop, washrooms......staff here are friendly and helpful.....you don't need to buy advanced tickets  cause walk in is very welcomed here like tickets at the door type  ....i definitely recommend this museum .... its a decent small size/medium  size of museum  ....really nice time visiting here in the late evening","This is a pretty cool spot and we had a lot of fun with my nieces. They have lots of different cool things that will have you wonder how is that possible. I definitely recommend going there. This is the type of place that you only go once. The only downside is that it is pretty short and I expected a little more things. But overall, it’s a fun place to visit and I recommend taking your kids or nephews, nieces.","Kids of various ages really enjoyed the exhibits. A little tricky with all the people in small areas, but everyone was polite and waited turns when necessary.  Make sure you're in the mood to read brief instructions and details, especially when accompanying kids who are inattentive.  We all commented afterwards that we were pleasantly surprised we enjoyed it as much as we did.  Gift shop was not as impressive, but that's a difficult sell.  Great first date option to help break the ice. Subscribe online and receive a discount prior to purchase.","Fun interactive museum with many rooms of illusions. Bring your phone because some of the illusions work better with your phone also, you will want to take many pictures. There are puzzles to work on and many cool props. The museums store has some great gifts as well."]

name: Laser Dome at the Pacific Science Center
reviews:
["The show was amazing but I really believe it should switch to a no photo/video area like a movie theater. I was reprimanded by staff for moving seats during the performance, but not the people in front of me who were recording with their phone brightness on full blast in a completely dark amphitheater, which was extremely distracting. Overall great experience in the staff was friendly and attentive. I feel like this is an issue with higher management.","A bit tricky to find (i am new to the area) went to the Lana Del Rey show, at first me and friends were not into it thinking we might be too old. seems more for kids but once we laid down and just let ourselves enjoy it it was so nice and so cool and the staff were great(we are 24)","We had such a blast at the Friday daft punk show!! The sound system was excellent, the lasers were intricate and mesmerizing, and the staff were lovely. Shout out to Michelle for being so warm and welcoming AND their incredible knowledge of laser technology!!","SZA and lasers go so well together. Excellent performance, with a very lively and energetic MC. My only wish would be using more of the dome for the lasers, as it is mostly on the front half. Still absolutely worth the price, I would highly recommend the evening shows for a fuller experience","The line to get in was long and there was only two people checking for tickets. They had overpriced concessions as well. The place felt overcrowded . Some people lay down near the exit doors.

The visualization reminded me of windows media player visualization from the 90's and windows screen savers.  Only 2 out of the 14 songs had an interesting visualization.

I wouldn't do it again given the price point and experience."]

name: Kids Science Labs - Seattle
reviews:
["Our, now almost 6 year old, child loved going to kids science lab for a few weeks over the summer. Everyday he would bring home a new project that he worked on at camp and was always very excited about what he had learned. We had his birthday party there last year and that went over very well. For about half the time, the kids are in a separate room, which you can see into, and the parents are in another room. So it was nice that the parents could chat while the kids were participating in a science experiment in the adjoining room.","We just celebrated a 7th birthday party over the weekend, and I was blown away by the amazing job by the KSL team (shout out to Nessa, Sailor, Neenah, and Kai!) to make the party fun and engaging for the guests and also memorable for the birthday kid. The KSL team took care of everything--from setting up food/drinks and the party room, making announcements to direct the flow of events, serving food/cutting cake/lighting candles/etc., and taking pictures of the event--so that the hosting parents are able to enjoy the party too. I would not hesitate to book a party here if you're considering!","We were lucky to be invited to a birthday party at this place.Found the place to be very interesting ,exciting and well managed.

Kids started with free play outside and can read books or do some colouring.

Then they went to the experiment room and did some very interesting experiments with the wonderful crew members.

Then the party moved to another theme decorated room where the cake was cut and snacks were served.

A small coffee station was a nice addition for waiting parents 😊

The place had two washrooms.

Good thing that the entry is Disabled friendly.

Overall a very good experience.Kids really enjoyed and the experiment return gift box in the end was a cherry to the cake 😊","We celebrated our boys' recent birthday here and we couldn't have made a better choice. Space and location were more than ideal and their staff was very friendly and courteous. Special recognition and thanks to Sam who was the lead scientist for our group. He had the kids (and parents) engaged the entire time. His ability to communicate with the kids at their level was impressive.

"That was the best birthday party ever!" -kid

"Wow, Sam is amazing." -parent","They did an excellent job hosting a birthday party for us - Nessa and her team are party planning gurus. I liked the check ins I received from KSL in regards to the party, as someone who maintains a pretty full calendar this place was the best. Thank you KSL!"]

name: Tropical Butterfly House
reviews: ["My new favorite place in Seattle is the butterfly house 🏠 🦋🦋🦋🏵🌸🌴 staff here give good information on the butterflies 🦋  ( please be careful were you walk on the ground  here watch for butterflies on the ground  )   . I wish this place was bigger   but  still enjoyable &  better then nothing .  I love ❤️ this place","This was by far the best experience at the PSC. Countless butterflies fluttered about in a very warm enclosure with food and flowers for their snacking pleasure. Busy but not too busy, plenty of space to enjoy the local residents. Would recommend if you already are planning of visiting the Pacific Science Center.","Awesome beautiful butterflies. Make sure to watch your step while walking, as some butterflies might be resting. It will be little warm inside, so clothe accordingly. You go inside in group and come outside with same group as they need to maintain number of people inside. Follow the instructions and enjoy seeing these beauties inside!","The Tropical Butterfly House is awesome. It promotes awareness of the conservation effort in El Salvador. Watching the butterflies flying inside the green house was very pleasant.","If you're looking for a calm, magical escape, definitely check it out!"]

name: The ART INN Seattle - Family Room with car parking included
Kids Discovery Museum
address: 301 Ravine Ln NE, Bainbridge Island, WA 98110, USA
reviews:
["Giving this place 2 stars only because my child had fun. On their website it says look for new pricing in December. I’m writing this on March 8th and prices have not been updated. Drove 35 minutes to come here to pay more than it cost to go to the zoo. Whole 2nd floor was shutdown as well.","Big enough to keep them busy. Small enough to keep track easily. Beautifully staged and engaging for all ages. Plenty of benches and comfy chairs. Coffee shop on the corner is good and they let you bring in drinks but no food. They have a small shaded outdoor area with pea gravel and toys too!","UPDATE:  Oh Hooray!  There is a new director!  I am feeling hopeful and will plan another visit soon then!  Insert smiley emojis!!  ****We have visited this children's play room 4 times now.  It is a lovely environment overall.  I appreciate the washing of hands prior to entry.  What the museum needs to change is the constant tidying by staff.  When children play, mess occurs!  This is part of the play experience.  With the staff constantly walking around tidying as a child is trying to play they are really giving the message that play is not really invited or allowed.  It comes across as obsessive.  Change your routine to having staff tidy about once every 2 hours or once an hour.  Encourage the children to have fun rather than focusing on a tidy play room.  Also, the staff @ every visit comes across rude.  Hopefully a new manager will be hired and it will become an inviting, fun environment!","Fun place for kiddos. Make a day out of it and take the Ferry from Seattle over! We played for probably 1.5 hours at the Discovery Museum. Had lunch and returned to the city. Fun! My kiddo is 2, loved it!","Small but fun kiddie museum in Bainbridge that's a short walk from the ferry terminal. Great for young kids (children older than 7 will likely be bored). My kiddo enjoyed playing for two hours (recommended max stay per covid restrictions).

Check the schedule before heading here and for program/activities update. You can buy your ticket online or in-person.

Recommend buying an activity box (we got the constellation themed one)to play at home and extend your KiDiMu fun."]

name: KidsQuest Children's Museum
reviews:
["One of the best experiences I ve had with my kid. Spent there a whoke day between 11am and 3pm. 2 floors of absolute fun. A real paradise for younger kids. I'd say 1-7/8 yo. The museum stated 0-10 but i'd say it's great from crawling babies to somebody of 7-8yo. Older may find it cool too but might be not comfortable being surrounded by all those little people. There is no food there but they several tables for snacks if you brought them. Big plus is the option to go out, have a lunch outside, and be back to adventures again. Since tickets give you a whole day admission. Highly recommended!","Great place for kids, including toddlers! There’s something for every child to enjoy, with a wonderful variety of toys and creative play stations. The storytime teacher was energetic and engaging. Parking can be a bit tricky depending on the time of day, and although you get access the whole day, it could be tricky to come back. We’ll definitely be back!","Liked it wish they had more parking, and a covered outdoor play area. Smaller facility than Seattle one but still fun, water ways and package delivery were our favorite parts.","This Museum is an amazing destination for kids to play, explore, and learn! This hands-on museum offers a variety of engaging activities for children of all ages.

One of the highlights is the water play area, where kids can splash, experiment, and discover how water flows and interacts with different objects. The grocery shopping pretend play section is also a favorite, allowing little ones to role-play as shoppers or cashiers, sparking creativity and teamwork.

For toddlers, there’s a dedicated area with age-appropriate toys and activities, ensuring even the youngest visitors have a safe and exciting experience. The museum also offers plenty of books to read, along with building toys and trains, encouraging both quiet moments and imaginative play.

Educational exhibits about farms, bees, and worms are thoughtfully designed, providing kids with fun ways to learn about nature and the environment. It’s a great opportunity for hands-on learning that captures their curiosity.

Overall, Kids Quest Museum is an exceptional space for children to have fun and learn at the same time. I would totally recommend it to any family looking for an interactive, enriching experience for their kids!","A lot of fun exibits that kept kids (6 and 4) entertained for hours. It does get crowded and the parking lot is small so come early or be prepared for a little bit of a walk."]

name: Seattle Aquarium
address: 1483 Alaskan Way Pier 59, Seattle, WA 98101, USA
reviews:
["Okay let me give my honest review.
We recently visited the Seattle Aquarium and had mixed feelings.

Tickets were about $140 for two adults and one child, which felt pretty steep for what we experienced overall. The original part of the aquarium was honestly underwhelming. It had that typical strong aquarium smell, and some areas looked a little grimy and uncared for. A few exhibits were empty or had very little to see (one bird in a large space, for example). It just didn’t feel like the price matched the experience in that section.

That said — the new building is AMAZING. The engineering and architecture alone are worth seeing. It feels modern, immersive, and beautifully designed. You can tell a lot of thought and artistry went into that expansion, and it completely changes the vibe.

Overall, I think the aquarium has a lot of potential, but at $140 for a small family, I expected more consistency throughout. If the older sections were updated or better maintained, it would feel much more worth the cost.","Don’t forget to go to both buildings!! Totally goofed and had to come back to see the other building. You have to pay at the original building and can go to the pavilion after. The pavilion tanks seem to be taken care of a bit better but overall very cool exhibits and animals.","The aquarium supplies is water from the Puget Sound!! One of the buildings is much more open to the outside than the other two, so if you're going while it's kind of chilly, be sure to take your coat! The new construction is for tropical fish and the water has to be heated to around 70° for them. That's my favorite building.","My boyfriend and I visited the aquarium for the first time in February and we loved it! All the staff is so helpful and educated. I can’t remember the front desk girls name, but she was so helpful right when we walked in! One of the best aquariums you could go to.","Discounts accepted (military, etc). Fantastic aquarium with nicely spaced exhibits.  Animals were active during 10am visit. Tanks were extremely clean. You could even see fish kept behind the scenes thanks to large windows.  We saw some cuddlefish that way.  Love to see fish and aquatic life not seen at other aquariums and this one hit the mark. Especially enjoyed the microscope exhibit showing sea life from the ocean water sample.  Staff were very knowledgeable."]

name: Seattle Public Library - Central Library
reviews: ["The Seattle Public Library Central Library is hands down the best public library I have ever visited! This 10 floor building is an architectural marvel. I absolutely love the open-floor design where you can look all the way down from the very top floor to the first. it's spectacular. The unique layout of the different floors and the vast selection make it a destination in itself. Even if you're not planning to check out a book, you must visit this building just to experience the incredible space. A definite Seattle highlight!","The Seattle Public Library is one of the most impressive libraries I’ve ever visited. The building itself is a work of art — bold, modern architecture with glass walls and open spaces that feel bright and inspiring. Inside, it’s incredibly spacious, with quiet corners for reading and wide areas that showcase the city’s creativity. The collection is massive, covering everything from classic literature to local Seattle history. Even if you’re not a big reader, it’s worth visiting just to take in the design and atmosphere.","Beautiful architecture! Definitely worth a quick visit if you’re close by. There are quite a lot of homeless people hanging around in here, but they seem to just be keeping to themselves. Lots of staff around too which is great.","I’ve lived in Seattle for three years and been to other SPL locations but only now thought to stop by this location and I wish I came sooner. This might be the largest and cleanest library I’ve ever visited. It feels very majestic and I can’t believe this exists right in the middle of downtown Seattle. Signed up for a SPL membership and will be back!","Design by OMA (Rem Koolhaas) and built in 2004. It is the culmination of Rem Koolhaas’s design approach on programming. Five distinct boxes with different programs float on top of each other, while the building envelope wraps around the volume to create interstitial spaces. The book stack box features an internal ramp spiraling upwards towards the study/reading area on the top floor. Incredible piece of architecture. A must go in Seattle!"]

name: Capitol Hill Branch - The Seattle Public Library
address: 425 Harvard Ave E, Seattle, WA 98102, USA
reviews:
["The design of this library is the best: large windows, high ceiling, mainly open layout. In addition to that, there are nooks by the windows with padded seats and backboards. The staff are always nice and helpful. This branch is not my home branch, but I like to visit it anyway whenever I am in the area.","It's pretty crowded in here today, which I'm not as huge a fan of, but even so, there are some computers available, and I'm on my laptop at the desks nearby.

The natural light, big windows, high ceilings, all of it is terrific. There's a real vibe in here, which I appreciate.

There are quite-comfy chairs, though they're all occupied at the moment (jealous!). I will definitely be back to this library next time I'm in the area. A comfy, nice space.","I have known for some time the Seattle Public Library branches in certain neighborhoods have been doing double duty as a social service center as well, Ballard, University, Northeast branches carrying a heavy load. When I visited the Capitol Hill branch, however, I was gripped by the kindness of the library employees and how far they will go to take care of their adopted community, some of them bringing high-protein bars,  handwarmers, and winter clothing at their own expense. While serving the more fortunate patrons just as dutifully, the Capitol Hill library workers were genuinely _seeing_ everyone and respecting each person's dignity. Absolutely impressed by the staff's resourcefulness and deeply moved by their kindliness.","This library is a bit crowded, for my tastes, which could just be a function of when I visited.

Even so, there are several computer available. I'm on my laptop at the desks nearby, and I enjoy the layout and big windows A LOT!

The high ceilings feel kind of epic and there's a bit of an architectural Moment happening here, if dating an architect for ten years taught me anything (and, boy, did it).

I am glad to know this is here, and if I'm nearby for other reasons, this is a very pleasant space to duck into to get some writing done.","Good library, honestly. Cozy, clean, there are bathrooms and free wifi. However it is public library, so the contingent here is all over the place, there is some smell, some kids yelling, but nothing crazy or uncomfortable."]

name: International District/Chinatown Branch - The Seattle Public Library
reviews:
["Such a nifty little branch, essential for the community. Largest Chinese, Japanese, Vietnamese and Korean collections after Central Library. Very patient folks work there to help visitors. ❤️","This is a small library but it seems under utilized so I've never had difficulty finding a place to sit. There are only a handful of computers available but there are laptops available for check out at the desk.

This has become my favorite spot to waddle to for post uwajimaya meal digestion.","This library has books in all languages, including Japanese, Chinese, Vietnamese, Korean, etc.
You can choose books from a large selection and enjoy reading in a calm atmosphere.
And the best part is that the staff are wonderful.
I am sure you will have a great experience","I was there to return my he dvds I borrowed and to update my address proof. The staff was very helpful and  were quick. This library branch is right in the middle of china town and you can explore the china town by walk.
There is only street parking and it might be bit difficult to get a spot.","I’m charging my phone in a zen like environment. I like the tea cups art."]

name: Douglass-Truth Branch - The Seattle Public Library
address: 2300 E Yesler Wy, Seattle, WA 98122, USA
reviews: ["The Douglass-Truth library is named after Frederick Douglass and Sojourner Truth and is such a nice part of the community. I really like the architecture here, the librarians are really nice. It’s also spacious, and the library is open and clean. Definitely worth visiting.","Very nice and clean library. Peaceful place to have a read. I think I lost my book mark because I left it in a book I returned here. Sort of weird hours on Tuesdays and Thursdays. Lovely local library!","This is definitely one of the cooler libraries not only is it have a really old part but they’ve had an addition that totally transforms this place in an amazing space KCLS is great at making all their libraries fit its community that they serve. The addition is underground you can’t tell it’s there you got to see this amazing library.","Friendly staff and awesome collection of books! I borrowed five books upon my first visit.\n\n[Update]: I now come here regularly to return books/borrow new books. It has such a great collection of books for history, travel, and outdoor activities. This is one of the places I would miss if I ever leave Seattle.","It's a very beautiful library but the staff are very judgemental against white people and asked me if I knew I was at the African American library. I kept getting side eyed and heard them saying that I must be lost because I'm white."]

name: Beacon Hill Branch - The Seattle Public Library
address: 2821 Beacon Ave S #5813, Seattle, WA 98144, USA
reviews:
["Great place to bring kids or to study. They have a large kids section that includes books written in Spanish, Chinese, and Vietnamese. There are educational games kids can play as well.

There is a great selection of CDs and DVDs as well as some private study rooms. There’s a parking lot that makes this location easy to access.

Staff were friendly and they put a lot of work into curating amazing resources and relevant seasonal content.","I love all the worker here except for the 2 security men coz there always picking on me and both of them ( black guy and Asian guy ) are power triper. But over all at that place I loved it and me and my boyfriend feels that we matter even though we're homeless... I am happy that I chose beacon hill to live and soon will get our own place here at beacon Hill area.","Great children's area with small tables, medium tables, lots of toys and books. They also have a playgroup and storytime with energetic teachers/conductors. We had a lot of fun!","Awesome library. Beautiful space, great staff, great kid’s section. Can’t wait to make it to story time.","06/29/23: this branch’s architecture is modern and attractive to me a lot. I feel comfortable and relaxed visiting this branch for my work and reading experience. One minor experience is that many homeless people are coming to use the Internet here, and they smell bad. I know the library cannot control it but hopefully, they can guide them to a public shower/restroom.

The staff is wonderful and hospitable. Love taking my girlfriend here for reading"]

name: Queen Anne Branch - The Seattle Public Library
address: 400 W Garfield St, Seattle, WA 98119, USA
reviews: ["Charming, clean, small branch in a safe area with plenty of safe street parking. As other reviews state, it’s quite small, so voices carry. I wish the staff would encourage people to speak quietly and have signs as reminders because it is cavernous so voices echo. Someone talking across the library sounds like they are standing next to you. It would be better if they put in sound absorption because the building is designed to echo with its rounded ceilings. When people don’t have self-awareness or library etiquette, it’s quite loud. Otherwise, a charming spot that has a wood-church-library feel. Would be heavenly if there was a fireplace, one can dream :P","Small but mighty, this is my favorite of the Seattle Public Libraries. The library is very well organized and clean. It’s very easy to place item holds online and quickly pick up. Thank you to this wonderful library staff.","Beautiful library that always has a wonderful book display for children according to the month or season. Everyone is respectful of trying to keep quiet and staff is very friendly helpful. Plus this location has the added bonus of Molly Moon’s and Top Pot being just 1 block away.","Beautiful library. Love the stain glass windows.","Love this branch! The staff are so friendly and always have great recommendations. The stained glass windows make for a nice reading ambience!"]

name: Friends of The Seattle Public Library
address: Central Library, 1000 4th Ave Level 3, Seattle, WA 98104, USA
reviews: ["Great staff and shop. Best organization going.","Awesome place to read, use wifi or use computer. A place when it's  hot or cold outside you can sit down and relax. Plenty of space to move around and of course restrooms.","Satisfactory experience overall. Very friendly and easygoing.","Love my books...but enough is enough! Donated to the greatest library system anywhere!"]

name: Fremont Branch - The Seattle Public Library
reviews: ["Whatever this building once was, its acoustics are not conducive to being a library. In fact I would call them antithetical. Several experiences there have also shown me that, despite being aware of this (how could they not?), the library staff do not seem to care and don't make an effort to speak more quietly to patrons or even amongst themselves, as if they were simply resigned to the nature of sound in the space. But come on, that's like, childhood lesson #3...'shhh, you're in a library'.\nThree stars because it fulfills its function as a repository of books, at least.","A very compact but cozy and efficiently organized space — do note that it can be a little noisy just due to the nature of the small space, would recommend earphones if you are planning to study or focus here. But otherwise for leisurely reading or browsing this place is excellent.","Not a great selection of DVDs, which is what I went there for. Also wasn’t too impressed with the book selection but it’s a pretty small space so they are doing their best. I love the outdoor garden and amphitheater area!","An excellent library with super friendly staff who helped myself and my friends rest and get water in the Summer heat! (Plus, I got to read the Dexter books as I recharged!) Truly an incredible and cozy space -- thank you all who work here for making this an amazing library!","Incredible library. Picked up the bezonomics book to read while charging my phone on the free chargers. Lovely experience, good ventilation, and quiet. Overall recommend!"]

name: West Seattle Branch - The Seattle Public Library
reviews: ["This is one of my favorite branches in Seattle. Spacious, clean, friendly customer service, good lighting. The building itself is beautiful and apparently, it is the oldest public library building in Seattle (1910).","Very clean, helpful staff, and an old school building with a nice aesthetic. Also they have books and computers you can use of course. What a library should be","We like this library, every person, who works here is very kind and helpful!\n\nWe really enjoy Story time with Les Lee! We are crazy about it, She prepares to StoryTime very well, puts her heart into it and you can feel it, every story time is different, she is amazing and funny! it is very magic! We’ll be going to her again and again! And we also like Volunteer, who helps Les Lee to prepare tables and gather the tickets, she is very kind, maybe her name is Katie, I don’t know exactly","Very helpful staff and a very beautiful building. Clean library well stocked with wonderful books. Spaced out where you don't feel like you're sitting on top of somebody or somebody is breathing down your neck this is one of the smaller branches but it is a super nice location you can tell the employees love their job there","I cane here a couple of times, it looks nice on the outside but not so nice inside. There is a homeless man who comes every single day. Constantly moves around, talks to himself and is a real nuisance. If you want quiet time to read, study or use the computer. This is not the place"]

name: Madrona-Sally Goldmark Branch - The Seattle Public Library
reviews: ["Staff here are great, every one is so kind and welcoming. Parking is limited, but there is off street parking. It's a smaller library but I've been to smaller in Seattle. Great for the neighborhood. I was able to hang out here for a few hours and it was as pleasant as can be.","My go to library. Librarians are friendly and helpful. Book drop outside for after hours returns. This library is small. I mostly do online book holds and pick up here. There are 2/3 parking spots right in front, or you can street park. They have a small kids corner that works for short drop ins. *Capitol Hill has a much bigger kids area with larger selection and table and chairs.","This is the smallest SPL I’ve visited so far. Love the art outside the library","Small, but selection is appealing (and one can always request a loan) and staff is delightful. I adore Xavier! My only negative experience has been encountering two women who may have been administrators rather than true librarians; they bridled when I cautioned about censorship, and the fact that relishing \"Huckleberry Fin\" and the like as a child did not make me  \"racist\".   (Never seen them since, thank goodness.) For the most part, librarians are my heroes. Feel lucky to have a branch in my neighbourhood.","Love this library! It's small, but they have a good selection. The books the staff pull out and put on top the shelves are really amazing recommendations. I've read so many books that I wouldn't have otherwise picked up because the staff showcased them this way. Thank you for all you do!"]

name: Montlake Branch - The Seattle Public Library
reviews: ["A small but cozy branch of the Seattle library, with a very earthy and woodsy feeling to it. The architecture is nothing special, but it does allow a lot of light to soak the building, and the light shines on the wooden paneling used everywhere here. The atmosphere is distinctly Pacific Northwest: classy and subdued and vaguely ecological, not to dissimilar from that which one would find in Scandinavia.
