I do not know if I would intentionally take a long journey to find this place, but it is a serene neighborhood library, and it is a good place to visit when one is in the area.","Small and cozy library , limited free customer parking restrooms available , One meeting room available , and there is no quiet study room in the library library representative are friendly , and they answer the inquiry with much patience, they have children' s space in the library environment is really good","Small newer facility. Wish they were open longer hours.","Love this library! Super cute, kinda small but perfect for the neighborhood. 3 computers for public use but they have laptops you can check out. Meeting rooms upstairs parking down below there's an elevator to get from the bottom entrance (& parking) to the upstairs. A patio that's only open during the summer, lots of outlets and areas to sit and study, two drinking fountains a little area for kids books and story time sometimes and there's a men's room and women's room both with closed doors I know I have an issue with some libraries how they have the doors to the bathroom propped open and it's 5 feet from the checkout desk not here","Nice library"]

name: Olympic Garage
reviews:
["Fine garage, working elevator, easy pay stations. Was fairly packed at 1pm on a Saturday, but spots available. Seemed fairly short, probably couldn't fit a tall vehicle.","⚠️ WARNING — Do NOT park at Olympic Parking Garage (5th Ave, Seattle)

I had a horrible experience here. My belongings were stolen from my car while parked in this garage. When I reported it, the parking attendant said there were no cameras and nothing they could do.

When we tried to leave, no attendants were on duty and none of the payment machines were working. We were trapped inside for about 15 minutes until someone finally showed up.

When the attendant arrived, he tried to get us to pay cash for a lower price, which felt extremely sketchy and unprofessional.

Between the theft, lack of security, no working machines, and shady cash request, this place is not safe or trustworthy.

Save your money and your belongings — use a valet or private garage instead. This garage is NOT worth the risk.","A lot of parking was available here! I was pretty anxious about parking here because I saw the reviews but I decided to give it a shot. My car wasn't broken into (but I also made sure to leave no valuables in my car) and it was really good. They don't have in & out privileges but it's nice since I didn't have to use my car a lot since my hotel was nearby and I walked a lot to the pier & waterfront! I paid $45 per day and it was cheaper than the hotel parking. I didn't see any homeless people wandering around here. If you are planning to park here, make sure to leave no valuables in the car!","Although the parking was close to where I needed to be. My car was broken into and the attendance were very lax the days ago didn’t seem to care and wasn’t listening. A lot of the stairwells are absolutely filthy and smell like urine. It is not a very clean place to park at all","Do NOT park here!!!!!

Truck got broken in to. Glass window smashed out and seeing all the other reviews makes me frustrated that management has no care. Management told us and the other vehicles that were broken into at same time (there were like 3 other cars and trucks that got hit as well)
"we don't have to watch your car, you parked here". Crack heads everywhere, no security like stated on website. 1/10 🌟"]

name: Greenwood Branch - The Seattle Public Library
address: 8016 Greenwood Ave N, Seattle, WA 98103, USA
reviews: ["The library had plenty of table and seats to study, it also had a rare table outside so you can study outdoors. The building is one story, and everything is visible. Thank you for building a library in this busy part of the city, now there is a place I can go to study and do research!","Good library overall, well organized and cleanliness maintained inside. Staff is friendly and available to help searching for any material to read there/borrow. There are few different seating sections and all are laid out spacious.\n\nCouple negatives: Bathrooms are dirty and the collection and number of books is far less than some of the branches. Even a hold was showing months out since there are limited copies.","The staff are good and friendly. However, the quiet room is supposed to be a phone-free and silent space, but some people bring food and eat there with the door closed, which causes a strong smell. Since it’s meant to be a quiet room, it shouldn’t be used as a lunch room. I’ve been there a couple of times, and the same thing happened each time, so I just ended up leaving. The staff didn’t do anything about it.","We love our local Greenwood Library! The staff is always friendly and helpful, making every visit a pleasure. The selection of books—especially for kids—is fantastic, with something new to discover each time. The facilities are top-notch, with a modern, bright space that feels welcoming and inspiring.","Too many books! As usual I walked away with too many books! 🙃  I love the staff picks section and the librarians are friendly and helpful.  10/10 would recommend.  I ordered a few books from another branch too- they arrived in 2 days!  Clean and welcoming environment, they even have an outdoor reading porch with chairs!"]

name: Columbia Branch - The Seattle Public Library
address: 4721 Rainier Ave S, Seattle, WA 98118, USA
reviews:
["Support your local library! I recently had the inspiration to engage again within the Seattle Library system and wow!! I already feel so immersed in all the literature I've considered reading but never purchased it myself.

The Columbia City branch is in a beautiful old building that has a good selection of practically every topic you can think of.  What's great about the Seattle library system is, if you're willing to wait with a hold, you have access to every library's stock within their system which is a ton of content.

And the content comes in your preferred format! I lean towards physical and ebooks but there's audiobooks, video, etc.

They partner with other apps such as Libby so it's easy to find content where you are at. Often holds take time but there's so much out there, it's virtually guaranteed you can find something similar instantly while you wait for the other content.

I've had nothing but good experiences at this local branch. The staff have always been ready to help and are pleasant people. Checking out content is extremely easy and often can be done without needing to wait in line.

All you need is a library card (physical or virtually on your phone) and a desire to read!

Recommended. If only all my tax dollars were this awesomely spent. 👏","Teach your staff how to properly greet people. It was my first time visiting and I didn’t get a single hello or smile. By the time they decided to stop their conversation to acknowledge me it was “you have a question?” with a look of constipation. Next time I’m dropping names. I am a secret shopper and if I were on assignment today your little book box would have gotten an F from the beginning.","This local library has an amazing and helpful staff.  The building is beautiful yet the only (and big) negative is parking. The lot is small and fills up quickly and there is no street parking.","Very cute and quiet public library. Staff are kind. It's a great study space","I love this library so much, especially this branch.
My favorite things about this library:
- the ambience
- how quiet and peaceful it gets"]

name: Magnolia Branch - The Seattle Public Library
reviews: ["I work at different libraries in the Queen Anne/Ballard area almost everyday. I love the Seattle Public Library system. The people who work at the locations I go to are really helpful. SPL offers great benefits to card holders as well like Linkedin Learnings and Libby (audio books) and the selection of audio books is excellent. The Magnolia location is small and they only have one meeting room, so it can be hard to book, just an FYI. I still come here most days though.","Nice library with the cutest reading nooks. Love all the greenery around. Good branch","Clean. Friendly staff. Easy to get to.\nFree parking. Awesome place to get something to read.","Furniture designed by master woodworker George Nakashima.","A very nice organized and friendly little neighborhood Seattle Public Library branch. Small parking lot but lots of street parking nearby, excited to visit this branch more now that I’ll be in the area. As always, lots of fantastic resources and services!"]

name: Northeast Branch - The Seattle Public Library
reviews: ["Went to get a physical card with my spouse who needed to reopen an old account: staff were incredibly friendly and helpful, gave us great recommendations on sections to check out (new releases, the tucked away \"quiet area,\" etc.). The library was clean, quiet, and had a great selection - not the biggest branch but definitely a gem :)","The NE Branch has a very safe and efficient system of picking up your reserved items. Masks required and distanced waiting lines.","The best location for a family new to this area. We depend on its books, audio resources, and the free print quota a lot! Staffs are so nice to any new comers. A gem in the neighborhood.","I love this branch. Helpful, friendly staff. Accessible, air conditioned, parking available, in a nice neighborhood","I love my new local library! It's a lovely, Modern Architecture little building that feels very cozy and nice inside. The staff is super kind and helpful, helping me get my new card all set up - it had been so long since I'd been to a Seattle Library branch that my old one had expired. The selection isn't the biggest obviously - that's the downtown branch - but it's not the tiniest either, and their Holds section seems to be ever-full of things they order in for folks from elsewhere. Overall a great library that at least in the few weeks I've lived here has felt like a solid space to be."]

name: High Point Branch - The Seattle Public Library
address: 3411 SW Raymond St, Seattle, WA 98126, USA
reviews: ["Another sweet, sweet Seattle public library branch in the south. What you see is what you get here. Kind helpers all around with check-in and -out processes, meeting room and study room use, computer and printing or scanning projects. They even regularly  offer homework help hours in their meeting room. What a delightful place to visit!","Today was the most amazing experience I've had here.  When I stopped by over my lunch break to see whether they had the book I was searching for, everyone was incredibly helpful, friendly, and welcoming.  Fantastic work, team!","Came here to focus, but there was a loud talker. Staff did not intervene. It's the new Loudbrary branch. Will not return.","I had the most wonderful experience here today. I popped in on my lunch break to check if they had a book I’ve been looking for, and everyone was so kind, lovely, welcoming, and helpful. Great job team!","so many kids just screaming. the staff doesn't care at all. very had to focus or do work in"]

name: Freeway Park
reviews: ["Designed by Lawrence Halprin in 1976; this park intends to weave the city on both sides together, through its undulating landscape and topography, and offers a place of serenity within the city. Yet, it is a huge pity that the park is now mostly occupied by homeless, making it difficult to navigate.","The name says it all; there was no entrance fee. I was planning on doing a walk through to just check it out. It ended up being so tranquil that decided to practice my embroidery, as seen in the photo.","Love this park. Dispite the looks it’s safe. Just be kind say hi to people and they’re gonna say hello back. Everyone has a story and some of the individuals here have amazing stories and are great people but do remember to be aware of what is happening around you.\nIt’s a good spot to relax and enjoy the sun. Has many diffeeent events and things for everyone to participate in","This little hidden gem is the oxymoron of the year!!! This labyrinth of a park has been in Seattle since I was a kid, in the summer it's a place featuring everything from barter type Booth's to outdoor evening movies on the lawn. Music venues arts and crafts or maybe someone is teaching art or how to juggle it can be a cornucopia of festivities or attractions but, just on its own it's a great place to get away right in the middle of downtown!!! Of course with any urban oasis there is the element of nomads so be mindful and attentive, we all have to share our beautiful city. I've never personally had any issues but when it comes to family it's just wise to be aware of surroundings. I believe that it's on City Park hours so it's closed after 10:00pm so staying in the open hours 6:00am to 10:00pm should be fine. It's a awesome get away check it out.","This is an interesting park. There are wonderful paths which takes you from Hubbell Pl to 6 Ave. It crosses thru I-5 safely. I'm warmer weather there is water features. On the 6 Ave side there are spaces where people do climbing. Be aware however that some places are for cruising and there are the bush bunnies that wide in the.... Bushes😉."]

name: Wallingford Branch - The Seattle Public Library
reviews: ["This library is small and tiny, but I got used to regularly order books from the Central library and they transfer them very quickly within a couple of days to the Wallingford branch. Also,  the staff at this library is always helpful and polite.","Very SMALL (apartment sized] library, children's books, digital offerings.  And VERY helpful librarians.","The employees there were very knowledgeable, helpful, and friendly. They helped me get setup with a card very quickly and I was able to print some pages I needed. Cute, little branch!","Pretty loud like most modern libraries however they have always been cool to me and nice, all staff including food bank people.","Small library, friendly staff. No rest room on some days and hours so come here empty."]

name: Sky View Observatory - Columbia Center
reviews:
["If you visit Seattle and the weather is good, you should definitely check out the Sky View Observatory. The views are amazing. They have lots of space, and they have bar that serves food and drinks. Better price than space needles, and parking nearby on the streets is cheap! The staff are very friendly and helpful.","Somebody recommended this as an alternative to space needle (which is more expensive and can be seen from here). Parking is a nightmare, use public transportation if you can. You can buy tickets in advance or on site, the price is the same. There is a bit of a wait for the elevator, both on the way up and on the way down. The views are 360°, you can see hundreds of miles each direction and it’s just so absolutely gorgeous. I came 20 minutes before sunset and stayed for 20 minutes after, highly recommend this timing. They have a bar with some snacks and drinks, the prices seemed reasonable.","The observatory was great: gorgeous 360° views on a surprisingly clear and sunny day. We went around 11am, had a couple glasses of bubbly at the bar at the top, and learned several new things about the mountains and history of the building etc. from the infographic murals at the top. Pretty interesting, and obviously beautiful views of the city. I definitely recommend this experience over the Space Needle, which is a full 300 feet shorter than this one, yet more expensive!","This was a good experience. It's much more affordable than the space needle. You can walk around the whole floor and this is specifically the 73rd floor. There are windows on all sides. Power outlets are available for charging. There's a restaurant and bar there for snacks. You are allowed to bring your reusable water bottle which is really something I value personally. Once you get up there you are allowed to stay until closing. The usher that handles the elevator crowds was really nice and happy to answer questions and offer up information.

The view was great! You can see Mt Rainier, and a lot of the city. If you are wanting to bring an SLR or tripods, I recommend you do so. There was excellent lighting for profile pictures around midday but as the sun got lower it got a bit trickier for those using phone cameras because you'd be backlit if using the city as a backdrop. Can still use angles to your advantage.

The bathrooms are gendered. There is no gender neutral restroom in the Sky Observatory. The women's stalls had two trash compartments for "hygiene products" instead of the typical one which was nice because one of them was getting pretty full. The latches on the stall doors would pop out if the stall was jostled so just something to be aware of.","I've lived in the area for nearly ten years and never went up. For $40 the elite tickets get you a nice drink, a window seat, and a treat. The chocolate lava cake was amazing.

Super romantic, best time to go ia just before twilight. You get to watch the lights come on and the sun set over the sound"]

name: PlayDate SEA
reviews:
["Great concept. Lots of space for kids to run around and play. Nice toddler area. But it’s way overdue for a cleaning and an update. The dust on the wall and ceiling ripped with insulation showing was a bit unsettling. The kids had a great time nonetheless.","We had our 6 year olds bday party here a few weeks ago and had the best experience! Didn’t know what to expect but the staff was EXCEPTIONAL.They took care of everything, we hardly had to lift a finger. They set us up in our room, rallied all the kids when the food was ready and then again when we were ready for the cake. So accommodating and friendly. We had the best time! Would highly recommend this spot for your kiddos bday party!","A little warm inside, but that could've been since it was a little crowded when we went. I loved how big the play area was and that there was a big area for the parents to sit and eat and drink while the kids play. There's also a separate area for kids 3 and under and there's staff members walking around making sure older kids, or kids with a lot of energy, aren't going in there. Just wish there were more of these in the area.","We took advantage of the food donation entry deal for the last Wednesday of the month. Had so much watching our kids explore and play with other kids. It was a great way to get some wiggles out!","Video attached  :)

Friendly staff, great pizza, chicken nuggest seems bit pricey but overall we had fun . It's really convenient to have food & drink while kids play.

There is a separated area for 0-3 yrs. It's dark and small and thankfully wheb we were visiting, we were the only ones there but I can see it can get really crowded in there easily.

We visited as soon as they open so not many kids were there and felt spacious and nice.

Restrooms are clean and they also have a family multi restroom for changing your kids diaper or more.

They seemed to have private rooms for party as well."]

name: Twinkle Land Play Cafe
address: 15510 Lake Hills Blvd, Bellevue, WA 98007, USA
reviews:
["We absolutely love Twinkle Cafe! It’s one of our favorite places to bring the kids to play. The space is clean, fun, and perfect for little ones to explore while parents can relax.

We also hosted a birthday party here and it was such a great experience. Everything was well organized, the staff was kind and helpful, and the kids had the best time.","We had an absolute blast here. They have a foam pit. They have a trampoline. They have a ball pit. They have an interactive screen that you can throw the ball pit at they have slides. They have climbing things, they have a café. They have a cotton candy machine this place is loaded with cool stuff. They have games they have a do it yourself section.

They are opening a new location in Lynnwood soon.

Highly recommend.","This is a place to visit if you have kids. Lots of various activities to keep them occupied and busy. It was our first time here and we had no regrets. Every dollar spent was soo worth it!","Twinkle Land is like a mini arcade and play place put together. It's in the back corner of a shopping center, but there's plenty of parking available here. It's pretty big inside and is great for birthday parties or even just a night out with the kids. They have a bunch of ball pits, slides, tunnels and more to tire the kids out. The staff are nice, helpful with getting things together. The place is pretty clean and bathrooms are very accessible. The price to get in is good compared to the long times you get for paly time. The games are a bit pricey, but some come with prizes. Overall a great place for kids above the age of 2.","Exceeded my expectations of a play place! I wish Washington had more of these places like in California, Arizona and Hawaii!
We paid $35.26 after tax for ONLY the play area. The arcade and sensory/craft area are separate/additional charges. A lot of people comment it’s an over-priced place, but I think it’s well worth the amenities if you make the day of it, which we usually do!

Pros:
-Sooooo many activities for kids to do whether it’s constructive play time, crafts, sensory, arcade games
-Unlimited play time WITH in & out privileges
-Conveniently located in a plaza near restaurants, or just a 5 minute drive to another plaza with more restaurants
-Free parking
-clean bathroom
-Really cute and decently sized dining space for parties! (It’s not a private party room)
-Friendly & helpful staff
-Great atmosphere, visually aesthetic & stimulating

Cons:
-People don’t close the child gate properly and leave it open, allowing opportunities for kids to run out. R
Staff regulation of this would be ideal or maybe a big attention sign.
-Crowded on weekends, which isn’t a con, but there’s limited seating and people will put their belongings on chairs and rarely sit in it bcs they’re wandering around. I saw trashed/unoccupied seats for at least 45 minutes. It’d be super beneficial to have more cubby space for diaper bags so the seats could actually be sat in
-People leave trash all over the counters and leave the seats sticky"]

name: Nick's Magnificent
address: 1430 NW Mall St, Issaquah, WA 98027, USA
reviews:
["Truly magnificent! What an amazing world Nick made here. The design, flow and execution of this place is truly top notch. I even sent my husband a text when I first got there (pictured) and then my grandson and I stayed for over 5 hours. It was the best day he ever had he said. No screens, just pure play and enjoyment. We had amazing food, so many enjoyable areas to sit and cool down, or for parents to socialize, work etc. every staff member we engaged with were awesome and kind. The place is priced so affordable, it blew me away to have a clean, comfortable parent/child establishment at a totally reasonable price. The owner was there dressed to the nines in theme, and is so passionate about the sanctity of this world he dreamed to life. Try it out, become a member, and make magnificent memories. When you are 100 years old on the front porch in your rocking chair, it’s memories made in places like Nicks you will want to reflect on.","Perfect place for working parents especially if you really have to work over the weekend.
You will easily spot a parent with a laptop getting their work assignments completed while their kids are busy playing.
The mini excavator, crane are one of a kind that I haven't seen in any other playgrounds.
There are 2 multilevel obstacles courses for kids at each end of the playground, one has slides and the other has ball shooting guns.
In between these two play areas you have cafe, mini excavator, cranes , mock theatre, jail and fire engine. They have plenty of costumes and you can see kids wearing them are running around.
There is also a play area for younger toddlers.
I loved how this playground has been architectured to have plenty of seating arrangements for parents at each level.
Cafe has good options of food, wine, beer, coffee and other drinks.
I wish there were more restrooms and diaper changing spots!

Parking is little tricky as there are fewer spots in the playground and also the street parking. Not sure about parking in adjacent establishment parking spot.

Cleanliness is amazing, I hope they carry it around as more folks discover this place. There is a timed entry when you book the tickets, not sure when they will start forcing it as the crowd increases! The ticket cost is only for the kid and adults are free!
.","Great place if you have the money. Plan on spending at least $50 if you are going to eat and drink. No outside food or drink allowed in here; I didn't notice any water fountain either so expect to pay for water. Aside from the expenses on concessions,  the price for admission is only for each child and I think it was well worth it. Very comfy and plentiful seating for parents at just about any location your child is playing  at. Large size place with much for children to do. Also, your kid needs to wear socks with grip on the bottom, they provide a free pair on your first visit","Nick’s Magnificent Indoor Playground in Issaquah is absolutely amazing! There are so many different activities to keep kids engaged, and you can really tell how much thought has gone into the upgrades and layout. Every time we visit, there’s something new or improved, which makes it feel fresh and exciting.

As a parent, I especially love how comfortable it is for adults too. The lounging areas are well set up, the coffee is great, and the option to enjoy food and even alcohol makes it such a relaxing, enjoyable experience instead of just “surviving” a kids’ play space. It truly feels like a place designed for the whole family.

My son has an absolute blast every time we go, and I love knowing he’s having fun while I can actually unwind a bit. This place is a total gem—clean, fun, thoughtfully designed, and honestly one of the best indoor playgrounds around. We’ll definitely keep coming back!","This place is exactly what it says on the website, I visited during a bus period (sat pre-lunch) and even then there was enough space for it to be fun and engaging. Heck some of the attractions I wanted to play on. The genius of this place is there are places for parents to stop and tag- team so they can rest rather than be on their feet and eyes on stalls watching the kids.
The attractions are clean and work, the capacity it can handle is excellent the staff are brilliant and accommodating.
Only downside really is with a play centre this big, they focused on play at the expense of parking (but I’m not going to complain when the place is this good)"]

name: Kids Magic Lab
address: 7325 166th Ave NE f235, Redmond, WA 98052, USA
reviews:
["We chose Kids Magic Lab for our daughter’s second birthday and it was exactly what we hoped for. The space is clean, bright, and well-organized, which makes a big difference when hosting toddlers and families.

They made a custom unicorn balloon arch that was beautiful and reasonably priced, and our daughter loved it. The staff was kind, organized, and helpful, which made the entire party feel easy and low-stress.

The kids stayed engaged the whole time, and several guests commented on how nice the space was. We loved our experience and would absolutely recommend Kids Magic Lab for birthday parties for young kids.","Great place to stay warm and have fun with your toddlers. My son loved it here, he never got bored like other places we've visited. This place is really fun, it's also in a great location.","Was in the area and went in to check it out. Wow would I have liked a place like this when I was a kid! The layout is well designed and it’s obvious that a lot of thought went into this place; imagination areas with themes, climbing areas, place to throw soft objects, etc Spoke with the staff and they were all friendly, knowledgeable and I witnessed active listening when the children were speaking to them. Smiles on the support staff faces was a welcome sight! Kuddos to the owner!","My daughters aged 6yrs old and 3yrs old love to play at Kids Magic Lab. All the toys and amenities are new. Super clean restrooms that are perfect for changing diapers for babies. They even have toddler classes and art classes available so when you transition your kid to Preschool you will definitely benefit from it. Highly recommend this place.","My child attended a birthday party here. We loved it. The biggest perk is that unlike most other indoor playgrounds, when you book this place for a birthday you so not have to share the space with other parties. I plan to have my child's next birthday here."]

name: Ballard Playspace
reviews: ["We had our son’s first birthday here today and it was a great space! Our coordinator Alexa was nice and helpful. It’s a big space that had an inflatable slide, yard games, TVs, and trikes for kids to ride around. We brought our own bounce house to add more fun and had some table activities for the kids. I think, if you’re creative enough, you can add more activities for kids to do if you’re having a children’s party here (for example, we did an arts and crafts table). The one thing I wasn’t a huge fan of was the lack of outlets for our food warmers, but this is easily remedied with an extension cord. We had a great time and enjoyed the space, it had great parking, and we were able to be there for 4 hours! Definitely would plan another party here :)","This is a super fun play area to bring little kids. We came here for a birthday party so it was nice and busy but not too overcrowded. There's a giant tube play structure that kids can run through and climb up. The slides are fun for the kids. They have a mini indoor field for soccer and/or basketball. The toddler area mostly has soft blocks for stacking and climbing and rocking chairs. The scoot cars are a hit with a path for kids to follow around the large play structure.","Great place for kids birthday party but the toy options are less. It has some standing tables for adults and few toddler tables and chairs for them to sit. Bathroom is clean. Entrance could be little confusing if you park on the other side.","Nice big space for children's party. Play area with bouncy castle with an area for eating and a separate kitchen. Parking area adjacent. Clean and tidy and friendly helpful coordinator.","It was such a great playroom. My kids loved this Playroom.  It was safe for the kids and educational as well. The place was spaciously clean and safe. The staves were so friendly and attentive upon watching over the kids.   I will surely bring my kids here again."]

name: Funtastic Playtorium
address: 4077 Factoria Square Mall SE, Bellevue, WA 98006, USA
reviews:
["We had a wonderful birthday party here. Everything went smoothly, the staff were so kind and helpful, and the kids had an amazing time. Thank you for making it special!","Attended many birthday parties here. Easy for parents to manage. Play area is safe and fun for kids. The facility is clean. One request is that 1 hour is too short to decorate the party hall and cleanup after party. Maybe 1.5 hours would be better.","...great place to bring kids to discover music, counting and climbing all in a secure environment. Callie was Awesome and made sure everyone followed the rules and left with the right child...Her customer service and attention to detail was refreshing..","I just saw a previous review saying it is for kids 5 and older. That is not true, I have been taking my son Keanu here since he was 2. They have a toddler section but not only that, they can learn to climb and develop motorskills and physical coordination at that age by allowing them to struggle a little bit. But here is the caveat, this is only doable when it is not busy. If it is too busy, you should crawl with you toddler and accompany them closely so they don’t get trampled. And if there are a lot of kids on the trampoline, don’t bother with a child under three, the other kids will bounce them around and they won’t be able to stand up.

There are bathrooms, a party room for birthdays in the back. Ball cannons, two slides one open one enclosed. There is no ball pit. The toddler section is only for 3 and under and has some developmental toys.

Warning: there are spinning carousel tea cup things that the kids can sit in and spin around. They can be dangerous if they are spinning fast and your child tries to climb out while it’s still moving. If children are using them, there should be an adult supervising them for that particular part of the play place.

Here are some videos and pictures from multiple visits. Some are from 8 months ago when my son was just over 2 years old. Some are from 2 months ago.","I had a great first experience here with my 2 daughters. Staff was friendly and helpful with my wagon stroller. Their toddler play section was nice, and my kids enjoyed it. The big playground is meant for your child to wander the maze. My 2 year old was able to run the route 4 plus times with no issues. I find this place safe and a good experience for your children. I will for sure be checking out the other locations in the area. Thank you, and God bless Funtastic Playtorium Factoria!"]

name: Safari
reviews: ["Super fun for my 2.5 and 4.5 year old. Every thing is cushioned, low to the ground, large space and obstacles to run around with. Some foam blocks to play with.  A small ball pit. Small corner with toys for small babies. They Sell some snacks, water, socks. Currently about $19 per kid with one adult for 2 hours. They honor military, have Military discount. I would come again on a rainy day!","My toddler had so much fun. Staff is kind and the play ground is kept clean. They have snacks and provide socks if needed.","My 6 year old had a great time here. Lots and areas to play in and challenge their physical abilities. Great price for 2 hours of playtime","Great playground for my 6 year old to burn off energy! This is a place for parent supervision, but had amazing spaces to get the wiggles out! Has a dedicated area for children under 2 as well for some lower intensity play time.","Wonderful for our 17month old daughter. She enjoyed playing with older kids who were also having a good time"]

name: Nixi City Play + Cafe
address: 1437 NW Richmond Beach Rd # E, Shoreline, WA 98177, USA
reviews:
["Easy to find parking, play cafe was very clean and coffee was good. However no food is available, no bagels or pastries. Barista was friendly.

I thought it was a bit overpriced and parents have to pay an additional fee if we want to sit in the play area. In total, it cost about $90 for us to take our kids there for an hour. It was $15 per kid, $5 per adult, our coffees, our kids drinks plus tip.

The rock climbing steps to get up were also quite difficult (and painful). My children got bored after about 30 minutes. There needs to be toys in the kitchen area and additional toys in the play area to keep children more engaged.","We recently celebrated our son’s first birthday at Nixi City Play, and it was the perfect experience! We booked a private after-hours rental, and from start to finish, everything exceeded our expectations.

The owners, Dominique and Giuseppe, were incredibly gracious and flexible, allowing us to bring in our own decorations and food to make the celebration feel personal and special. The space itself is spotless, thoughtfully designed in a Montessori style, and truly caters to both little ones and big kids. Babies, siblings, friends, and family all had an absolute blast.

Parents especially loved the upstairs bar seating, which gave them the perfect view of the kids while they played. It felt safe, welcoming, and fun for everyone.

We are so grateful to Nixi City Play for making our son’s first birthday so memorable. We couldn’t have asked for a better space or a better team to work with. Highly recommend!","I was very fortunate to have the pleasure of discovering this unique play cafe. When you first walk in you are greeted so kindly, the staff and owners are very clear about how pricing works and answered any questions that I had. Not to mention the coffee is delicious! I was able to bring my son here so he may play and gave me time to focus on a bit of work with my computer (did I mention free WiFi). Overall a wonderful place I would definitely recommend to anyone, even if you are just popping in for coffee it is great.","My two year old daughter loved this place. Lots of toys to help promote imaginative play, climbing and education! Feels cozy, and clean. Great food and drink options for both parent and kiddo. Will definitely be back with my daughter.","We just celebrated our son's 2nd birthday here and it was so great! We all had a great time and the kids enjoyed themselves. It was a great size for our small group and looked super cute with all our decor. Artem was fantastic and everyone loved him. Thank you Nixi! ♥️"]

name: Funtastic Playtorium Alderwood
reviews: ["Wow! So much fun! The kids had a blast (5 & 2)! The facility was nice and clean, was inside the play structure too and that was clean! The woman at the front desk was lovely. We had a great experience. You have all day to go in and out, we were able to hit the mall for a break grab lunch and head back in. Socks are required!! So don’t forget to bring those.","We booked 2 back-to-back time slots for my son's birthday party. In addition to getting extended time in the party room, they were able to accommodate more kids in our party, included in the price. Akash (the owner) was very responsive, accommodating, and kind in the whole planning process. Our host, Sarah, was also very professional and helpful. Also loved the fact that we could bring our own food. We ended up having a large party of 70+ people. What could have been a stressful day, ended up not because of the staff at Funtastic Playtorium in Alderwood. Highly recommend this venue for large groups for your kiddo's next birthday party!","This place was fantastic - just as its name implies! It was clean, fun and safe. Our 3 1/2 year old grandson loved it. He could go down both of the slides independently, and he also loved the mini trampoline. He had a good time in the ball area where you put balls into an opening and they dropped out slowly from the top. So worth the money!","My daughter had a blast.  It was a great place for her to play safely on a day that was too cold for the park.  I'm 6'0\" so following her inside to play wasn't really an option, but when she had a breakdown I was able to crouch down and crawl in to pick her up and brush her off.  The place has plenty of dings and isn't 100% spotless but is remarkably clean for how busy it is with children.  Staff vacuumed and wiped down surfaces several times while we were there.","We had our kid’s 3rd birthday party in the party room. The staff was super friendly and accomodating. We had a party host who attended to us throughout the event and helped with setting up the decor. Overall wonderful experience and hopefully we will arrange more parties here."]

name: Kids Cove @ The Square
address: 212 Bellevue Way NE, Bellevue, WA 98004, USA
reviews:
["No enforcement of height restrictions or any rules makes this place unsafe for most of the children it is designed to cater to (sign says under 6).

If you somehow manage to go when it is not completely packed and you have parents who are mindful of their kids, it is a really fun place.

However, that is a rare occasion and I cannot recommend this place for kids under 5. You have kids beyond the height restrictions running at full speed, throwing toys, jumping, and using the structures as a means to shove and push each other.

I have seen too many kids get hurt while their parents are either nowhere to be seen or glued on their phones. It really is a shame because there are so few affordable spaces indoors for toddlers in King County and the structures and seating themselves are very nice.","Safety Hazard: Unenforced Height Limits Lead to Injuries for Younger Kids

This facility is a major safety concern for young children. While height limit rules are posted, they are completely ignored by staff and customers.
Older children well over the limit are allowed to run freely with toddlers. During our visit, I saw kids were knocked down, run over by a much larger child who should not have been there. I witnessed multiple similar incidents.
Management must actively enforce their own rules to protect younger, vulnerable patrons. Until they do, this is a beautiful space that is unfortunately a safety hazard.
I cannot recommend this play area for families with children under 5.","Take the main elevator to the 3rd floor and you'll come across this awesome play center for little kids. Also it free with a latte baresta nearby with some goodies. It's a tad small however it seems to be designed for kids under the age of 5 years.","This is a great area, but it’s dated and falling apart!  Playground items are faded to the point of being unusable, ripped, and peeling.  For a kids area, the bathrooms are standard adult bathrooms.  No smaller, lower toilets and no stools at the sinks.  IKEA has better bathrooms for children.
They really need to modernize some of the smaller structures and give the space more kid friendly facilities.
Lower sinks outside the bathroom would be a nice added touch as well to clean dirty hands after playing.
Great place on a rainy day.  I appreciate parents watching their kids and making sure all the kids are playing nice with one another.","Busy little play area on the third floor of Bellevue Square. It’s right above the Starbucks on the second floor. It’s nice to grab a snack and coffee before heading up. We got here early so no complaints on it being too busy but it was filling up quickly. The no shoes policy is great. The kids are all running around with socks or no socks on. The floors are padded. The structures have a soft and easy to grip exterior. Some kids get rowdy but overall it was a good time. No food or drinks within the circle but there are plenty of tables on the exterior to sit while watching the kids play."]

name: WiggleWorks Kids
address: 15600 NE 8th St Suite G2, Bellevue, WA 98008, USA
reviews:
["They’ve just updated it and it seems to be trinkle land light. Colorful and appropriate now for even a bit older kids. Both my 1 and 5 year old had a blast here at a birthday party","This is probably the best, most affordable indoor playground on the east side. This place got all new equipment January 2026 and it’s been fantastic. Staff are super friendly and create a welcoming environment. The monthly pass is definitely worth it, especially in the winter months. There is a lot of different kinds of equipment great for developing physical skills like balance. Thankfully it’s definitely geared towards positive early childhood development—no enticing touchscreens for the kids! Massive ball pit, giant foam legos, slides, balance beams, and so much more. Love this place.","New renovation is really good.. Pricing is well adjusted.. Not very expensive.. Team Member Lou is always very kind and friendly , keeps the place clean and maintained as well.. Kids love to come back.. Only removing 1 star for managment not repairing stuff fast.. The ball machine hasnt been working since few days already.. Pls do the needful..","The remodel looks great! They have a ball pit, a trampoline, several slides, and a fun little obstacle course. It's a little more exciting for older toddlers and kindergartners now. Big bathrooms to fit multiple kids.

Things to note:

Bring grippy socks. The new mats are a little slippery. Old Navy across the hall sells grippy socks if you forget, like I did.

Before the remodel, there were two counters for parents to sit at to get some work done with some peace of mind that their kids weren't going to run out without them seeing. The counter near the exit is gone now though, so just keep that in mind.

Max height on the play structures is 48".","The photos speak for themselves. I’m surprised this place passes inspection. My 3.5yr old was telling ME he was done after 30min, and that’s very telling. Never again."]

name: Ahoy Kitsap Playland
reviews: ["Its an super cute space! Theres a play area for 5 and under with slides and toys and a kitchen play set etc. And also the large play structure for 5+(littles can do it but some of it might be challenging without parents help) The big area has super fun slides on the lower level and a giant spiral slide on the top level,theres climbing spaces,nets,tubes and theres a \"spider web\" tower thats 5 levels amd has a trampoline on the bottom so if you get down quickly or slip through its a soft landing 🤣 They have snacks and allt of ice cream and milkshake options! Theres rooms for birthday parties too!","This place is cool. Loads of fun for my 3 year old. Don’t love how cold it is or that I can’t bring in my own healthy snack options for my extremely picky toddler. Love that I can leave and come back though. Over all it’s clean, safe, and the workers were kind.","My family and I had a blast with our 13 month old. She absolutely loved it. There is so much for her to do. We love how there’s food and drinks. Makes it really fun for the whole family. We love the little things in life. Watching our daughter crawling and walking around all over the place was the best thing to see.","We have been taking our children there since my first child was 2. Between birthday parties and fun time when the weather is poor, this has been a great experience for everyone. In the winter time we sign up for the membership because it's a great local play to take the kids when the park isn't nice. It's also great that they have wifi, so while one parent watches the kid the other can get some work done.","Today was our first visit for a preschool field trip. While the facility is beautiful, clean, and well-equipped with a great variety of play areas and a nice cafe, our experience was marred by a serious safety issue.\n​About an hour in, an unsupervised child began pushing other children. He pushed my daughter down three times—once nearly into a table and another time causing her to fall onto a large toy. I witnessed him push approximately eight other children as well.\n​Despite multiple parents notifying the front desk, no staff members came to monitor the area or attempt to locate the child’s parent. We spent the rest of our visit on edge, constantly intervening to keep our children safe. While I understand I signed a waiver and that it is my job to watch my child, I expect the staff to maintain a basic level of safety and supervision when a known issue is reported. It’s disappointing that such a nice facility is overshadowed by a lack of management. We likely won’t be back."]

name: Wunderkind
reviews:
["This place was was perfect for passing our crappy-weather morning! Plenty of coffee options for the morning, as well as beer and wine for the afternoon. They have some food options as well, including wraps, sandwiches, muffins, and plenty of snacks. None of the food is house made from what I can tell, but we weren't here for the food, we were here to play. The duplo room is perfect for littles, but the upstairs room also has a cordoned off duplo section so if you also have older kids, they can all be in the same room. The other awesome thing is that outside food and drink is allowed, unlike some other play cafes in the area.","We've held two birthday parties here - Wunderkind is the best! They entertain the kids, and setup and teardown is pretty easy.","We had my son's second birthday here. Highly recommend for parties, such an easy space. all of our guests enjoyed themselves.","A real neighborhood treasure! Great place to take the kids for a fun afternoon, particularly on a rainy day. They have a nice selection of food and drinks (for kids and adults alike!). And the kids will be entertained for hours on the two large Lego rooms (the downstairs one for younger kids, there upstairs one for older kids).

They owner, Nick, is a very nice guy and runs a variety of after-school and day-camp programs. They also have good WiFi here, so you can even get work done while they kids play.","They have two stories for Legos. They also serve coffee, beer, wine, and light deli food. They do charge for playing, but it is very minimal.

You can reserve birthday parties at the location for a reasonable price. You can reserve the smaller room on the main floor or the room upstairs. You can also reserve the entire place before or after they close on the weekends.

Staff is friendly, not a lot of parking available but there is street parking as well."]

name: Play at the center
reviews: ["Great place for kids if you visite the space needle","Pretty cool playground for kids. I see an awful lot of teens and adults playing around on there as if they were helping an imaginary child.","It’s not what it use to be feeling when I’m there it feels like I’m not a kid anymore and I’m not and so the nostalgics it all wore off and now I’m just a fat balding middle aged man that rides around on an electric skateboard cause I’m to lazy to ride a regular board, or am I just smarter and wealthy then I was at 13 when I started but anyways the place is alyeight!!","Fantastic place for the kids to burn off excess energy.  Seems well maintained.  Some staff were there assessing one of the swings for wear and discussing repairs.","the ropes to climb up to the slides is actually pretty fun to climb on though it does get pretty high so make sure your child is old enough or strong enough to prevent injury there is also a merry go round to spin around on and when I went there the merry go round wasn't broken for once"]

name: The Ridge Activity Center
reviews:
["Absolutely love The Ridge Activity Center!

We had such a great time at The Ridge Activity Center in Bothell! It’s the perfect spot for families with kids of all ages. Our almost 2-year-old toddler had a blast in the soft play area designed just for little ones—it’s safe, clean, and full of fun things to explore. Meanwhile, our 5 1/2-year-old was completely entertained with the larger play structures, slides, and climbing areas.

The space is well thought out, with plenty of seating for parents and a great mix of active play and imaginative areas. Everything felt clean, well-maintained, and super family-friendly. We’ve been to a lot of play centers, and this one is definitely a favorite. It’s a place we can go back to again and again—both kids ask when we’re going back as soon as we leave!

Highly recommend for a fun, safe, and energy-burning day indoors!","First time here! This is a great family center, and our kiddos really enjoyed their first time at the playzone. What made it extra nice was the staff, everyone was friendly and welcoming. Shout out to Jaelyn! She went above and beyond helping me with my little guy when he refused to take cold milk, even taking the time to warm it up for him. She is such a sweet, hardworking girl, and her kindness really meant a lot to me. We truly appreciate the wonderful experience!","We came to Ridge Activity Center for our friend’s child’s birthday, and it was such a great experience. The space is very engaging for kids, with lots of opportunities for movement and play. The birthday party covered the cost for us, but I looked it up afterward and found the pricing to be very reasonable for the level of enrichment offered.

We arrived toward the end of the day, so the play area was understandably a bit messier, but overall the pads and activity zones appeared well maintained and clean. As parents, we always wash hands before and after play regardless, and we felt comfortable letting our child explore.

Overall, it was a positive experience, and we’ll definitely be returning soon.","A fun and clean place where kids easily spend a couple of hours playing, they have a sand box and huge activity gym. The place has food too (pizza mainly ) didn’t have the pizzas personally but it did smell good!","I like this place! My son and I go here every now and then and compared to other play places and park playgrounds, The Ridge has the fastest slides! Even I get a little thrill from them when I follow my 3 year old up to the top.

There is also a toddler area that is closed off for safety. A sand table for kids to play with. And a water table as well.

The bathrooms are clean and convenient. Place has plenty of parking.

The cafe is pretty good like another reviewer mentioned. The price is a little high but not gouging. I don’t mind, I like to support the place.

There is a cool interactive rock climbing wall the kids like and the entire play structure is themed like a tree house. There are plenty of tables for the adults.

I saw a good room for birthday parties. Admission is not too bad. A little higher than places like funtastic playtorium."]

name: Safari Auburn
address: 1101 Outlet Collection Way Suite 1049, Auburn, WA 98001, USA
reviews:
["Safari  in Auburn is such a fun and safe spot for kids to play and burn off energy! As a parent, I really appreciate how clean and well-organized the play areas are. There’s plenty of space for kids of different ages, and the staff do a great job keeping an eye on things and making sure everyone plays safely. My kids absolutely love the slides and ballpit areas — it’s hard to get them to leave! It’s also nice to have comfortable seating for parent. Perfect for birthday parties or weekend playdates. Highly recommend for families.","We were in the area and I needed something for my toddler to get out energy. This was perfect and she loved it! Pretty clean and attendants walked around to monitor and clean.","This place is a great space for get all the wiggles and giggles out. It’s spacious and clean. Plenty of room to run and play. I def recommend if you have little to come right around opening if you can.","We go to Safari Auburn often, and every visit is always a great experience. It’s such a fun and safe space for kids to play, and we love how clean and well-maintained the whole place is. The staff are always friendly, patient, and helpful, which makes us feel comfortable bringing the kids again and again.

The play areas and games are perfect for kids of all ages, and everything feels safe and thoughtfully designed. The kids always have a great time and never get bored.

The prices are very reasonable, especially considering how much fun the kids have and how long they can play.

Overall, Safari Auburn is one of our favorite places to take the kids, and we’ll keep coming back!","We had my granddaughter’s third birthday here, it was beautiful and when was over they could stay ad long  as  they  to play!"]

name: Gas Works Park Kid Playground
address: 2101 N Northlake Way, Seattle, WA 98103, USA
reviews:
["Gas Works Park Playground is unlike any other. Our grandkids had the best time climbing the giant rope structures and exploring the colorful play elements. It’s designed for all ages, with enough challenge for older kids and safer options for the little ones.

The setting is what makes it unforgettable — industrial towers in the background, open green space, and sweeping views of Lake Union and the Seattle skyline. We loved watching boats and floatplanes while the kids played nonstop.

If you’re visiting Seattle with kids or grandkids, don’t miss this spot. It’s playful, imaginative, and one of the most memorable parks we’ve visited.","Our last stop at Gas Works Park was the Norrha Kid Playground. It has several interesting play structures and a seating area to enjoy views of the lake.","Beautiful location thus 5 stars. Wish there was a little more information about the original equipment and educational info. Didn't see any signage or map. Adding something like that would be nice.","I honestly don't get the appeal.  This is a weird park. There is geese poo EVERYWHERE... not a place you'd want to picnic or even walk around.  Yes, there is a pretty view of the Seattle skyline, but there is no place to sit and enjoy.","Playground with a view. No bucket swing set for infants.
Better for older toddlers and preschool age kids."]

name: Elevated Sportz Trampoline Park & Event Center
address: 18311 Bothell Everett Hwy #140, Bothell, WA 98012, USA
reviews: ["Took my 5 year old son here and he had a great time! There’s a lot more to do in addition to the trampolines. He especially loved the big play structure and arcade games. One of the employees (Arthur) was super nice. After we couldn’t get a little toy to come out of one of the quarter machines, he gave us some extra quarters so we could pick some different toys. We came in on a Tuesday morning which was a great time, because it wasn’t crowded, and you get a better price on weekday mornings.","Our first kids’ birthday party — and finding the right venue was definitely not a piece of cake! After reaching out to quite a few places, Elevated Sportz truly stood out for their amazing customer service.\n\nA big thank-you to Arthur and Victoria for being so helpful throughout the planning and on the day of the party. Every team member was incredibly kind and friendly, making the whole experience even more special.","​\"Our recent visit to Elevated Trampoline Park with our grandsons, aged five and one, was marked by both positive and negative experiences. While the children enjoyed the arcade and slides, we were dismayed that our one-year-old was charged the full $18 admission fee, despite being unable to utilize much of the equipment. Furthermore, the constant surveillance by an employee felt intrusive and unnecessary. The park appears to cater primarily to children aged five and above, presenting challenges for single mothers with toddlers who  older children. The restrooms were in a state of disrepair, featuring cracked flooring and a sink secured with tape. The laser tag room emitted an unpleasant odor. Additionally, the employee assigned to monitor our party spent time on the first floor, leaving other children on the trampoline area unsupervised. In conclusion, while the park offered some enjoyment, the overall experience was marred by several significant drawbacks.\"","We were there for a birthday party. They had lots of parking in the back and lots of space in the park. Make sure you wear a mask sock because they wouldn’t let you go bare foot.","It's a comfortable place for kids for there mental and physical relaxation. If you want to arrange a small kids party it can also be arranged here. Very cooperative staff."]

name: The Jungle Gym
reviews:
["I had the best experience in signing my 8 yr old and 5 yr old up for their first ever gymnastics class! The coaches were so helpful and patient with them, and the staff went above and beyond to make us feel welcome! My girls keep asking when their next class will be, they loved it so much! Thank you for everything, Jungle Gym! :)","I recently attended a toddler birthday party at Jungle Gym in Burien Washington with my 4 year. old granddaughter. The facility itself is well organized and clearly designed with little ones in mind, which made this experience especially disappointing.

Most concerning was how my disability was handled. I am a below-the-knee amputee. I am physically fit, mobile, and fully capable of supervising and assisting my grandchild. However, I cannot remove my shoe without removing my prosthetic leg, which would make it impossible for me to walk.
When I explained this to the manager after being told I must remove my shoes, her solution was to offer me a chair so I could sit instead of participating. Initially, she even suggested placing the chair near the entrance door to watch and not enter the room. When I expressed that her solution was upsetting she said she could move a chair to the middle of the room.  No alternative accommodations (such as shoe covers) were offered. She pointed to the wall to show me the rules. The implication was that I should sit and watch while everyone else actively supervised and spotted their children.
This response was insulting and demonstrated a complete lack of understanding of accessibility and inclusion. I was not asking for special treatment — only the ability to participate in the same way as other parents and grandparents were.
I have never before been refused entry or participation because of my disability. Jungle Gym needs a clear and thoughtful accessibility plan and staff training to ensure that people with disabilities are treated with respect and dignity.
I hope management takes this feedback seriously, because no family member should feel excluded or marginalized in a space meant for children and families. My grandaughter was very disappointed that I wasn't able to help her and I also felt concerned not being able to spot or coach her when she needed it.  I live close by the gym, but I can't imagine going back.","I give this place a five-star review because the jungle gym was really really fun closer to the end of the party they open the zip line for kids to go on The employees were not strict they were really nice it looked like they're already cleaning the whole thing and we're just outside I definitely recommend anybody nearby to come to this gymnasium.","We hosted a birthday party here recently, and I cannot say enough good things about our experience. We were looking for a place to host our five year old's birthday that was fun for the kids and low key for the parents: we liked the contained environment for the kids, the combination of free play and coordinated activities and space for the grown ups to chat. The Jungle Gym offered all of that, with the opportunity to add-on experience as wanted. They followed up with us a week out from the party so we were aware of the schedule of activities, what they would provide/what we needed to bring, etc. (they have parties back to back, so bring things that are a quick clean up!) We initially made a mistake scheduling our party, and they were quick to respond and help us reschedule our event at no additional cost. Planning a party at home takes a lot of effort: our experience at the Jungle Gym made the day feel easy!","The “world’s friendliest gym” was not so friendly for me. I continue to feel like an outsider as a woman of color in this city and unfortunately this place was not an exception."]

name: Children Play Area
address: 3rd floor, Bellevue Square, 575 Bellevue Way NE, Bellevue, WA 98004, USA
reviews: ["This lovely play area features ships and marine life with Puget Sound as its inspiration. It is perfect for younger kids to climb and slide. Since it's on the third floor with only the cookie shop and Sharkey's Kid Salon, it's also relatively quiet and low traffic. My 3 y.o. loves it.","Such a beautiful place for kids to play! I love that it is on the third floor near the center of the mall.\nThe play areas are diverse and there are plenty of places for parents to sit.\nIt's clean and maintained well.","Descent kids play area. Equipment is new and clean. Softer then it looks and the three toddlers we brought here (us plus friends) seemed to really enjoy running and climbing all over for over an hour. Only complaint is there is no secure door on the exit so you constantly have to watch to make sure they don’t get to escape.","Nice place but not sure why parents don’t take off their shoes to keep clean area for their children to play in","Closed for renovation till July end"]

name: Sky Zone Trampoline Park
address: 455 Andover Park E, Tukwila, WA 98188, USA
reviews:
["We recently took our daughter to Sky Zone in Tukwila for her birthday party, and it was an absolute blast! The place is packed with wall-to-wall trampolines, foam pits, and other fun attractions that had all the kids (and even some parents) jumping, flipping, and laughing nonstop. My daughter had the biggest smile the whole time—she couldn't stop bouncing and trying out new tricks.
The birthday party setup was super easy and stress-free. The staff was friendly, helpful, and made sure everything ran smoothly, from the private party area to the food and cleanup. No hassle for us parents, which was a huge win! The energy was high but safe, and it felt like a perfect way to celebrate.
If you're looking for a fun, active birthday party venue near Seattle, Sky Zone Tukwila is definitely worth it. We'll be back for sure—highly recommend! 🎉","We were visiting Seattle and my son decided to try this place- paid with his own money. What a circus. We waited a half hour to even check in, and they were so obviously over capacity. Kids and adults on every single surface. Literally on walls. Kids were running every attraction, as there weren’t ANY staff members running them. The women’s bathroom had three stalls- first was covered in vomit, second was covered in urine. Being over capacity and understaffed meant there were accidents all over the place- we watched several kids fight and one be escorted out for it. Another kid had his nose streaming blood and yet another was getting foam picked out of his eye after he got buried under other kids in a foam pit. My own kid got slammed into by a teenager in the little kid area. This place costs way too much to justify running like this.","Patronized Sky Zone for my GDaughter's 10th Birthday.
It is definitely a place for the young and old.
I didn't buy anything to eat or drink, well I bought my Gson a water,  $5....
Kevin was very personable and went the extra mile to help my Family.","Two things that are wrong with this place.  First, my son showed up 3 person birthday party.  He started to feel lighted headed after 10 minutes there.  I gave him $20 to buy food and/or drinks for himself.  To find out this place is a cash  less business.  No where on its websites or phone answering system do they say its cashless. My son had to stop the activity for most of the time there.  This is a kid/teen park and this place expects children to have credit cards.
Second, Sky zone doesn't respond to to comments about there facilities unless they are get a party bo.  I felt 2 messages over the weekend.  I had a friend call about  booking a party and she got a call the next day and its Wednesday and still waiting for someone/MOD to call and its 4 days later.  Sorry, for birthday party for my youngest will not be booking now.

Chad still waiting","This was my first time at Sky Zone, but my 3-1/2 yr old grandson is an old hat at going as he's a member.  I was VERY IMPRESSED at the activities available for kids of all ages here. My grandson always has a great time and continuously challenges himself to do hard and "scary" things. I thought the place was great!"]

name: Sky Zone Trampoline Park
address: 24000 Van Ry Blvd Suite 100, Mountlake Terrace, WA 98043, USA
reviews:
["I was told that my child had to wear the store’s socks, and the front desk insisted that I purchase a pair. However, I clearly saw many other children inside who were not wearing the store’s socks. I don’t understand why the rule was enforced on us but not on others. It felt unfair and inconsistent.

If this is truly a requirement, it should be applied to everyone equally. Otherwise, it gives the impression that customers are being treated differently, which is very frustrating.","The facility has awesome jumping structures and options for kids, staff are really nice, but it was sooo dirty when we went today. There were stains all over the place, it was seriously so gross. There were also various broken straps and features, and while trying to fix one structure with another mom, for fear our kids could slip under it, a staff mentioned it had been broken for awhile but was perfectly safe because it was anchored down… I was surprised as my friends rave about the facility so many it was just a really bad day? I honestly want a refund on the membership, will be calling tomorrow to discuss this.

1st photo the straps were broken, next two photos both structures were broken, blue phone was structure that has aparently been broken for quite some time, last three photos some of the photos of stains, but there were manyyyy more.","I recently had a birthday party at SkyZone in Mountlake Terrace, and it was an amazing experience! All the kids had a fantastic time jumping around, playing games, and enjoying the trampoline activities. The staff was friendly and helpful, making sure everything ran smoothly and safely. I want to give big shoutout to our party host Zack who made our experience 100 times better. Thanks to him the party area was clean and spacious, perfect for celebrating with friends and family. Overall, SkyZone provided a fun, energetic, and memorable environment that made the birthday celebration truly special. I would definitely recommend it for anyone looking to host a lively and enjoyable party for kids!","I really enjoy this trampoline park — it’s clean, well-supervised, and a great place for people of all ages. As an adult who uses jumping for bone density training, it’s incredibly helpful to have a dedicated space to move safely and stay active.

Today, though, I had several young jumpers repeatedly enter my square while I was mid-air, even after I kindly asked them to stay in their own area. This isn’t a complaint about the kids — they’re energetic, curious, and simply following each other. That’s normal.

What’s not safe is when the adults who brought them aren’t actively supervising.

The park has very clear rules:
one person per square — for everyone’s safety.
Staff handled the situation professionally once I informed them, and I appreciate their attentiveness.

I do want to gently encourage parents to stay engaged and present with their children while they jump. Sitting on the sidelines chatting makes it easy to miss when kids drift into other people’s squares, which can lead to accidental collisions that no one wants — especially when adults are exercising near them.

A little awareness goes a long way.
Children take their cues from the adults who accompany them.

Overall, this is still a fantastic facility, and I’ll continue coming. I just hope all visitors — especially parents — help reinforce the safety rules so everyone can enjoy their time here without any close calls.","I had my party at Sky Zone and it ended up being even better than I expected, mostly because of our host, Kathy. She was super helpful the whole time, making sure everything ran smoothly and even helping serve so we didn’t have to worry about a thing. She was friendly, organized, and just made the whole experience feel easy and fun. Honestly, she’s a big reason the party was such a success. If you ever want to book a party at sky zone, make sure to ask for Kathy."]

name: Urban Air Trampoline and Adventure Park
reviews:
["Horrible experience. Every worker was rude, mean, and acted like they hated their job and the customers. No one was helpful at all, and the attitudes were terrible from start to finish. This is NOT a good place for kids, and I would never bring my little brothers back here.

Huge waste of money. Not worth it at all. If I could get a refund, I would in a heartbeat. I won’t be coming back, and I’ll be telling everyone I know to stay away.","Great people! Energetic team! Very warm welcoming! FUN ATMOSPHERE!!","This place was the worst place I have ever taken my kids to. The staff was so rude and disrespectful. Also the guy in the café he was chubby and short. I’m pretty sure he is the café manager or lead but he had such a bad attitude with us when I was complaining about my food not being cooked on time and them getting my order wrong 3 TIMES!! So unprofessional and unacceptable. Save your money and go to Skyzone on the other side of the block !!😡","I would not recommend Urban Air Adventure Park to anyone. Our experience was honestly terrible from start to finish. The staff were extremely rude and unprofessional, making us feel unwelcome the entire time. Instead of being helpful, they came off irritated and dismissive whenever we had questions.

On top of that, the overall atmosphere just wasn’t enjoyable. Between the poor customer service and lack of clear communication, it turned what should have been a fun outing for kids into a frustrating experience.

There are plenty of other places that actually value their customers and treat families with respect—this is definitely not one of them. Save your money and go somewhere else.","Really disappointed with this place. We took our kiddo here expecting a fun and straightforward experience, but the rules were not clearly explained at all. Nowhere did they clearly state that I as a parent aren’t allowed to even be around or interact with the attractions unless you pay. It led to an awkward and frustrating situation where staff approached us after the fact instead of making things clear from the beginning. The lady kept following me around to tell me the same thing over & over again.

If you’re going to enforce strict rules like that, they need to be communicated upfront—whether that’s at the entrance, on signs, or when you check in. It felt unorganized and honestly a bit unfair. Definitely could have been handled much better.

I will definitely not be coming back."]

name: Urban Air Trampoline and Adventure Park
address: 1502 SE Everett Mall Way, Everett, WA 98208, USA
reviews:
["Super cool play place for the entire family! It was pretty packed because it is new but honestly there was so much to do there wasn't really a wait for anything. My kids loved this place so much and can't wait to go back! Great for all ages. Staff were very friendly as well. Huge parking lot, clean bathrooms. Great price too, you get to do a lot for the price! Definitely worth it!","This place is crazy fun! Price is reasonable, food is not to expensive! Plenty of room to run around! Employee's where very nice and helpful. Ziplines, big slides, Lazer tag, bumper cars , climbing wall,","I took my three kids to Urban Air and this place completely exceeded our expectations. I thought we were just going to a trampoline park, but it is on a totally different level. It has trampolines, yes, but it is so much more than that.

There are obstacle courses that actually challenge the kids, massive slides including a huge green one and a bright yellow one that they could not stop going down, laser tag, indoor bumper cars, basketball courts, soccer areas, and even slam ball. Everywhere you look there is something happening. It feels like nonstop action from the moment you walk in.

They also have this incredible sky ride where the kids are strapped into a harness and glide all the way around the building. It is almost like an indoor zip line that circles the entire place. My kids kept going on it over and over again. That alone made the trip worth it. Watching them fly around with huge smiles on their faces was awesome.

The entire time we were there, my kids kept saying how much better this was than a regular trampoline park. And I get why. It is not just jumping around. It is a full on adventure experience. They never once said they were bored.

There is also a cafe where you can grab food and take a break. We had pizza and were able to sit for a bit while the kids kept rotating between attractions. It is also set up really well for birthday parties, with plenty of space and dedicated areas that make it feel organized.

If you are looking for a place where your kids can burn energy, try new things, and leave talking about it the whole ride home, this is it. We will definitely be back.","It is a good place for kids to have fun and have a birthday party. They tried to fit every kids adventure sport in one small area that makes it very crowded and not enough space even to stand. It is Like putting Sky Zone, Flying Squirrel, and other adventure parks inside a small building. You can tell the stuff is very tired, i won't blame them. The place  have ok food for the kids. This place is like you go to the buffet and  you get to take one bite of every food but you don't get to eat.","We went for a birthday party on a Saturday afternoon at 1pm - prime time chaos levels. The staff at the front were super nice and helpful in making sure we signed the waver, but they didn’t know much else - like where our birthday party was or if they had play bands or where to put our shoes - did we need socks…basic info.

I’m not faulting them for the overstimulating bright, loud and chaos that is kids play areas. But I will fault them for being dirty - considering no one should be weaning shoes, the floor everywhere should not be sticky. Empty tables left covered in food, every glass wall covered in hand prints and the place stank.

There was a decent amount of equipment/machinery broken or out of order. And while there was staff at the entrance - I was hard pressed to see ANYONE working in any of the high traffic areas like the ball pit obstacle course or trampolines. No staff member looked over the age of 18 which made me nervous operating machinery like the bumper cars. At one point the guy “working” just walked away while a little girl sat screaming stuck upside down for the remainder of the ride. All of the staff seemed pretty unconcerned with literally anything happening around them.

The party rooms are pretty nice in that they have glass walls and good tables - not enough chairs though, there were barely enough chairs for the kids and as with all these places, NO place for parents to sit. So we all just stood around, over capacity staring at each other.

All in all - this place is fun for kids, but BEWARE if you have littles, watch them like a hawk. The entire time I felt like my son was going to lose an arm or leg and no one would notice. I even saw a little girl come off the giant jungle gym and ram her face into a metal handle that was NOT wrapped and she instantly had a nosebleed. Wavers or no wavers, they should be safer, have a maximum capacity for some of the areas, more staff and a janitor crew."]

name: Activate Tukwila
address: 300 Andover Park W Unit 650, Tukwila, WA 98188, USA
reviews:
["If you're looking for an activity that promotes movement and the option for cooperative play as well as competitive, this place is great!

Being a really big fan of escape rooms and beat the bomb, this is a nice addition to add to the list.

You are given wristbands that you scan to enter different rooms. Each room has a main concept of a game with different variations to choose from. As you pass each game, you proceed to the next level (you will exit the room and need to reenter). Your progress is saved so you can start from the higher levels the next time you visit (which I plan to do).

This place is very unique and the price was reasonable per person. I felt like an hour was a great amount of time to spend here but could be convinced to do 90 minutes instead as you save $3.00. One of the rooms had a coming soon sign so I'm excited to return and try it.

Staff was very friendly and helpful. Jayden offered our group a tour and even let us know what their personal favorite games were.","Amanda and Sam were great hosts! We had such an amazing first visit. So many games it was the most fun active gaming I’ve done in a while! I can’t wait to come back!","So much fun for our family! 60 min was plenty. Bring lots of water, athletic clothes. Very organized and clean. Kids are already asking when we will return.","So much fun !!! Employees did a great job job at explaining how everything worked they have lockers to keep jackets and lose belongings!!
