You will be running around you being active and having fun a lot of the games are A good combination of mental and physical will definitely be coming back","My kids had a blast here! 90 minutes wasn’t enough - great movement, high energy and interactive games! Competitive and fun! Will be back soon!"]

name: Sky Zone Trampoline Park
reviews: ["This place has gone down hill. Barely any staff to walk around and correct bad behavior, kids run around breaking rules doing dangerous stuff like flips in to other neighboring foam pits while kids are flipping in to it too, I’ve seen numerous fights, kids cutting lines for the slide and taking the slide Mats. The staff does nothing and watches. Big kids in the little kid areas, and OMG dirty dirty dirty.","Love the park had lots of things to do and tired me out at the end of the day. You do need their socks though so that can be expensive if you don't have trampoline socks","There is no trampoline park here. They have been planning to start one here for a long time, but nothing has happened.","This is a very good place to go for birthdays but it does get busy I recommend going on the weekend.","Has a good day instead"]

name: Adventure Alley
address: 2135 Sheridan Rd D, Bremerton, WA 98310, USA
reviews:
["We love this place! We had my son's birthday party here earlier this month and it was amazing. They have 2 separate party rooms with plenty of space in both of them. The staff are amazing. Check in is quick and easy. Do bring socks for everyone in your group as this is a no shoes play. It is a constant rotation of toys and fun with new structures and ideas. They allow outside food and drink and they are consistent on cleaning the toys and areas of use. The owner is almost always available and if they aren't there, the staff is incredibly helpful and knowledgeable.","Perfect for the littles!

We chose this indoor playground to celebrate my one year old's first birthday, and it was a great decision. The space was age appropriate for my daughter and her older cousins, everyone had a great time. We rented their party room, which was spacious and comfortable enough for the 15 people who attended.

Some recommendations I have is bring tape and socks. We had to use tape for the banner, instead of tacks (which was ok). Also to go into the play area, they ask for you to remove your shoes. So if you don’t have socks you can just buy socks there.

My daughter was so happy and we are so grateful for how easy and enjoyable the entire experience was. Would 10/10 recommend.
P.s. I attached a video that you just have to click on to see.","Locally owned and operated. Great place, my kids love it.","Fun place for little kids to play, little sections with different activities. Stayed for about two hours and the entire time there was a little girl playing with no shirt on and appeared to have no adult with her in the main room. Right before I left another adult was trying to figure out who her parent was since she peed through her pullup and the little girl was now taking her pants off in the main room. The adult went to the front desk worker, and it ended up being the owners child. The owners were busy the whole time checking people in/setting up parties.. but it was disheartening to see the little girl not be changed/watched consistently.","Celebrated my daughter’s 3rd birthday. Booked the party room, had an hour and a half. Overall it was a stressful experience, 15mins to set up is not enough time but I understand that other parties are happening after.

The coordination of having multiple parties was not set up properly. We had multiple groups of people walking into the room with older children, touching our cookies, cakes and goodies bags.

I addressed each party respectfully as this was NOT their respective party room. Still adults from other parties were peeking in and look at what we were doing. Yes there is a door but I’m watching my child play so no I will not be closing the door. And as an adult, they should be respectful as to things happening around them.

The concept of a play area is wonderful and I understand that they just opened but there is always room for growth. To add to our experience one of my guests step on a screw so that was an unpleasant.

To allow a cushion, it is a Saturday and majority of places would be packed. That being said older kiddos mingling with little-littles was stressful in itself. My child got told to go away by older girls because they were in their corner.

Would I recommend this for Saturday parties, no. Would I recommend going on the weekday, I’m sure that would be better. Talked with the owner as I left and expressed the same concerns/thoughts.

Wished improvements: longer party room time, set up time & respective room privacy encouragement."]

name: "Artists At Play" Playground
reviews:
["Great playground esp for older elementary age kids to climb. So much to do in this area by the Space Needle. After being in lines or at museums, this was free play and exploration without waiting around. Diverse families. Was there with kids 8 mos - 11 years old. Less to do for the baby but tons for the three 7-11 year olds. Bring hats & sunscreen in the summer, not much shade.","We stumbled upon Artist Playground in Seattle as an alternative to visiting the Museum of Pop Culture—and we’re so glad we did! This spot is a fantastic way to spend a few hours, especially if you have kids. It’s hands-on, engaging, and full of creative energy. The activities are well-thought-out and fun for all ages, and the environment encourages imagination and expression. Whether you’re traveling with children or just looking for a unique experience off the beaten path, this place is a must-visit. Highly recommended!","Great free spot for kids.  Nice park and clean but does not offer much shade.  Could use more shaded areas.  Artful and unique playground for kids aged 5-12.  The metal slide was hot too touch, will burn the lil bum especially if wearing shorts.  Needless to say, we did not risk the kids going for a slide down.  Next to the Museum of Pop Culture.","A wonderfully vibrant, fun, and dynamic play space! Our 10 year old and 12 year old had a great time! Also lots of shady benches for parents to sit and watch the kids have fun. And best of all: it’s free! Get your kids off their phones and take them here to burn off some energy.","Decent free spot for kids, but be prepared for crowds and no shade.

It’s free to play for kids ages 5–12, which is great. However, there’s absolutely no shade, so on sunny days it gets hot quickly, and it’s usually pretty crowded.

There are some benches for parents to sit and watch their kids, though honestly most parents were glued to their phones instead of supervising.

The location is convenient—close to the Space Needle. Along the paths, you’ll find a few vendors selling fruit carts, popcorn, or even offering psychic readings. We also enjoyed some live music from street musicians while we were there.

Overall, it’s a fun stop if you’re in the area with kids, but go prepared for the sun and the crowds."]

name: Seattle Children's PlayGarden
reviews: ["One of our favorite playgrounds in Seattle very unique spaces for kids to explore. We came for open play one Friday and we were disappointed that there wasn’t much difference just the water was on for the kids to play with. You must explore the full park with the kids including the hidden slides, hidden bunnies area and the pirate ship.","Exploring the Seattle Children's Play Garden today. 💛 It's tranquil beautiful grounds and pea patch along with interactive children's play areas were delightful! The park is fenced in and has wonderful shady picnic tables to enjoy your lunch.","The playground looks new and is very colorful and there are quite a few different activties stuffed in this compact park. It's definitely on the smaller side compared to some other playgrounds and playfields, and the park doesn't feel very open.","By far the best place to hangout with your 1-7 year Oldboy or girl. This place consists of nature, hand made features animals and more. It is free and well maintained. Friendly people and great place to meat new friends for your kid and for yourself.","This garden is a utopia for kids and their parents. You can hang out on the playground, walk the beautiful gardens, find some hidden pathways, and even see some cute bunnies and chickens."]

name: The Little Gym of Seattle at Maple Leaf
address: 8830 Roosevelt Way NE, Seattle, WA 98115, USA
reviews:
["We’ve had such a wonderful experience at Maple Leaf Little Gym. My daughter absolutely loves going each week — it’s the highlight of her schedule. The second we pull into the parking lot, she’s already wiggling with excitement and asking if it’s her turn yet.

The staff are truly fantastic. They’re warm, patient, and genuinely great with kids. You can tell they care — they remember names, celebrate little wins, and create an environment that feels both safe and encouraging. My daughter has grown so much in confidence, coordination, and independence since starting here.","I have the pleasure of taking my nanny kid to The Little Gym Maple Leaf. She enjoys her time there and looks forward to it every week. Her face lights up when I mention gym and teacher Carina, who she LOVES and does an excellent job with the class. I appreciate how they switch things up every week so the kids never get bored. Plan a visit to Little Gym Maple Leaf!","Both of our sons have taken classes here and they really love it. We have been part of Funny Bugs, Giggle Worms and Good Friends programs over the last 4 years. The instructors are kind, patient, funny and they do a great job of adjusting the activities based on a child's skill level.  The classes are very engaging and our boys never get bored.

Bonus is that I usually end up having a chat with other parents in the viewing area, but it's also a nice spot to watch your kids having fun, or catch up on reading or work or whatever
.
Highly recommended, thanks Maple Leaf Little Gym team!","Our son started here at age 2 and has had so much fun and such a great experience for 2+ years. Teacher Jackie is awesome and such a tremendous member of the Maple Leaf Community. Grateful to have this in our neighborhood and highly recommend for little ones.","Little Gym has been FANTASTIC for my 3 (almost 4) year old son. The teachers are phenomenal - wonderfully creative and clearly well versed in all things child development. I’ve been so happy with our decision to come here. Such a great way to burn off energy in a friendly, happy environment."]

name: The Little Gym of Seattle at Interbay
reviews:
["We are very grateful for The Little Gym of Interbay!  Especially during all stages of growth (toddler, preschool, elementary & middle school), staff are consistently curating activity spaces, interacting in everyone’s lives in such an empathetic way.  Their expertise & experience can only be rivaled by incredibly impressive commitment, professionalism & knowledge.  Our family feels so fortunate that our son is able to call himself a decade-long member of this curriculum. You all rock! 🤸","Teacher Alexa and Teacher Jayda make class so joyful and wonderful for my 5 year old. They have such positive energy and my daughter is always so excited when she knows it’s a gymnastics day! Teacher Lake is incredible and brings so much enthusiasm and joy and even his own musical talents to my 2.5 year old’s class! We all love love love little gym interbay in our family and can’t recommend it enough!","Can we give 10? Asking for a friend.

Little Gym of Interbay is IT. If you’re a parent looking for an extracurricular activity that actually checks all the boxes, this is your golden ticket.

The staff? Top-tier. Like, can-I-hire-them-to-help-raise-my-kid kind of good. They’re warm, energetic, insanely patient, and know how to channel toddler chaos into focused fun. It’s like they have a superpower for turning “I don’t wanna!” into “Can we go to Little Gym right now?”

Our kids absolutely love it here, and not just because they get to flip, tumble, and bounce off the walls (in a socially acceptable way). They’re building confidence, coordination, teamwork, and learning how to challenge themselves without feeling pressure. It’s the kind of place that sneaks in life skills like perseverance and grit while your kid thinks they’re just having the time of their life.

One of my favorite things? The “fishbowl” setup. Kids are inside having a blast while parents get to watch all the action through the big viewing windows. You get to see your child light up as they nail a new skill, all without hovering. It’s the perfect balance of independence for them and peace of mind for you.

From a parent’s perspective? It’s clean, organized, and safe. The programming is thoughtful and age-appropriate, the schedule options are great, and there’s a real community vibe.

Bottom line: if you’re looking for a place where your kid can grow physically, mentally, and emotionally, with staff who truly care, Little Gym of Interbay is worth every penny and then some. Your future Olympian (or just well-adjusted mini human) will thank you.","Terrific birthday party location! They set up and take down and even cut and distribute the  birthday cake! Highly recommend. Our 4 year old had a blast running around with all her friends from school.","I’m a nanny and my nanny child went to the little gym,  alexia was his teacher and she was great, at the beginning he had a hard time coming but Alexia helped so much , she was caring, understanding and awesome ( still is) the third class we went to he wanted to go right in and play . Thank you alexia for making his time with you fun and caring. Sadly we have to stop because we are transitioning him into school. Will miss coming to her class."]

name: Seattle Gymnastics Academy - Ballard
reviews: ["Birthdays at SGA is so FUN (and hassle free for adults)! Instructor-led play time and room all set up for cake and food. The staff here is extremely helpful and ofcourse wonderful with kids! Especially loved how they set up for a group photo at the end!","I registered my kid for a week of their summer camp to the tune of $775, and less than two weeks later (still in February,) I called to cancel the registration. They're only going to give me an 80% refund. Honestly? Why am I even surprised. What an enormous grift and a huge letdown from an organization that I had thought well of up until now.\n\nEdit: They initially said they were going to take over the weekend to \"consider\" my request (and my honest appalled shock... couldn't have been the 1/5 rating I just left them) but instead they called back just now to tell me they were going to give me a full refund. All I can say is that for you reading this, be COMPLETELY SURE you're going to be able to attend anything you enroll your kid for there, since this seems to be their standard operating procedure. I'm not giving them any more stars for how condescending they were on the phone. You're not doing me a favor here - this is bare minimum business ettiquette and I'm still appalled.","My oldest child is nonverbal and autistic. He has been attending classes at SGA since early 2022. He is currently enrolled in a special Olympics class (organized by the fabulous Coach Katelynn) for kids under 8 years old. We couldn't be happier. Coach Millie is amazing! We love our special little team! My middlest did learn & play together and my youngest will once he is old enough as well. The coaches are absolutely stupendous; Luke, Katie, Jack, are just some we've worked with and adore. The support and community here is just wonderful! I also volunteered with Coach Katelynn and Coach Elaine with their regular special Olympics program for a season and that is fantastic as well.","Had a great time hosting a Saturday evening kids birthday party at SGA! The staff members did a phenomenal job wrangling nearly 20 kids at the tail end of their workday, and I really appreciated their communication/ collaboration when it came to the flow of what would happen next (sign-in, games, cake, etc.) Additionally, the instructor for our class on Saturday has been fantastic, and the front desk staff friendly and kind.","My kid enjoys the indoor playground... when you can actually get in. They recently changed the online registration program to one of first come, first served... at least according to the website and the three emails they sent about the change. We showed up today and were told you can now call up to an hour ahead of time and reserve your spot, but none to worry because they still had space for us. But then while we were waiting in line to register, enough people had called in to reserve that there was no longer space for us and we had to leave. Come on people, do better. Looking forward to giving Tumbles up the street a try when they open next week. It shouldn't be so hard to find activities for our kids at places with clear procedures and competent leadership."]

name: Seattle Gymnastics Academy - Columbia City
reviews:
["Generally, the coaches are great. We have been at SGA in Columbia City for 4 years. Coach J took an active interest in developing my kid's ability starting at 4 years old and now she is an Xcel athlete. Coach H was my kid's favorite teacher.

However, the SGA president fired both these wonderful coaches due to her 1950s puritanical morals. The coaches that the kids love the most are now gone. Given that this was done in the middle of competition season, the athletes' safety and well-being are harmed.

SGA's senior leadership is awful and stuck in the past. We had such wonderful experiences with the coaches. It's a shame that SGA leadership does not listen to the concerns of the parents. Until SGA has new leadership, I recommend parents look for a better venue to foster their children's interests in gymnastics.","Disappointing experience after three years with SGA

My 7-year-old daughter has been taking gymnastics lessons with Seattle Gymnastics Academy for about three years. She started at the Burien location and later transferred to the Columbia City gym when she joined the XCEL Team program this July, coached by Hailey and Lindsey. The program costs around $700 per month for 3-hour practices twice a week, so we expected a positive and supportive experience.

Unfortunately, after a few months on the XCEL team, my daughter began to lose interest in attending class. She said her coach was "not very nice" and that she often felt singled out, which completely took the fun out of gymnastics for her.

We even paid an additional $1,200 Competition Fee Deposit for an upcoming competition, plus another $300 for the required competition leotard, warm-up jacket, and team backpack, which makes this experience even more disappointing.

We don't mind the costs -- as long as it brings joy to her life and she remains passionate about it. Unfortunately, that excitement is gone. I don't believe my daughter has lost interest in gymnastics as a sport -- she still practices her routines and does cartwheels at home -- but the fact that she no longer wants to attend practice tells me this has everything to do with SGA and her experience there.

I truly hope SGA takes a closer look at how some of their coaches, including Hailey and Lindsey, interact with younger athletes. Encouragement and kindness make all the difference -- especially for kids at this age and level.","Both of my daughters have been going to SGA CC since they were toddlers and only breaking during Covid. The recent firing of three coaches for the most insane reason has left my oldest daughter heartbroken and struggling to decide if she wants to continue with the competition season. She has worked so hard to get this far and I used to feel safe sending her there but I don’t anymore. Hailey, Jayme & Lindsey built something special, they were the heart of SGA Columbia City. The president has made it known that every family is just a transaction.","We have been at SGA for 5+ years - multiple kids, multiple classes/teachers, and different levels. I appreciate the kind, supportive environment that SGA strives to facilitate for their students. The classes strike an excellent balance between fun, activity, and building new skills. I’m so grateful that we live close by, especially during the dark winter months- definitely helps my kids get their wiggles out and has grown their love of gymnastics.","Updating from 5 stars to 1 based on a recent experience with the competition team. My daughter was thriving under the tutelage of some amazing coaches, who were fired mid-season without an explanation from management. The whole situation was handled extremely poorly, and I have lost confidence that leadership is in place that can handle the demands of running this business and keep our kids safe. Will update again if things change to help me regain faith in the leadership team."]

name: The Little Gym of West Seattle
address: 7025 California Ave SW, Seattle, WA 98136, USA
reviews:
["We began attending The Little Gym when my daughter was six months old, and she is now 15 months. We truly love it. The classes are consistently engaging, well-structured, and so much fun. I may be a bit biased, but our teachers are absolutely exceptional — special recognition to Teacher Dillion and Teacher Lauren for the care, energy, and enthusiasm they bring to every class.

As a first-time mom, this season of life can feel overwhelming at times, and The Little Gym has provided such a supportive and welcoming environment for both my daughter and me. It has been a meaningful part of our routine and has made a lasting impact on our lives. We are incredibly grateful to be part of this community.","We absolutely love the Little Gym of West Seattle!
We moved to the area from out of state, and I'm soo thankful a new mom friend recommended it.
We tried it out and signed up about a week after moving. It has done wonders to help my little one socialize, and to help me connect with other moms/parents!
We LOVE Teacher Dillon. He has brought big smiles to my little one from the very start. We loved Teacher Ginny and were sad to see her go, but we are also very happy with our new Teacher Lauren.
Every instructor provides a friendly and welcoming environment which has made it so easy for my now almost 15 month old to thrive there. He started at 10 months, and has made leaps and bounds in his confidence and mobility since joining the Little Gym.
It is truly the highlight of our week since moving here, and I can't praise it enough.","The Little Gym of West Seattle has been such a gift for our family. My 6-year-old absolutely loves going each week, and I love watching her grow stronger, more confident, and more coordinated every single time. The balance of fun and skill-building is perfect — they’re not just running around, they’re actually learning real gymnastics fundamentals, listening skills, teamwork, and body awareness in a way that feels like pure joy to them.

Coach Eva deserves a special shoutout. She is incredible with the kids — patient, encouraging, and so tuned in to each child’s personality. She knows how to challenge them just enough while still making them feel safe and proud of what they can do. My daughter lights up when she sees her.

And the Parent Survival Nights? The absolute best. The kids have a blast, and parents get a much-needed break knowing their child is in a safe, structured, super-fun environment. It’s a win for everyone.

Highly recommend The Little Gym of West Seattle if you want your child building confidence, skills, and big smiles all at once.","Kiddos have been coming for years! It's a great place for them to play, build confidence, move, and make friends! We have loved our classes there and all the teachers too! Really impressed by the teachers - they are all so engaged, committed, and sweet. The little gym is a nice addition to our weekly routine. Everyone looks forward to it!","Teacher Lauren is the BEST! She is wonderful with kids and makes everyone feel welcome. She knows everyone’s name and makes a point to connect with each family. We love coming to  Little Gym each week. It is a great community and it’s fun to see all the children grow!"]

name: Seattle Athletic Club
reviews:
["As someone who just joined SAC a few months ago, I need to push back on reviews suggesting the contract is deceptive or manipulative. The contract is easy to understand, and we had ample time to ask multiple clarifying questions in person while signing up. There is no deception or manipulation at any stage, so please take the time to read the contract and ask questions before signing anything. Although I don't love every single aspect of the contract, just imagine how high their expenses are in Pike Place Market. This is a local, small business, not a massive conglomerate. If you don't like the contract, don't sign.

With that said, I love this gym! There are so many pathways to better health, classes, squash, an excellent selection of free weights and weight machines, and lots of choices for cardio including unique ones like a rope trainer, a tread wall, and multiple treadmills and ellipticals. The saline pool is amazing, always at the perfect temp, and only 4.5 feet deep so most people will find it easy to stand if you need a break or feel uncertain about swimming. The locker rooms have saunas, steam rooms, and hot tubs, and they even have massage therapists and trainers on site.

They do a lot of maintenance and they are always improving their offerings. It's clear that they truly care. It's also quite a large gym, very impressive given the location, so there is usually plenty of space. Staff are friendly and helpful, and other attendees are friendly too. It feels like a nice safe space with a diverse community of regular people. It's the best gym I've ever been a member of. I also really like that they have a whole art gallery and participate in the local art community. SAC is a positive force in the community and it's really worth considering if you are looking for an unpretentious gym.","If you’re looking for a masterclass in how not to run a business, Seattle Athletic Club Downtown delivers. The staff - short-handed, out of sync - found time to “tag-team” a prospective member instead of providing basic customer service. Their treatment showed zero respect for disabilities and selective attention depending on who walks through the door. Don’t waste your time here; there are far better clubs in Seattle (Pro Club, Ballard are AMAZING.) that understand how to treat people, maintain clean updated facilities, and actually invest in long-term customer relationships. For a fitness club, they’re remarkably out of shape where it matters most.","Weirdly exploitative experience tarnishing otherwise great gym. I pay the rather steep $155/month membership just to play basketball here because they're the only indoor basketball court in the downtown area. They just announced renovations to their indoor basketball court for 2-3 months from now through October.

Here's where it gets weird. You'd think that since we can't use the court anymore because they initiated renovations on a court that was totally fine, they could put a free pause on our memberships for the period. But no, in the fine print of the contract, "The Club will not reduce or suspend dues during the time when the facilities are not available."

So my options are to
1) Be billed $50 per month to freeze my membership (1/3rd of the regular monthly fee) to NOT use the gym, or
2) Give 30 days notice of cancellation, pay another $155 for the 30 days, and then pay $250+ to rejoin when the court is done with renovations.

Even if it's in the fine print, this doesn't feel right on principle. It's not a couple day repair. It's bleeding your members for months while you do a voluntary renovations. I actually really liked this gym, the front desk is always very helpful and welcoming. I hate that SAC is providing negative value to their basketball members just to make a few hundred dollars while doing a renovation we didn't ask for.","I’ve been a member of this gym for two years and have mostly enjoyed my experience. However, the facility is in serious need of updates—the infrastructure feels stuck in the 80s, and the locker room is painted a dark "pee" like color that doesn’t give off a clean or modern vibe.

More importantly, the customer service has been disappointing. I  have been charger a recurring locker-fee, even though I turned in my locker key and completed the paperwork, yet I continued to be charged a recurring locker fee. When I raised the issue with Natasha, the Membership Director, she placed the blame on me and insisted I pay for a service I wasn’t receiving.

This lack of accountability and unwillingness to help is unacceptable. Unfortunately, because of this experience, I’ll be cancelling my membership.","Seattle Athletic Club on Western Ave is a premier fitness center offering a wide range of equipment, classes, and wellness services. Members value the professional staff, clean facilities, and variety of programs that create a supportive and motivating environment for all fitness levels."]

name: Yesler Community Center
address: 917 E Yesler Wy, Seattle, WA 98122, USA
reviews:
["⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ 4 out of 5

I visited Yesler Park with my toddler and overall, we had a great experience! I’m giving it 4 stars mainly because the splash pad is a total win—perfect for hot summer days. My daughter had the best time running around, and there were other kiddos there which made it even more fun for her.

The playground itself is definitely geared more toward older kids, maybe ages 5 and up. There are a couple smaller structures toddlers can explore, but I wouldn’t say it’s fully toddler-friendly. Still, the splash area definitely makes up for that!

There are picnic tables and plenty of open space to sit and eat or relax. There’s also a community center right next to the park, though it was closed when we came by on a Sunday.

The park has trash bins and bathrooms, and while most of it was pretty clean, there was a bit of litter near the play structures. That part was disappointing—it’s always sad when people don’t clean up after themselves.

That said, the vibe was calm and quiet, and the location is super convenient—close to public transportation, including bus and train access.

All in all, great spot for a casual family outing, especially if you have little ones who love water play!","The community center feeding the kids/community today for the holidays, really touched my heart .. keep up the good work! It’s all about the youth having positive role models, along with a safe space to enjoy their sports, and other little community center activities ..
I feel as though Yesler is going in the right direction; if the staff can start some type of skills training/internship for example barber or plumbing this place would be magnificent.. I really hope they implement a program soon .. but other than that this is a great place.","Large space with indoor basketball area. Public washrooms and single occupancy shower/toilet options.

Vending machines, filtered water fountain with water bottle filling options.
Computer lab.

Great park!!
Splash park in the summer.
Wonderful view of the city from atop Yesler way.","Great community center.  It is a bright, welcoming place.  Restrooms are clean.  The surrounding splash park and play area looks like good fun for kids.  There is a lot of construction happening nearby.","Went there for a family hang and parked in the garage - headed out at 4:45 and the garage was close leaving me and my baby stranded. Still walking home because the car seat is in the car. The garage says it closes at 5 on a Saturday. Very disappointing."]

name: My Gym Bellevue Children's Fitness Center
reviews:
["My son had his birthday party and it was awesome!
Kids had a blast and while staff were giving super fun activities to the kids, adults could relax and chat ☺️
The cake was cool looking and delicious 😋
The decoration for monster truck theme was a perfect set up 🛻","We brought our kid to MyGym to help develop her motor skills. We are grateful for the extra patience and attention they gave her. Shoutout to Patrick who patiently worked with her to warm up to the classes! It has been a very rewarding experience.

We also had a birthday party there and it was a lot of fun!","Our five year old has been going to My Gym Bellevue for over a year now and he absolutely loves it. The Ninja Jr class and the Gymnastics class are amazing and the teachers are so great. Mr Patrick, Abraham, Mia, and others are absolutely wonderful with families and children. Our boy has a ton of energy and we love having a safe and fun place for him to run around, tumble, and do obstacles! We also had our son's birthday party here and it was a blast. Highly recommend My Gym Bellevue!","I have been taking my daughter to Mygym since she was 6 months old and we love this place! We look forward to our mommy and me classes every week. We love all the social interaction and fun obstacle courses they build for them. Highly recommend this gym to any families!","We love My Gym! The instructors are so friendly and fun, and you can tell they really care about each kid. We recently celebrated my son’s 4th birthday there and they made everything so easy, from booking online to party setup to clean up. All the kiddos plus older and younger siblings had a great time!"]

name: Miller Community Center
reviews: ["Less toddler play equipment than other recreational centers, but still plenty for a quick visit. Friendly staff and well maintained facilities.","This community center is top-notch, and the playground has everything a child could want for a fun-filled day!","The playground was fun for my kids, but there was no bathroom available in the area","They have public showers available to people in need bring your own toiletries but they supply a large sized paper towel thing for you to use . Bathrooms are pretty decent and clean","Been here a couple of times attempting to get into the competitive pick up volleyball Mondays & Thursdays. They have a small 20 spot parking lot, but there is ample street parking also if the lot is full. The front desk is helpful if you have any questions. I think this is the best rec center in the area, I heard it went through a recent renovation. Plus the location is convenient for folks like me that live in CapHill!"]

name: Jefferson Community Center
address: 3801 Beacon Ave S, Seattle, WA 98108, USA
reviews: ["For gym is cool but they really only had little cars and scooters and my toddle got bored quickly. Staff is friendly. The playground and spray park are awesome.","Love this park! Has great views, lots of grassy areas, and the community center has some amazing free programs like toddler gym! Only downside is that the playground (besides the swings which had both baby swings and a swing-chair) is a bit big for younger kids. The steps into and out of the playground are pretty steep, so watch out for that, and even the small slide was too steep for my 16 month old. I totally recommend bringing some bubbles for the grassy areas!","My sis needed to use the restroom. There is no restroom in the main building so you have to work across the park to get to an outdoor one. This park was not here when I was a kid living nearby.  The park did not arrive until gentrification did.","Significant community resource. Free Tai Chi Wednesday from 0930-1100","I really can't give much of a review because I was in my car in the parking lot while I was there. But the packing lot gets 4.6 stars because there wasn't a trash can in site from my perspective."]

name: Seattle Gymnastics Academy - Lake City
reviews:
["We had a great experience hosting my kiddo’s
4th birthday party at SGA Lake City. The staff was incredibly helpful—they handled the setup, assisted with serving cupcakes, and even helped us load the car at the end. The space was perfect and kept the kids fully entertained; jumping into the foam pit and swinging off the rope were definitely the highlights. I loved that it was a private space for our group, which is a much better vibe than the crowded trampoline parks. We had a fantastic time and will likely be back next year!","The receptionists gave contradict information to use and made me feel they intentionally tricked us. My daughter was sick on one day in Sep. I called in 2-3 hours before the class to check what's the sick policy. The receptionist told me they can go to makeup class for free which happens on 1st week of every month. So we didn't insist in coming to the gym. The next time we went to the gym in person for a class, and I asked again to confirm the time for makeup class. The receptionist said there's no makeup class here at the gym. It's the gym time on second week of Oct for $5. The saying is completely different from first receptionist. Also every time when receptionist made a mistake, client had to take it though they apologize. Last time when we had a class cancelled, the date was written wrong. We already went to gym by the time and wasn't able to do anything. I feel they don't care about us at all. Two points go to the coaches. They're great and I'm happy with them.","Large facility with a good variety of equipment.

Unfortunately, they have a hypocritical policy of asking all the customers to remove their shoes to keep the place clean, but the staff walk all over the place with dirty shoes on. Including walking on the equipment. Gross!

I think we'll go to other places until the staff stops wearing shoes in the gymnasium.

Update: Thanks for the reply. Having a pair of shoes dedicated to use at the facility is a reasonable approach.","Fun for the kids but safety protocols are severely lacking. When you pick up your child they don't even ask your name let alone check your ID or implement another verification. They just ask "who are you picking up" then they instruct that child to leave with you. Makes you wonder how safe they are while you have your child under their custody","Seattle Gymnastics Academy is an outstanding place for children to explore and grow their gymnastics skills. The coaches are exceptionally skilled, offering personalized attention to each student to ensure they are both challenged and supported. The facilities are top-notch, clean, and well-equipped with everything needed for gymnastics training. The environment is not only safe but also incredibly nurturing, fostering confidence and discipline among the students. I highly recommend Seattle Gymnastics Academy to any parent considering gymnastics for their child. It's a great investment in their physical and personal development."]

name: Pratt Park & Spraypark
reviews:
["Surprisingly well maintained neighborhood park despite being in a busy area. We like the  spray water park they open during the summer months. It can be a little dusty due to the worn areas where the grass is patched. There are a few picnic tables and work out equipments. I'm not sure if there's public restrooms, but the common areas are clean. There is plenty of parking off. Bring snacks and drinks for the little ones.","08/08/23: this park is awesome and peaceful within this neighborhood. It has many playgrounds for kids, basketball, and especially the water area. The park experience was enjoyable, calm, and relaxing. I met many friendly locals here. Would love to be back soon","There are seasons of the unhoused people with garbage littering the grass.

This park offers the community a large grass area where the local schools come during lunch time.  There is a cover basketball court with a number of baskets where I practice my free-throws. Playset area for the kids to climb.  BBQ stations for the adults with trash can for the ash.  A water splash & spray area with restrooms.

Wish that the city could do more to up-keep this area since it's a regular spot for me & my dogos.
There are a steady groups of kids from the nearby schools & preschools & daycare that come to the park to get outdoors.

We as a city need to better maintain & safeguard places like these.

Midas 5-stars for the open areas, the water spray play area, & playground.
Midas 3-stars for the lack of park maintenance.","It’s a big nice wide park with tables and bbq grills but to be honest I would rather bring my own bbq grill, it’s great to bring your kids and dogs here for a nice walk and it’s good for a little reunion with your family also the city is making some changes to the park so they’re adding even more stuff to it the park also has a small playground and training equipment so yeah basically an all around good park and good experience.","The new and colorful equipment, the children like it very much, the environment is clean and hygienic, and the faint floral fragrance of nature fills the air. It feels very good to sit on the grass in the evening."]

name: Northacres Park
reviews: ["Great park to take your kids and fur kids to. They have 2 playgrounds and a water park that is open from 11am to 8pm daily (depending on weather of course). There is also a nice little trail that loops around that is a nice little walk. Definitely one of our favorites.","Super fun place for the kids to let loose, and cool off on those hot sunny ☀️ days. The park offers two whole playgrounds one is for ages 12 months - 3 years old and other is 4-and up, then there’s this whole other area for water 💦 sprinklers. Highly recommend to visit if your ever in the area or just want to hop the I-5 real quick to give kiddos and parents a break from traffic.","Beautiful park... Lits of vegetation, birds, & other wildlife. Peaceful ✌️","Beautiful park with trails running through woods of tall tree and blackberry bushes. There is a refreshing fountain water play park for parents to bring their children to play, as well as 2 play equipment areas and 2 ball fields on either end of the park. Within the trails, you will find entrance to a lovely off leash dog park with a big dog run for your fur baby to get their running out. A little bit of everything for every member of your family as well as the beauty and calm of nature. Parking lots are also on either end of the park by the ball fields and another where the playground area is in the center. In the summer, a free lunch program for children is also provided with a community dental health truck present in the parking lot by the playground in the morning hours around 10.","We went there for our great niece's birthday party. She loved playing in the splash pad with the other kids, her cousin and grUncle. Didn't see any picnic tables but we brought out own lawn chairs and fold up table."]

name: Lake Union Spraypark
address: Valley Street and, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, WA 98109, USA
reviews: ["Amazing place to watch the sunset while listening to the jazz player.","We went out of our way to visit after checking the hours on the parks website. The spraypark wasn’t functional and then we had a very disappointed kid on our hands. It would be great if the city had some way of letting folks know if things were functioning as expected.","Lovely little park away from the hustle and bustle of downtown Seattle. 😁","Its a small spray park  but still  good and refreshing","Currently not on? Usually a great place for kids to play in the summer"]

name: Northacres Spraypark
reviews: ["Fun for kids of all ages. Buckets, spray \"guns,\" and fountains galore. We visited during a \"heat wave\" and the park was busy but not overwhelmingly so. Plenty of space to rest in shaded areas and right next door to the playground.  Win-win!offering. The city government also provides lunch and snacks for children during Summer time.","Absolutely LOVE this place during the summer. Only issue is the tiny front parking and the chaotic back parking. Filled with plenty of trails, 2 kiddy park and a splash pad, what more could a kid ask for!  There's usually someone shady hanging out in the back parking so generally we avoid it. Each playground has its own area designated by age. Unfortunately every time we've gone the playground bully has chosen my kids as their target\nSo please have ACTIVE SUPERVISION on your children, not every kid can be kind. ❤️","Great park with everything! Spray park, mini playground for youngsters and a bigger playground for older kids. Pair that with bike trails and an ice cream truck that swings by in the summers. It’s fantastic and just off I-5 for quick access.","The water part of this park is open after Labor Day weekend. Water turns on right at 11 am, stays on until 8 p.m.\nGood for kids from toddlers through age ten. Lots of shade options for parents and snack times.","very beautiful and feels like a genuine forest. birds singing and greenery provides a satisfying and calming experience of wilderness!"]

name: East Queen Anne Playground & Wading Pool
address: 1912 Warren Ave N, Seattle, WA 98109, USA
reviews: ["Very well maintanned playground for young kids. The slides, swings are not worn out and safe for kids. The kiddie pool is great for the kids to waddle around in during the summers. Great neighborhood.","The whole reason we came was for the wading pool..... and there was no water! That was July 3rd 2025.... I thought it was a seasonal thing! Despite that,  we got there st about 7:30 PM and managed to have a great time, enjoying the fact that there were  hardly any other people there.... so few, in fact, that I was able to let my dog off her leash.","This charming little park is located in the East Queen Anne neighborhood. The wading pool is a delightful feature for children during the warmer months. However, due to the park's size, it can become quite busy, especially when the wading pool is open. Parking is limited, as there is only street parking available.","East Queen Anne playground is in the upper Queen Anne neighborhood, nestled inside of some pretty narrow streets. Street parking is the only thing here, and on nice days, you may have to walk a few blocks to park. It's quite a big playground, with swings and slides plus play areas and a giant sand box area with leftover toys. The wading pool is only on certain times of the year it seems. The bathrooms were in good shape.","Good little piece of Queen Anne history. Definitely reminiscent of a century of different park ideas. The size and variety of the trees proves that. A slice of many generations of Americana. Picturesque.\nOne can imagine Norman Rockwell painting long old now children playing baseball. A bit of an oasis."]

name: Fritz Hedges Waterway Park
address: 1117 NE Boat St, Seattle, WA 98105, USA
reviews:
["The small, cozy viewpoint beside the river is an enchanting gem that invites you to experience nature's wonders up close. Nestled beside the serene riverbank, it offers a tranquil escape from the noise of city life. The calming sound of flowing water and the gentle rustle of leaves create a soothing ambiance that instantly puts you at ease. The view of the river is mesmerizing, and you can watch the water gracefully meander along its course. It's the perfect spot to immerse yourself in the beauty of nature, take a leisurely stroll, or simply sit and savor the peaceful surroundings. If you're seeking a tranquil oasis by the river, this cozy viewpoint is a delightful destination to embrace the serenity of nature's embrace. 🏞️🌊","A great place to calm and enjoy the view! Kids (and dogs!) are having fun in the bay. There are some street parking along the way. I really love visiting here. There are some chairs on the deck so that you can take a seat and draw or journal. I tried to go picnicking there, but bees often come to the food or me, which is quite scary for me, so I am not sure if it is a good choice to picnic there if you don’t like bugs.","This pretty little park is north of lake Union and affords some interesting views. You can see the I5 bridge and University bridge.
There are plenty of chairs to sit on and enjoy the lake. It is a place you can stop by with some coffee or tea. On a sunny day, you can chill out here.
There are boat launch ramps too. Stop by here to enjoy the northern part of Portage Bay!","Great park by Lake Union. This park is relatively new in the area.
- Great spot for sunset
- Nice vibe on sunny days, people are having picnic, reading books, eating food, launching their paddleboards, or swimming.
- There is paddleboard renting place nearby
- You can also watch floating houses right across the lake","Urban Oasis by the Water

Fritz Hedges Waterway Park is one of Seattle’s best-kept secrets. The landscaping is beautifully maintained with chairs installed to relax by the water. There’s direct access to the lake, so you’ll often spot kayakers and paddleboarders gliding by.

It’s perfect for a low-key hangout, reading by the shore, or enjoying your coffee with friends. A true gem for locals and a must-visit for anyone who appreciates peaceful urban greenspaces with a splash of nature.

🌿🌊🌞"]

name: Spraypark
address: 1100 SW Cloverdale St, Seattle, WA 98106, USA
reviews: ["Nice free place for your kiddos to cool down on hot days. Public bathroom right next to the spray park and playground right next to it. There is also a soccer field and tennis court in this park.","Really cute small splash park.","Great park"]

name: Wallingford Playfield Wading Pool
address: 4219 Wallingford Ave N, Seattle, WA 98103, USA
reviews:
["Nice new and safe playground for 5-12 y.o. kids. Has two separate playgrounds for different ages. Very tall monkey castle with plenty of fancy tools","There isn't much of an area for little tots to play aside from the dirtiest swings I've ever seen! Nor is there much shade...and the grass looks pretty had.

But my nephews who are older toddlers enjoyed running around the perimeter of the park in the pseudo trails. Pretty dusty and slippery up there though.","The wading pool is great for little kids and babies. It has a sloped side, so crawlers can explore, and goes to maybe two feet deep. Not much shade though, bring your sun umbrella. Often has water toys and music too!","Small but a neighborhood classic. The maintenance gentlemen has been there for years. He can be a bit straight forward for kids but he keeps the place clean, no splashing, and plays sweet tunes.","Best wading pool in Wallingford. But also only wading pool in Wallingford.

Go early if possible. The water does tend to get crappy as time passes by. Also to note — go only if the temperature is above 75F. Else they will not be open.

Infant friendly as well. But make sure you take sunscreen and maybe some sunshade as there is not much shade around the pool during the summer."]

name: Volunteer Park Wading Pool
address: 1417 E Highland Dr, Seattle, WA 98112, USA
reviews: ["Very nice wading pool. Clean and nice surroundings.  We enjoyed our afternoon here.","Great activity for younger kids especially if you take with you some kind of water gun.\nWater turns on at noon. Enough shade.\nPlaygrounds and swing sets nearby.","Busy on hot weekend. Perfect for young kids and families. Shallow water. Plenty of shade on the side.","Rude employee who is monitoring the pool usage. He's unnecessarily aggressive, when it would be much more professional to be polite and informative. The issue when this pool fills up is that kids aren't permitted in the stream of water, where the pool fills from. I don't understand why the attendant doesn't fit right by there to deter kids from trying to play in it, rather than screaming at them from across the pool in the shade.","What a great place. Perfect for kids to play in the water, while families to enjoy picnic at the shaded park around the pool. Plenty of parking in the park and neighborhoods."]

name: Waterfall Garden
reviews:
["Amazing little escape from the city, in the city. I've had this place saved for awhile now and we were in the area anyway. It's beautiful and smells of fresh flowers. The water is clean and so are the tables and chairs. Very well maintained and not vandalized which is surprising for the area. Walking distance to Saigon Drip Cafe (get the dumplings and matcha green tea, yum)","A peaceful little reprieve in the heart of the city! I felt like I should’ve planned better and brought a lunch. Someone else was indeed enjoying a coffee— strangely the only other person there around lunchtime on a Friday. This would make a really cute date spot!","Tiny little water feature nestled between buildings. We didn't get tickets or anything (trip was on a M-F during work hours).
There were places to walk in and relax for a few minutes.
There are beautifully tended plants and flowers.
If you aren't staying near by, there is interesting stuff to see on the way to the tranquil garden, like a vacant lot that holds what I can only imagine to be Satan's time out chair.","The Waterfall Garden Park in Seattle's historic Pioneer Square is a true hidden gem and a delightful surprise amidst the urban landscape. Though small, this pocket park offers an incredibly effective escape from city noise and provides a moment of tranquility.
Highlights:
* Powerful Waterfall: The centerpiece is a 22-foot high waterfall that cascades with significant volume, creating a surprisingly loud and soothing roar that effectively drowns out traffic sounds.
* Peaceful Ambiance: With plenty of tables, chairs, and benches, it's an ideal spot to enjoy a quiet coffee, eat a packed lunch, or simply sit and relax. The sound of the water is incredibly calming.","Cute and peaceful place to recharge and get away from the hustle and bustle of the city. The roaring waterfall blocks out all the noise and there are even already flowers blooming and lots of chairs to sit at"]

name: Crossroads Water Spray Playground
reviews:
["I visited the park first time  for my daughter’s play date and I loved it.  It was the beginning of June and summer sun was hitting. The water springs and sprinklers really helps and kids love it.  Big space, Big park

Nice for picnics and depending on the time of day, you could find your mind mending process achievable here.","Crossroads Water Spray Park is a fantastic spot for families looking to cool off and have fun. The park features a variety of water sprays, fountains, and interactive water elements that entertain children for hours. It's well-maintained, clean, and safe, providing a great environment for kids to play. The surrounding area also offers picnic spots and a playground, making it an ideal place for a family outing. Overall, it's a delightful and refreshing destination on a hot day.","I deferred to the children and they were not impressed. Although the surroundings are beautiful, not enough water for their liking, no swings, and no wading pool.
Plenty of green areas to run around and for the adults to relax. There are shaded areas and bathrooms facilities available.","Well maintained Bellevue Park with parking and safe for all ages. Sometimes great outdoor movie events. Bellevue keeps this location safe from vagrants. You won't find needles on the ground here like in Seattle.","Perfect park for all ages. You cycle skate picnic.  My kids love it here. Summer are best time as you have a beautiful spray park"]

name: John C. Little, Sr. Park
reviews: ["It is nice, clean, and spacious park that is near my house. I love the kid's playground, and picnic area but not the restrooms. Homeless people use restrooms to use drugs, so it doesn't look safe to get in. More importantly, they also have a spray area, and I cannot wait to see it works in the summer.","New to the neighborhood & park is walking distance so we frequent daily. Small park w spray area. Bathrooms are maintained daily BUT the local homeless always turn it into a cesspool. Some less than desirable characters always near by but it's not the park's fault. It's a shady area. Small community garden attached. Small picnic area. Decent park for what it is. We make the most of it. :)","love this park.  almost go there every day.","Everything is great except for the big slide. The hole in the center of the platform is very dangerous for little ones. I barely grabbed a one year old (not mine)who almost fell through. Yes the parent was right there also. Scary moment but ended with no harm done except a shocked baby and a grateful dad.","Great park for all ages. It has two different park structures for ages 2-5 and ages 5-12. I love that it has a tire swing. It also has regular swings too. There is this fun umbrella like structure that spins around when you hang onto it. During the summer, you can play in the water fountain area. There are two shelters for families who want to barbecue. Make sure you reserve a spot during the summer weekends, otherwise it's first-come first-served. There is also a big grassy area adjacent to the park, great for picnickers. There is only street parking but it is easy to find since it's located across a nursing home."]

name: View Ridge Playfield Wading Pool
reviews: ["Located in the middle of a decent neighborhood. Large ballfields for any kinds of activity. Nice playground for kids and there is a wading pool in the summer time. A small basketball field available. Street parking should be fine most of the time as never see the field occupied according to my experience","Terrible, 60+ year old wading pool that is unattended by the attendant that sits around and reads mystery novels while the pool fills up with leaves and filth. Needs an updated water works play area like Beacon Hill's parks.","Great local park! Very nice playground and a big swing set, with a huge open play field for baseball and soccer.","Everything you need there. A wading pool, big slides, big swings (for every ages !!)\nOnly bad side is that the water fountain doesn't work but I'm sure it will in summer","Ballfields are nice, playground ok, parking can be a challange"]

name: Spraypark
reviews: ["Great place when it's really hot! Some shade to sit in if not too busy."]

name: Ron K. Bills Spraypark
reviews:
["A lot of tents around the field (to the right of the sculptures & behind the tennis court. *Nothing wrong with that.

Appears the place is neglected a bit. Could use some maintenance. Other parks in the area are way more maintained.

Nonetheless, still a cool area. Lots of kids playing in the jungle gym type playground. Small Basketball present. Tennis court has a mini court to play spikeball.

Grass on field is a little high. *I can only hope park gets maintained in the future. 4/5","Cute Park to bring the kiddos! Nice spray feature for those hot days so bring your wet clothes and have fun! Make sure they wear sandals tho the water makes a very slippery ground by the spray feature. My son got ouies on his knee and foot because he didn't want to listen to his momma who knows best to keep his flip flops on.","Very simple design wise as interactive fountains go, but a great place to cool off for free for all ages.","Nice park","Nice for kids"]

name: Van Asselt Playground Park
address: 7050 Beacon Ave S, Seattle, WA 98108, USA
reviews: ["Spent a good amount of time playing here. One time my family brought me here to play badminton but I didn't want to play so I made flower crowns and got a really bad allergic reaction. Another time I found a stick I really liked and some other kid took it but my brother got it back for me. Used to go to a lot of baseball games here which was kind of boring but there was always some stuff to entertain myself with.","08/01/23: this park is large with tons of good playgrounds and beautiful views. I enjoyed the giant soccer field and the peaceful view of nearby neighborhoods. Would love to be back and highly suggest people visit this awesome park","A nice park, plenty of room for youth sports. The nearby Community center has a very nice little playground with exercise equipment.","Fun, safe place for the kids to play. The playground surface is soft and ready for kids to run and tumble in. There is a wide array of equipment and play-gym  equipment. A very attractive option for parents looking for an activity area for their energetic kids!","had a blast with the wadding pool. nice fun clean park"]

name: Highland Park
address: 1100 SW Cloverdale St, Seattle, WA 98106, USA
reviews:
["Great open place for the kiddos to play! Also starting Saturday the spray park will be back on as well! Sometimes during the summer they also hand out lunch and snacks here! Bathrooms are pretty clean!","✓IF YOU HAVEN'T HAD THE PLEASURE OF EXPERIENCING THE REBUILT 110% UPDATED (that's an understatement in my opinion!) HIGHLAND PARK PLAYGROUND & SPRAYPARK THEN YOU SHOULD KNOW A FEW THINGS BEFORE ARRIVING::::::>
1.the perimeter of the entire park/playground sits on a few different residential side streets depending on which way you would be driving soon in a nutshell it's seems nearly impossible for a parent or a family member or friend not be able to have eyes on whomever from a distance
2. HIGHLAND SPRAY PARK AND PLAYGROUND IS AN ENTIRE DIFFERENT LOCATION ACTUALLY (NOWHERE NEAR EACH OTHER IF YOU'RE WALKING) FROM HIGHLAND PARK SOCCER FIELDS. (hate to admit that itty bitty bit of information has slipped my mind. . . more than once 😑🤐🤔😸)
3.ideal spot for watching sunrises & sunsets
4. when daylight starts to dim no matter the season temperatures etc. it gets really REALLY windy there so make sure to have a jacket available if needed and to secure any pop-up structures in the grass with stakes or something similar
5. I have now attended 7 different kids birthday celebrations here and each time the parent is super satisfied with their free gathering spot but regardless of your childs age there's something for everyone big small short tall brown skin white skin THE PLAYGROUND DOESN'T DISCRIMINATE EVER","Highland Park Playground has gotten 2 wonderful upgrades in the last couple years. First a spray park and this summer a new playground. The new playground has a lot of great features, swings, slides, multiple climbing structures. One of the structures is very unique with multiple rubber matts strung between 2 supports. kids can climb, bounce or just lounge on it.","What a fun park to play at! My son and I had a blast playing on the playground here!","I live close by and around daily. The landscape out front is all dead and should be cleaned up.  Not a good look when the neighborhood yards are well maintained. The shrubs out front have been dead for at least 2 years.hope that thus will be taken care of soon. The neighbor of your property."]

name: Soundview Playfield & Wading Pool
reviews: ["Soundview playfield is a big neighborhood park with quite a few ball fields and other facilities like tennis courts and a playground. There is a lot of open space here, seems ideal for locals to walk and jog. I came by in the morning and it was quiet and nice here.","Very quiet and peaceful park. I like standing under the cedar trees and feeling small in comparison. It has plenty of parking, it's well maintained & in a safe neighborhood. It has a children's playground too.","FYI, as of 8/4/24, the wading pool doesn’t have water in it! We’ve seen a number of disappointed kids arrive in swimsuits and with water toys, so wanted to give an update!","Great wading pool! There was shade over about half the pool and it wasn’t very crowded at around 1 pm on a Saturday. The pool varies in depth from about 1-6 inches, so the shallowest area is good for babies to sit in. They empty and refill the pool daily, so the water was very clean too!","I take my dog here and throw the ball around for her because there's just so much space. I've heard that some people will get ticketed for this though even when no one's around so watch out."]

name: Georgetown Spraypark
address: 750 S Homer St, Seattle, WA 98108, USA
reviews: ["Great splash pad! Kids loved it! Small sprinklers for the little ones as well as big ones.\nSome picnic benches near by with shade and a nice playground.\nOnly downside: not the best neighborhood.","This park has a lot of fun things to do in the playground but kids enjoy more playing with the water and spend a lot of time doing that and I got to sit in a bench and watch them enjoy and run around."]

name: E.C. Hughes Playground
address: 2805 SW Holden St, Seattle, WA 98126, USA
reviews:
["The playground was updated 2 years ago and is excellent, with structures for two age groups, swings, and some creative extras, all on soft wood chips. The rest of the park is good, but showing its age. The bathrooms and concrete pebble wading pool are both serviceable. Half a dozen picnic tables and grills are distributed throughout the north end of the park.
The 3 basketball hoops (full-court + half-court) are on serviceable asphalt. The two baseball diamonds (North-East and South-West) are complete with dugouts and have natural infields. The two soccer fields (South-East and North-West) have decent goals of various sizes and rough natural turf (not exactly good, but not dangerous).","Decent size park on the top of the hill (It's quite a climb!) with a ball field, a playground, a wading pool, a basketball court, some picnic tables and swings. Seems a nice place to chill in the neighborhood. There are public restrooms too, not the cleanest but they are open.","Street parking. Has a bathroom area. Has a play area, basketball court, baseball, soccer and swings. Also had benches, grill for BBQ outdoor, and shades. Such a nice hidden spot with much potential but the stuff here are old. Would come back. Wish it was more updated. A lot of good things here.","Awesome neighborhood park that has everything! Two baseball fields and soccer goals (can get a bit muddy). Basketball courts. Awesome new playground with swings and slides. Fun balancing balls. Splash pad/wading pool in summer time, assume that's when the bathroom building is open...","Cute little playground for the kids to run around. Took the kids here one day to get out of the house. Now we don’t live in the neighborhood but just happened to drive by. The playground is a bit dated but still in good condition.

This park has a big open field, swings, a small sand lot and a wading pool.

There were toys in the sand lot, at the time we went, that didn’t belong to anyone (at least I don’t think). So if it belongs to the park or if someone nice left it there for everyone to play with, that was a nice gesture as my kids enjoyed playing with the toys.

We will definitely make a trip here again."]

name: Schmitz Preserve Park
address: 5551 SW Admiral Wy, Seattle, WA 98116, USA
reviews: ["Great spot to get lost in the woods in West Seattle.  Very mature growth trees and a maze of trails around the preserve.   Very green and forested.   No signage in park for trails, but Google Maps is pretty spot on for the trail system in preserve.","Right off the main road but feels like you’re in a different world. Can be hard to find the entrance as it’s literally off a Main Street with little signage, but once you find it, it’s quite wonderful to walk through. Could spend hours in here lost in the greenery and calm.","Hidden Gems!\n\nSchmitz Preserve Park has the same entrance as Japanese American Exclusion Memorial. Place to relax and chilling.","What an incredible opportunity for W Seattle! The colors, the sounds, the location and the simple joy of just walking and observing your surroundings. Such a treat to spend some time in here going solo...talk about replenishing the spirits! Outstanding trails! Go get some!","My favorite place in West Seattle and possibly one of my favorite places in the entire city. This is one of the few stretches of old growth still remaining in Seattle: most of the area looked like this before the arrival of the settlers, all lush ferns and mossy trees that are so dazzling in their height that their uppermost branches remain hidden. There is nothing particularly artful or consciously designed about this area: aside from the paths and bridges winding through it, much looks exactly as it did before Seattle existed. There is one area that particularly captivated me, wherein these immense Western red cedar trees emerge out of the monumental stump of a much older tree that no longer exists. The stump is so large and worn that it looks more like a stone Cliff from a distance, but it is in fact the foundation of a collection of new trees that have emerged, their trunks arcing and winding at different angles to provide space away from the parent tree, only then to shoot upward, their grainy and richly textured branches reaching toward the sunlight. One turns so sharply from horizontal to vertical that it creates a right angle not dissimilar to the curve of an elbow. It is in red cedar trees like this that one feels most fully the age of the area, feeling that there was an entire world here before the Victorian establishment of the city."]

name: Wolf Tree Nature Area
reviews: ["What a great hiking area to have nestled in the city! There is plenty of parking in the north parking lot of Discovery Park, bathrooms, and lots of space to run around and play in the main area. The main road is paved, but the hiking within this area is not.","This place is absolutely beautiful! There's a few paths in this trail. It's so easy to get lost in all this beauty! (In a good way)","Lots of trees down on the trail. And tragically the iconic alder - cedar symbiotic support trees over the boardwalk gave way sometime this weekend 10/24/20.","Very peaceful and not busy.","Nice short loop behind the parking lot. Great for a quick trail or if you have toddlers"]

name: Redmond Watershed Preserve
address: 21760 NE Novelty Hill Rd, Redmond, WA 98053, USA
reviews: ["Such a nice place to get in a good walk in the Redmond area. Really well shaded and has a decent amount of up and down terrain ensuring you get a workout. Especially beautiful after the rain with all the moss and mushrooms. The parking lot does fill up fairly fast so try to get there early on weekends","Beautiful, large Preserve with many trails into deep woods. Just yesterday we tried the other side of the park and found even more trails! Perfect place for hiking and being in nature. There's bathrooms and picnic tables just before the parking lot, which is large and has disabled parking and access as well. The Tree Frog Loop is paved and a wheelchair could easily use it.","Such a beautiful area to go for a walk.  We loved the paved trails. The skunk cabbage were so pretty among all of the green. The skunk cabbage are the yellow leaf plant in the photos.","Beautiful trails. Parts are covered with trees and the trails are wide and well maintained. There are lots of trail runners, bikers, and even some horseback riding. I could see it being a bit muddy on a rainy day but it was in the soft side when I went today.","Green and mossy trees align this nature preserve.  Trails where one can walk , hike even trails for biking. ( some trails no) see signage. Big area adjoins the Puget Power Pipeline. It’s a popular bike route.  The dog will love this walk , guaranteed!!  BUt, no dogs signs were posted in some areas as Horses are allowed in some areas.  Horse 💩piles in middle of trails walkways! Ridiculous they don’t have to pick it up! Us dog owners have to and dogs poop on plants or side of trails not directly on walkways.  It’s everywhere old piles and new.  This park links to others making it just a huge area for all.  There are areas dogs aren’t allowed. This area is huge stretches miles and miles.  I got lost hence the pics of signs. We been going for 5 days and still haven’t walked it all.  Good luck! There’s water on one end which I didn’t find."]

name: Arboretum Waterfront Trail
address: Arboretum Waterfront Trail, Seattle, WA 98112, USA
reviews:
["It’s beautiful but not currently fit for running 😭😭

When it passed through (520-arboretum) the entire trail was on the brink of becoming one with the lake, and it hadn’t rained in days!

Otherwise the design is very unique, and the views off the trail both over the lake and into the marshy islands is very relaxing.

Take your time when passing through, and don’t try to jog!

Give up if you aren’t wearing close toed shoes, and probably don’t come here after heavy rains.","I’m not sure if this is hidden, but it’s certainly a gem. It feels like a menagerie of parts—bending walkways that get submerged in the water, floating pieces positioned at odd angles, muddy paths. This all contributes to a dynamic and fun trail.

It connects north of the Arboretum to Montlake, and as the name would imply, provides many gorgeous water views. You can see boating and sports activities, as well as wildlife, out on the lake. I once saw a harbor seal hunting American Coot here. Felt like a real life nature documentary.

As lovely as the trail is, there are some negatives. Parts of the trail are constantly muddy, I think as a result of tides. You’ll want to wear shoes that you don’t mind getting dirty, and ideally are water-resistant. The other negative is the trail’s proximity to traffic.

Right behind the trail is the Evergreen Point Floating Bridge (520 Bridge). You even walk under it on the way up from the Arboretum. It’s not that bad though, hence 5 stars for the trail. Walking under the bridge is kind of cool, and as you walk along the trail, you hardly notice the bridge’s presence.

Definitely add this to your next walk at the Arboretum. And if you like birdwatching/wildlife, don’t forget your binoculars!","a trail offering sweeping views of union bay toward husky stadium and laurelhurst with boats occasionally cutting across. characterized by its trail traversing dense woods never far from the water’s edge, twisting, weathered wooden bridges, and a pocket of (relatively) undisturbed marsh habitat. expect water lilies blotting out still water surfaces, both common and uncommon bird species, and plenty of well-placed benches or platforms on the bridges or side trails to take a break, sit down, and take in the fresh air and picturesque scenery.

floating bridges don’t seem to have waterlogging issues this time like i’ve seen in past years, though it’s probably a frequent occurrence when water levels rise. regardless it’s advised to bring shoes you wouldn’t mind getting wet or dirty with the mud you’ll find even during warmer months. some areas may form puddles too long to hurdle during wet seasons.

started today from the montlake bridge area going towards foster island — although the road noise from the 520 can seem pervasive at times, its distance from the trail mostly adds to an atmosphere of separation from the city when the sound softens, whether it’s as the path twists wide or enters thicker patches of brush. the trees and rushes sometimes hang a bit low and/or across the path, but trail runners should still be able to manage as the main trail is generally wide enough for easy passing. most visitors are leisurely walking and i didn’t see too many dogs out here today.

beautiful in summer, beautiful in spring, incredible in autumn on an overcast, drizzling day. a magical experience today","Fun and exciting day trip. Lots of ducks and geese.","This is my favorite spot to be in nature. It has unique islands you can walk to. It's has beautiful views on lakes Washington and Union. Located in the beautiful Montlane neighborhood. This is what it's like to live in Seattle."]

name: Interlaken Park
address: 2451 Delmar Drive East, Seattle, WA 98102, USA
reviews: ["It's a beautiful, somewhat wild little forest, but be prepared for some steep hills and plenty of ups and downs.","Interlaken Park is a narrow and meandering green space. You can access the park through Interlaken Blvd from both sides or 19th Ave from South. There is only one main road/path so it's not hard to navigate. Very quiet spot to take a walk or jog. A great place for runners to enjoy together with Volunteer Park and Washington Arboretum.\n\nThere is no lighting on the trails so make sure you bring your headlamp if you are up for a run in the trail before sunrise.","This metaphysical shop has a great selection of books, tarot decks, talismans, rings, & pendants, and statues. Everyone who works there are friendly, helpful people with deep roots in our community. Best in Seatle","We didn't get to explore much of the land of the park, with the exception of traveling end-to-end via the Parkway. One section was not great on foot due to limited space on the roadway (there wasn't a sidewalk), but there are some trails going off the road on which people can run. The scenery is splendid. I'm sure the ride (by bicycle) is gratifying both up and down.","A beautiful and clean park, but a coyote followed my dog and I. It circled back and came toward us from behind. I luckily heard a branch break in its steps when it was about 15 feet from us. It wasn’t phased by me yelling, and required me throwing sticks to scare it off. Needless to say, I won’t be returning with my pooch 😔."]

name: Leschi-Lake Dell Natural Area
address: 3525 E Terrace St, Seattle, WA 98122, USA
reviews: ["Perfect if you want a genuine get away. This place is beautiful, but rough around the edges. It's a lovely place to with a friend. Alone, maybe not ideal. But still amazing?","Unmaintained, scenic view is barely visible over overgrowth, lots of trash","Fun little find. Great view for an evening walk.","Lovely area. If you're afraid of isolated spaces, stay clear. Seems like a potential crime could happen here. Still, as a white male I felt fine and appreciated seeing Bellevue from a Distance.","A beautiful viewpoint to appreciate the beauty of the Pacific Northwest"]

name: Schmitz Park Trailhead
address: Schmitz Preserve Park, 5463 SW Stevens St, Seattle, WA 98116, USA
reviews: ["Discover an incredible hidden paradise in West Seattle! Stroll along enchanting trails that lead you to serene creeks and charming waterfalls, straight out of a fantasy novel. This well-maintained gem offers a peaceful retreat from the hustle and bustle, making it the perfect spot to connect with nature and find your inner calm. It's an extraordinary place to explore and meditate—truly a slice of tranquility waiting to be uncovered!","Schmitz Park in Seattle is a hidden gem in the city. This beautiful park offers a peaceful retreat from the hustle and bustle of urban life, with its lush greenery, towering trees, and scenic trails. The tranquil atmosphere makes it a great spot for a leisurely walk or a relaxing picnic. The park is well-maintained and provides a wonderful opportunity to connect with nature right in the heart of the city. I highly recommend visiting Schmitz Park for a rejuvenating escape into the outdoors.","I recommend parking at this trailhead versus any other entry point to Schmitz Park... plenty of street parking. Once inside you really start to feel the magic of an old growth forest... it's easy  to forget about the urban setting surrounding the park. As I made my way through these lush trails I was fascinated to see all the mushrooms and fungi spread about this preserve.","Fantastic place to take little kids! Easy walking and lots to look at. My toddler loved waiting in the stream!","Schmitz Park is awesome. Old growth in the heart of West Seattle. I lived real close to the park and would take my kids down to the trails. It's one of my favorite parks. There is also a unused road that goes down to the beach. It can get pretty muddy, so wear boots or go in the summer."]

name: Foster Point Trail
address: Foster Point Trail, Seattle, WA 98112, USA
reviews:
