["Beautiful park! Located conveniently next to Washington Park Arboretum/The Japanese Garden, you could easily spend a few hours walking around both parks.

The bridge leading through the water from Foster Island to Marsh Island is pretty cool, there are tons of lily pads surrounding the bridge. The trail on Marsh Island can be muddy.

Overall, worth checking out, especially if you’re a Seattle native and you’re tired of taking visitors to the same tourist attractions over and over again.","The Arboretum as a whole is such an amazing and wonderful park to find in the middle of a metropolitan.  We're incredibly lucky to have this in Seattle.  The Foster Point Trail is one of my favorites  in the park.  Any time of year is going to be an excellent time to visit.  Each season offering different views, smells and levels of use. Obviously this whole area is just hammered during the summer but still worth a visit.","Foster Point Trail meanders through Foster Island and Marsh Island and connects Washington Arboretum with East Montlake Park. It's easy to notice the trailhead at the north end of the Washington Arboretum loop.

On a good day, it's a beautiful trail hike/jog with unique views of Union Bay thanks to the special "from-inside" vantage point.

When I used the trail, there was a segment marked with something like "Danger: Wasp" which made me quite uncomfortable because the trail is already pretty narrow. Otherwise I really enjoyed the little adventure above the bay.","Actually, I wanted to visit the Washington Park Arboretum UW Botanic Gardens to take a walk and pictures. However, it has not special view or something. It was just a park for a walk. So, I decided to stop walking through it and to go to the north of the Washington Park Arboretum UW Botanic gardens. Finally, I found this spot. This place is not crowded as well as has a fantastic view. If you go to this island when sun sets, you can see the most beautiful view in Seattle. Thanks.","Easy city trail, quite short. Nice view over water at the end"]

name: Kingfisher Natural Area on Thornton Creek
address: 17th Ave NE &, NE 104th St, Seattle, WA 98125, USA
reviews:
["Really didn't get a chance to walk through it. Probably best for spring or summer time walk. Unless you got some waterproof shoes and good traction. No Parking. Street parking in front of homes. From the little bit of what I seen it looks fun","The good: Beautiful creek, quiet, nobody else there, paths are a range of DIY ruins (which I like), nice foot bridges at Eastern end of paths

The bad: Steep / slippery / muddy, not for the less nimble, no open spaces / views / sun, homeless encampment under bridge at W end of path

Maybe good if you are nearby and want creek access for kids to play, although I'd be wary of the water quality. Shame we have open spaces like this and aren't maintaining them.","Absolute oasis near Northgate! You get the impression the neighborhood doesn’t want to share the secret on this great bit of woods. Trail heads are somewhat hidden and the path is slightly overgrown but this a lush green belt rich with life. Be like the spotted Barred Owl I saw and enjoy the creek - even if the locals squawk.","Amazing, huge green space right in the middle of a very residential area. Not many encampments, but there are a few approaching the bridge at the far end. Like a scrappier version of Ravenna/Cowen Parks.","You could almost convince yourself you are deep in the Cascades here.  Check out the trails that amble along Thornton Creek and neighboring hillsides, what a delight right in the heart of the city."]

name: End of Burke Gilman Trail
address: Seaview &, Golden Gardens Park Rd, Seattle, WA 98117, USA
reviews: ["Your reward for a walk, run, or bike ride is this lovely view! Burke Gilman connects to Sammamksh River trails and can let you go up to 42 miles unbroken except for a short piece of Ballard.","This is a great spot to start the Birke-Gilman bike trail.","Every new beginning comes from some other beginning's end","Great run end point, Ballard to this is about ~5 miles.","It actually feels more like the start of the trail rather than the end of it, but either way, it serves its purpose."]

name: Discovery Park
reviews: ["Beautiful park with lots of small trails. Perfect for walking with friends or your dog. Once you get to the beach area, it’s magical on a sunny day. Restrooms are scattered across the park and are in good condition though toilet paper may run out by the end of a busy day. Lots of wonderful picnic areas with a view it by a pond","A beautiful park and a great place to spend a sunny day! So nice to be in nature so close to the city. We attempted the main loop with a stroller which did fairly well until the Bird Nest area and then we went back on the paved path. So not sure if a stroller could do the whole thing but I won’t be attempting again. It wasn’t terrible but a hiking carrier would’ve been easier.","Great place for a beach outing, especially at low tide, we find pretty big crabs buried in the sand hiding from seagulls. There also is a lot of great driftwood to try to build with, and bathrooms within a short walk. Important to know that the close parking is for ADA permits mainly but you can drop people and stuff off then go back and park to walk back to the beach. The walk is almost a hike according to my kids, a few sets of wooden steps and took us about 30-40 mins with an 8 year old with a good attitude.","This park is a gem of Seattle! It’s big enough to feel you’re on a hike in the wilderness, paths are well maintained and you can find trails for all levels. The highlight is the beach and the Lighthouse.","Lots of woods & an area to run your dogs with no cars. Really nice place. Native American museum, a beach & a lighthouse."]

name: Elliott Bay Park
address: Elliott Bay Trail, Seattle, WA 98119, USA
reviews:
["This waterfront location is absolutely stunning. Thoughtfully designed with curated greenery and beautifully manicured grassy slopes, it offers a perfect blend of natural beauty and intentional landscaping.

Separate pathways for walkers and cyclists wind gracefully through the area, adding to its charm and accessibility. Well-placed trees, vibrant flower bushes, and a dedicated rose garden enhance the serene atmosphere.

It’s the kind of place that invites you to slow down and stay a little longer—truly captivating and hard to leave.","This whole stretch of park along Elliott Bay is Seattle at its finest. It's a wide, well-maintained trail that's perfect for getting away from the downtown noise without actually leaving. Whether you're walking, running, or biking, the views are consistently spectacular. You get the Puget Sound, ferries, mountains, and even seals if you're lucky. There are plenty of benches and grassy areas to stop and relax. It’s the city’s front yard, and it's fantastic.","It looks fabulous as it's on waterfront. Curated greenery with manicured grass slops. Separated pathways walkers and cyclists with nice looking curves. Well placed trees, flower bushes and dedicated Rose garden. You will always feel like staying back for a while. It's really intoxicating.","Beautiful trail for a walk. There is a small but lovely rose garden and pocket beach. Plenty of benches throughout the trail. The trail goes until Ursula judkins viewpoint through the rail yard.","Easily one of the best viewpoints in Seattle. On a clear day you can see the majestic Mt Rainier rising above the water in the far horizon. Against that background are container cranes in the port, creating a really interesting contrast between our city and nature. You also get a good view of Seattle on one side, and the cruise ship on the other.
The Elliott Bay Trail passing through the park is an excellent trail to hike or jog, and this corner park is the most colorful (and photogenic) area along the trail. Take your time to appreciate all that you see!"]

name: Taylor Creek & Trail
address: 68th Ave S. &, Holyoke Way S S, Seattle, WA 98178, USA
reviews: ["This is a very nice trail deep in the woods with a great sense of peace and nature. I visited during an early morning and really enjoyed it.There are a few not-so-steep stairs and a couple of wooden bridges so it's a pretty moderate short hike.\n\nMy only complaint is that there are a few intersections but the trails are not well marked so you may get into a dead end. The park isn't that big anyway so it just means you need to turn back and choose another path. If you want to do the little loop as you see on Google Maps, the intersection with lots of short tree trunks is where you want to turn.","Tried out the trail for the first time today and loved it. It's a narrow trail with lots of stairs and some bridges. The trees are gorgeous and the sound of water is very soothing. There were very few people on the trail, but it was also raining, which might be why. There are only a few spots for parking, which might keep the trail from being too populated. Looking forward to returning!","Nice little walk through the woods and over a couple of bridges.  Thoroughly enjoyed the trek.","Good for a short trail right in the middle of the crowded Lakeridge neighborhood. Has couple of wood bridges to explore, a small stream running along. Nothing to complain except the parking place is wet and muddy","Has a cool bridge, a small river, it’s a nice quick hike. I take my dog and she love to walk here"]

name: Burke Gilman Trail
address: NE 93rd &, NE 94th St, Seattle, WA 98115, USA
reviews: ["Wonderful urban trail for hikers, runners, and cyclists. I enjoy running along the trail, both to Golden Gardens and all the way to Kenmore. It is pretty quiet and peaceful in the early mornings especially the segments farther away from the city. For some parts near Lake Union and UW, bikes and pedestrians have separated designated lanes but for the most parts the path is multi-use so pay attention to faster traffic.","Lovely paved trail. We’ve been on it a few times and it’s well-used by cyclists, joggers, and walkers.","It’s a good bike path, but there are some pot holes/roots sticking through concrete that make it hard to navigate around sometimes. Also, some of the bikers think they own the path and will zoom past you going extremely fast with no warning.","Longest bike/pedestrian path through the north Seattle neighborhoods. It crosses busy streets every 10 blocks or so. Even though there are crosswalks and lights there is still a risk from cars turning straight into the path. More than once I was almost hit by drivers who didn't look, didn't stop. If you're a cyclist, be prepared to do a lot of shouting, to announce your approach to pedestrians (On your left!) and at drivers, keeping your hands over the brakes at all times. Sometimes dog walkers let their dogs suddenly cross into the oncoming path, and hitting the brakes hard to avoid an innocent dog is just one of the many hazards along this path.","It's a beautiful hiking place. I just loved the quietness and the beautiful trees"]

name: Grace Cole Nature Park
address: 16735 30th Ave NE, Lake Forest Park, WA 98155, USA
reviews:
["I came here because I was feeling down and I thought it was going to be an easy 36 min walk from home but I was wrong! I enjoyed every minute of it and the whole park was by myself, I did not encounter a single person until I was leaving. I crossed paths with a couple deers when I got to an area where it says to keep out, I wanted to get a little closer to take a photo of the deers but i didn’t want to get in trouble, I also heard something walk on top of the hill of that area and it wasn’t a deer, it was walking like a person but i couldn’t see what it was I only saw the humanly figure or shadow.","Got my cardio! Steep groomed trails with rest benches & a nice creek. Lots of birds and animals - including something quite big rustling around in the bush that I couldn't see! Not too big, took (slow) me 30 minutes to climb the hill and back down, but what a beautiful patch of NW forest. 🐶 friendly & very clean.","Very pretty park with a decent sized hill and a few benches. It’s the perfect size for a weekday walk with the dogs. There are a lot of families and everyone has been friendly whenever we go here.","My favorite local park, it's a wonderful place to explore. It's in the process of being restored to a more natural habitat. Nice trails to walk.","Out of the way, accessible only by car (if you don't reside within a mile) small park in LFP.

Split in two adjacent portions, the south side  has narrow dirt trails, out and back, uphill climb with park benches spread out for rest and nature contemplation. The north side has a board walk to the Brookside Creek head water where beavers and ducks reside.

Wonderful 'cozy' passive recreation park for small kids and adults. Is currently hosting a Summer STEM Story Walk sponsored by the city and BnBuilders.

Limited parking lot, street parking."]

name: Frink Park
address: 398 Lake Washington Blvd S, Seattle, WA 98144, USA
reviews: ["Such a wonderful local park with trails that are never busy and lots of hidden spots to hammock or just relax. There are hidden trails not listen on the maps that lead to cool clearings, old bridges, and huge trees to climb.\n\nThe winter photos are from that insane snow day in 2019– many locals including us got to ski in the park!!!","This is an excellent neighborhood park with great trails with multiple entry points. There are wooden and concrete steps and bridges. No restroom facilities. There is an ornate bridge over the creek that runs through the middle of the park. Great urban vegetation. Tons of elevation gain for a small city park. Not much in the way of parking.","Love Frink Park. This place not only make you feel like you're out in the middle of nowhere. It is indeed a good trail for running or strolling along. I love being on this trail during Autumn.  You can hear and see the birds are singing, the leaves are dazzling, the trees are swerving, the coziness of autumn colors, the sunlight sunshining thru each leaves and the refreshing air breezing thru your face. Ahhhh Nature! Lol","Right next to Leschi Park, Frink Park consists mostly of woods and trails, with Lake Washington Blvd running through it. The trailheads aren't very easy to locate and the different trails are not marked inside the park. Since the park isn't huge, you won't really get lost either, you'll probably just need to walk some extra distance to get to where you want to go.","I passed by this park on my Sunday jog. Looks like a nice urban oasis."]

name: Angle Lake Park Nature Trail
reviews: ["A quaint path I found by this huge park next to the lake.  It was so nice n quiet til some junkie rolled thru screaming nasty words with kiddos around got them thinking it's OK to act like that.  I understand he didn't have all the wires up there working but come on I still think we know right from wrong no matter what frame on mind we all in.  That is ALL. Oh plz keep your jabs at me about mental health comments to yourself.  I'm already on that boat with ya.  Have a beautiful day ❤️","This trail definitely exceeds expectations! It has some well-maintained lake-front spots, the gravel trails on the hill are fairly nice, and the paved main strip parallel to the lake is smooth but short. Can access from a connecting path just north of the Marriott. (If you're coming from the Angle Lake Station, this is the best place to access.) Connects to the larger angle lake Park. (See maps.)","Nice park and lovely (but short ) nature trail. We were pleasantly surprised by this little park, very nice and was a short walk from our hotel :-)","Connected by a paved walkway through a narrow strip of property along the waterfront, this nature trail is a short walk south from the main part of Angle Lake Park. A dozen trees have small educational labels. As well, there are other signs along the rough dirt trail loop as it ascends ~20ft up away from the water.","Nice park with a beautiful view of Angle Lake. Pleasant place to walk with trees and flowers. Plenty of covers and grills to cook food. Grassy lawn to lay out in the sun."]

name: Central Park Trail
address: 2150 S Norman St, Seattle, WA 98144, USA
reviews: ["A peaceful park sitting inside a community, you can see the neighborhoods from walking along the trail, it will be a good walk.","Oh man this is the best trail I have ever hiked","Nice walk :) enjoyable","Large and clean.","Nice field for soccer"]

name: Myrtle Edwards Park
reviews:
["This is one of the best urban parks in the country. It's not a "beach" in the sandy sense, but a long, beautiful shoreline trail that gives you some of the most iconic views of Puget Sound and the Olympic Mountains. The path is split for walkers and bikers, which is a huge plus. It’s perfect for a long walk, a run, or just sitting on a driftwood log and watching the ferries go by. It connects seamlessly to the Olympic Sculpture Park, making it a great way to combine art and nature. A top-tier city park.","This beautiful park is perfect for enjoying the outdoors. It features a small beach, plenty of grassy areas for picnics or lounging, and a jogging and biking path that runs along the bay.

This park is half of what used to be Elliott Bay Park and is an amazing spot for taking photos.","Beautiful long narrow park along the waterfront near downtown Seattle, west of the Sculpture Park and far more impressive.   It somehow is not very well known among many Seattlites.  It has a long walkway and bike paths, amazing views of the city as well as the Olympic Mountains and Mount Rainier - on a clear day.  Best enjoyed in the summer as it can be dark, windy, and cold other times of the year.","This beautiful park is an ideal destination for anyone looking to enjoy the outdoors, offering a peaceful atmosphere with a variety of amenities, including a small beach area perfect for relaxing by the water, expansive grassy spaces that are great for picnics, lounging, or simply soaking up the sun, and a scenic jogging and biking path that winds along the edge of the bay, providing both recreational opportunities and picturesque views.

Formerly part of what was once known as Elliott Bay Park, this area now stands as a remarkable spot on its own—an excellent location for capturing stunning photos thanks to its waterfront setting, natural beauty, and thoughtfully maintained surroundings.","The best park with water access and beaches in the downtown area. It stretches quite long to the north enabling nice long walks along the shoreline. In clear days you can even see Mt Rainier from here! It neighbors with Olympic Sculpture park making this whole area a highlight of the day!"]

name: Golden Gardens Beach
address: Seaview Pl NW, Seattle, WA 98117, USA
reviews:
["First time visiting Golden Gardens Park--and wow, what a gem! It feels like one of Seattle's best-kept secrets, especially for locals who know where to go for some peace by the water. The beach is absolutely stunning, with sweeping views of Puget Sound and the Olympic Mountains in the distance. Whether you're into sunbathing, swimming, beach volleyball , or just strolling along the shore, there's something for everyone here.

One of the best parts is the convenience--there's a good amount of free parking, which is a rare treat in Seattle! Just try to come early, especially on weekends or holidays, as it can get busy.

The vibe is laid-back and family-friendly, with picnic areas, fire pits, and lots of space to relax. We had such a great time and can't wait to come back.

Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy a beautiful day by the ocean. Cheers!","On a sunny Sunday’ afternoon with cool cool winds touching you, sounds of water’ & kids enjoying at beach with lots of activities’ The Perfect Natural Setting for a family outing to rejuvenate 🎷🎺🎺🎵","Great beach. On Jan 1 a lot of crazy people take the plunge and they are all super happy!!?","A beautiful and spacious beach, one of the city’s best","Great beach with a bathroom and a small cafe for those who need a drink, food or ice cream. Lots of space. If you go far to the right, there will be less people abs more options for shade.

There are also several picnic 🧺 areas with barbecue grills

There’s also a nice big grass field for kites 🪁 so bring one if you have one"]

name: Ravenna Park Picnic Shelter #1
reviews: ["Nice park 👍 alot to do, plenty of room for many different groups to have a good time,  a picnic, throw a football 🏈, kick a soccer ⚽ ball. Lots of trails to go on walks. Lots of people jog here. Over all very fun no matter what you want to do, this park has something for you! 👍","In community park just blocks from UW campus. Clear walking trails. Two different play areas for young kids. The bathrooms by the sundial play area were closed and the womans bathroom and portable bathroom by the other play area were closed due to vandalism.","It is a nice place to relax at, and it is a good location to smoke dabbs at too.","Nice and quiet park","Great place to play"]

name: Marina Park Picnic Area
reviews: ["This is by far my favorite place in Kirkland. Lovely view, amazimg people. Lots of stuff to do around here and all the boats and events going on all the time. I try to come and walk around at least a few times a week. Lovely.","Beautiful Marina park in Downtown Kirkland. Plenty of shops and restaurants just a block away. Enjoy the nicest sunsets","Visited from Texas. Beautiful place for a picnic! The Yachts, water, view of the area was a great experience!","A beautiful spot.. can be a bit windy though and no shade","Awesome beach park. Really enjoyed."]

name: Alki Beach Park
address: 2665 Alki Ave SW, Seattle, WA 98116, USA
reviews:
["Winter and windy.  It's a unique beach.  There is sand.  It's not a mud flat like most of the beach along the sound.  At high tide, the western end of the beach is under water, but the east beach has sand. At low tide, it's got to be a wide sandy beach.  Of course, that means on summer days, it's busy and people are everywhere. The west end, where we spent more time, has a seawall from the early days when excursions came for the weekend or the summer to enjoy the beach and the several bathhouses.  Stairways down to the sand, now overlaid with sand have bronze plaques with stories from the earlier years.","While visiting Seatle I had the opportunity to visit Alki Beach Park during the off season in September.  The weather couldn't be nicer and crowds were minimal. Alki beach park offers a georgeous walkway as well as a view of Seatles skyline. Its a photographers dream come true with the skyline  ocean shops boats restaurants and shops nearby to take plenty of photographs of. Ample wildlife also gives cause to enjoy the park as the park website credits spotting seals as well as other wild  life to enjoy. The beach is clean and well kept. Parking is available alongside the street.","I adore Alki Beach Park for many reasons. The serene ambiance envelops visitors in peace and tranquility, allowing for a deep connection with nature that always lifts my spirits.

With its stunning views of the Seattle skyline across the water and the gentle sound of waves lapping against the shore, it's a picturesque escape from the hustle and bustle of city life.

I often take long walks along the sandy beach, breathing in the fresh ocean air and soaking up the beauty of the surroundings. Whether it’s the vibrant sunsets painting the sky or the joyful laughter of families enjoying a day outside, every visit to Alki Beach Park feels revitalizing.

Whenever I find myself in Seattle, this exquisite spot is always high on my list of places to spend quality time, indulging in the natural beauty and peaceful atmosphere it offers..","Alki Beach is such a wonderful place to spend a day! The promenade is perfect for walking, biking, or just enjoying the ocean view. The beach itself is clean, spacious, and offers stunning views of downtown Seattle and the Olympic Mountains. You can grab a coffee, sit by the water, and watch the ferries go by — it’s so peaceful and relaxing. There are plenty of restaurants and cafes nearby, and the sunsets here are absolutely magical. Definitely one of the best spots in Seattle to unwind and enjoy nature!","Found this beach Park in Seattle with good views of the Olympic Mountains, and the slight skyline. The walking / biking path is ideal, the sand is perfect for relaxing, and there’s plenty of places to grab a bite nearby. Great spot for a sunset stroll or a low-key beach day."]

name: Picnic Point Park
address: 7231 Picnic Point Rd, Edmonds, WA 98036, USA
reviews:
["I took my five-year-old here last night for the very first time. We had an absolute blast.. parking was easy and using the overpass over the railroad tracks was phenomenal. The beach was stinky because of the salmon season but, we still had an incredible time, throwing rocks into the water.

I would definitely come here again","So beautiful, I can’t believe it’s taken me this long to visit. Easy access and amazing views. My son also loved seeing the train go by. A great spot to go for easy access to the Sound.","Beautiful beach with train and awesome views. Ramps for wheelchair or walker accessibility but the incline is such that someone would need to either be really strong, have strong help, or have a power chair. Uploading pics to show what I mean. Parking is limited but didn't take me too long on a Sunday evening with 80+ degree weather. Recommended. ♥️","Picnic Point Park is a relaxing and beautiful spot. We visited on a cloudy day, and it wasn’t crowded at all. Loved the peaceful beach walk and the views.

However, there was a lot of seaweed along the shore, which made it tough to get close to the water without waterproof shoes. Still, it’s a great place to visit especially in the summer!","One of Puget Sound secret pocket park tucked away from the busy city of Edmonds, Mukilteo, Everett and Lynwood.

The park has ample parking for busy days. No bathroom but the have a portable toilet honeysuckle.   Wheelchair accessible, and because of the ramp you can haul in your picnic or fishing gears.

It's very quiet. A nice spot for day camping. Not much tables, so bring your own. No water in the area. I will not trust that creek water coming from the residence uphill.

Overall, a relaxing spot to end you busy day."]

name: Picnic Shelter #3
reviews: ["Great for after hour partys. Not that gen z has partys anymore. But they had to make the fireplace smaller so we couldn't shove whole pallets in there anymore. Its really something special to see flames shooting out of the top of the chimney. Bathroom located directly adjacent so you can get all that beer out. Great place to be!","I personally Love this beach, my new favorite. Great parking, easy access to the beach, various wooded trails leading down to a spectacular beach. Great for taking photos.","This is a fine example of what makes West Seattle so great. Also right next to the public 🚻 and close to parking. You can reserve through City Parks","This shelter is in the best location of all the shelters. It is right on the beach whereas some are up on the hill in the trees. It is also very close to clean public bathrooms. We brought firewood as all the shelters have fireplaces and we built a fire for marshmallows. Ideal place for a large gathering. People can hang out in the shelter or spread out to the adjacent picnic table in the sun.","Beautiful walk on a sunny day. Located on the shore of Puget Sound on the west side of Lincoln park. Go for a stroll or have a picnic. This is a great spot. Watch the routine of the Washington State ferries as they load and unload at Fauntleroy. If you take the short walk up the hill you can explore Lincoln park in depth."]

name: Matthews Beach Park
reviews:
["Great views, friendly neighborhood. Plus the water is good for swimming, it stays shallow for a long way out and is not too cold. They have bathrooms, picnic tables, and even a BBQ Grill. During the summer they open up the indoor shower and changing rooms. It's one of my favorite parks. About 15 minutes drive from Shoreline.","Definitely my favorite park in a very long time! It’s a large place with a good sized parking lot, including five-six disabled parking spots that all have unloading zones.

There’s two gender neutral bathrooms that are single stall, but incredibly large. This is a very disability accessible place. The doors are heavy, but wide. They’re incredibly filthy though, and there’s been no toilet paper a few times I’ve gone so make sure to check before you sit. 😂

There’s a nice strip of beach where you can access the water, and a playground for kids. Lots of grassy areas, mostly flat but there’s also a small hill. I highly recommend this park for picnics and I saw a lot of people on dates there.

There’s picnic tables, built-in charcoal grills, trash cans, and recycling cans. The pathways are smooth and paved, and wide enough for even a motorized wheelchair. Truly my favorite park of all time, the only reason it’s a 4/5 is because the bathrooms truly are a nightmare.","Best park in the area. Good parking and facilities. Wonderful guarded beach area with deep end and diving island and a nice playground. The only downside is how filthy and degraded the bathrooms and changing/shower rooms are. Otherwise best park in NE Seattle","Nice beach! First time experience here was for the Polar Bear Plunge. I would love to visit here for the summer for sure! They have showers and lockers available inside the restrooms. Parking is ample but can get right for events like the plunge. I noticed people just parked all along the street, right squeeze tho since narrow roads. Overall such a beautiful park and beach. The play area has a variety for kids to enjoy.","Cute little gem with lots of trails for strolling or biking. We loved the playground which was a lot larger than I thought when we first arrived. There was a sand pit, and a little tree fort type structure. Plenty of picnic tables around."]

name: Pioneer Square Habitat Beach
address: Washington Street Public Boat Landing, 199 Alaskan Wy, Seattle, WA 98104, USA
reviews:
["It's important to know what this place is before you go. It's less of a "beach" for people and more of a "habitat" for salmon. While it’s a cool project and a great step for the waterfront's ecosystem, it's not really a place to lay out a towel. It’s a small, rocky shoreline tucked between the ferry terminal and the pier. The best part is that it provides public access to touch the water right downtown. A nice, quiet spot for a 10-minute break to watch the ferries, but don't expect a traditional beach experience.","This is a nice little beach access located in the heart of Seattle. Very peculiar. Perfect to watch the sunset or just enjoy some nature while waiting for the ferry. I do not advise swimming here, as it could be dangerous for your health. It is a special spot where you can appreciate the contrast between natural beauty and city architecture. Both very impressive.","This is a nice little spot to enjoy nature while waiting for the ferry. It's relatively quiet and quite peaceful.

Add on to what I started or create your own rock pile 😉

There are a lot of really cool rocks on the beach, too.","Nice waterfront place to stop.  Also great to kill a few minutes if you're early for the water taxis.  It's a great re-use of what would otherwise be a neglected sea wall.  Appropriate plantings, scattered boulders at a good size for sitting and a sloping rocky beach into Elliot Bay.","Super cute little rocky beach with big rocks to sit on. Especially nice when taking a break from work to walk over there. Several kids there having lots of fun!"]

name: Discovery Park Beach
address: Fort Lawton Beach, 122.434422, Seattle, WA 98199, USA
reviews: ["The beach is a highlight of the while Discovery Park area. Has a more sandier (and windier) and stonier (and quieter) sides. And of course the lighthouse!","Discovery Park Beach is a family- and kid-friendly spot that’s perfect for a hike or a picnic. There are easy trails and stair access, making it great for everyone. Along the way, you’ll find wild berries growing—it's so fun to see people picking and enjoying them. We loved listening to the ocean waves and exploring during low tide. Such a peaceful and beautiful place!","This my first visit as I don't live in the city.   I thoroughly enjoyed hiking the loop trail to my South Beach destination.  The trail was well maintained (but needs some work around some stairs).  The variety of different landscapes was an unexpected surprise.\n\nRestrooms are availlable but you may need to search for them - stop at the visitors center to use the facilities when you arrive and then you should be good for a while.","Beautiful place with the beach, light house and trails by the water. I imagine it's even nicer on a beautiful summer day. We parked at the north parking lot and took the north loop to the light house and the south loop back to the parking. There's no parking by the beach unless you just want to pullover to take some photos.","Absolutely stunning! Discovery Park is a breath of fresh air right in the heart of Seattle. With its incredible views of the Olympic Mountains and the Puget Sound, it's like stepping into another world. The trails are perfect for a peaceful walk or an invigorating hike, surrounded by nature's beauty. It's a must-visit for anyone looking to escape the city hustle and immerse themselves in tranquility. Truly a gem!"]

name: Waverly Beach Park
address: 633 Waverly Park Way, Kirkland, WA 98033, USA
reviews:
["Very scenic and tranquil park with amazing views (including that of Mount Rainier), and really cute park benches for just relaxing and soaking in the views! Weather was perfect when we visited in early June and we got an amazing view of Mount Rainier from here. The whole place was really bustling as well which was great, and the restrooms were clean and well-maintained with well replenished supplies (toilet paper, hand soap)!

Overall a rather small but nice, well-maintained and cozy park worth visiting! You can also consider incorporating this as part of a walking trail with Heritage Park and Marina Park — which are both also free public parks with equally scenic views located nearby! 😌","Great, tucked away, secluded, cute little park with a sandy beach, designated swimming area, green area, picnic area, publicly available char coal grills, children's playground and restrooms. Everything one can ask for. It gets crowded during the hot summer days.","There is a long pier with ladders for swimming. Water is clean and transparent. If you have kids this place would be perfect for them because of playground. I recommend this beach for family rest, but remember that parking lot not so big. People often leave their car on the road in summer.","Great park with a playground for kids, has a little beach, wooden bridge, grassy area, grill and restrooms. Parking lot next to the park with handycaps spots, upper level and street parking outside the entrance.
Beautiful sunsets 🔥","Beware. Unrealistically restrictive for swimming. We came with our family to enjoy the lake on a hot day. Despite being in the designated swimming area, with a life jacket on, standing right next to my son, with lifeguards on duty, we weren’t allowed to swim beyond a ankle level cutoff 1 meter from the shore. The water was ankle depth for my 5 year old son and the shallow area was covered in algae. We are very safety conscious individuals, but the lifeguards only care about liability, not about safety."]

name: Chism Beach Park
reviews: ["A wonderful park. It has everything you need for both activity and relaxation. A beach, a park, a small trail, and a beautiful view of the lake.","Pretty view. Super clean park. Nice public restrooms. Very quiet. It's not suitable for trails or walking.","One of the best beach parks while I was growing up in the area. Usually less-trafficked than other local beaches as it is slightly tucked away commanded to Enatai or Newcastle. Lost of open green space, playground, and large dock for swimming.","Beautiful little park that’s hidden away from the main roads. Lovely little playground and walking pathway with a small little beach. Gorgeous views of the water even on a cloudy day. No lifeguard on duty so swim at your own risk (it’s pretty cold)! It is disability friendly having a separate parking lot closer to the park. The regular parking lot is at the top of a hill and can be a bit of a hike down and up to and from the park.","One of my fave parks in bellevue! So many nice areas to sit and soak up the sun at! Lots of parking and they have bathrooms as well! Life guards are here too. There seems to always have a party going on so don’t expect to have the one picnic table free for you lol, they only have one! Other than that, we will def be back! :)"]

name: Pritchard Beach Bath House
reviews:
["My family has been coming here several times per summer, for years. There are plenty of lifeguards on duty, and the vibe is communal. Parking can be tricky, but if you get there early you'll typically be fine. Otherwise there is a lot of nearby street parking! Bathrooms are typical, showers do work, and there are routine patrols by SPD. We've always felt safe there. Love that the lifeguards do their best to keep the dock clean.","December 13, 2012
I alywas enjoy my time at Pritchard Island at 8400 55th Ave S, Seattle, WA 98118, It offer
#Swimming : The beach has a roped-off swimming area, a diving platform, and lifeguards on duty during the in-season, & swimming raft.
#Facilities : There is a bathhouse with toilets and a shower area, they are industrial and not new, but clean and tidy. There’s one picnic table and two benches , otherwise bring a blanket.
#Wildlife : There is decent birdwatching & some #ducks that come by to say hello when you near the water’s edge.
#Parking : There is a decent sized parking lot.
* Other: There are #trails for #walking & #hiking
* It is a quiet beach with views of #sewardpark & #mercerisland","Beautiful place to bring a picnic blanket and chill by the water for an August afternoon.
Heads up: the grassy area is nice in August but it is also relatively dry (and hard) and angled just enough for you to have to work hard to not slide off your picnic blanket.

Good parking, quiet area, lifeguards on duty (see picture for more info), and great view of Mercer Island.","Pritchard Beach is a family friendly Lake Washington beach with a beautiful view of Mercer Island, and tons of naturally tumbled stones. There are lifeguards during the summer during peak hours, and the kids love the swimming platform. The bath house is only open during peak hours, so plan ahead.","Me and my mom and my dog go here every single day and. It's a great place to relax and watch the sunset."]

name: Lowman Beach Park
address: 7017 Beach Dr SW, Seattle, WA 98136, USA
reviews: ["Beautiful place to chill and watch the sunset over the islands","A MUST SEE BEAUTIFUL PARK! This park has beautiful views and almost 360 degree before of water. The ferries going buy! I love taking my break here at this beach and picking up the beautiful shells and rocks. One of Seattles gorgeous beaches","Great little park next to a beach with lots of driftwood. Has some swings and there's lots of rubbish bins so no excuse for just dropping it. The driftwood has been there some time and children obviously love it, making little dens in a couple of places. Parking is free but very limited.","This pocket park features easy access to the waterfront and spectacular Puget Sound views. Only street parking is available and there are no bathrooms or picnic tables. Just two swings comprise the play area. A hand carry boat launch looks like it's designed for high tide. Perfect for a picnic if you bring your own chairs or a blanket.\n\nThe dilapidated but much loved tennis court on the water is gone, but it's unclear what will replace it.","Super chill little local spot. Great to lay out in the grass, check out the rocks and driftwood, or paddle out into the sound. Parking is somewhat limited compared to Lincoln Park, but there's usually a few spots. There's swings and and forts for the kids, but no bathrooms, so keep that in mind. There's also a relatively flat pathway connecting down to Lincoln Park where you can walk along the shore out to the point. Overall, this is one of our favorite spots in West Seattle and we always bring friends and family when they visit."]

name: Pritchard Island Beach
reviews:
["Pritchard Island Beach is a hidden gem in Seattle! It offers a peaceful retreat with stunning views of Lake Washington. The small, sandy shoreline and grassy areas make it perfect for picnics, sunbathing, or just relaxing. The park is clean, quiet, and not too crowded, even on sunny days. It's a great spot for a quick escape from the city bustle. I highly recommend it for a serene lakeside experience!","A cute little beach with roped-off swimming areas, a diving platform, and lifeguards on duty during the in-season. There’s a bathhouse with toilets and a shower area, they are industrial and not new, but clean and tidy. There’s one picnic table and two benches , otherwise bring a blanket. There is decent bird watching and some ducks that come by to say hello when you near the water’s edge.

Parking lot is decent sized, I’ve seen some drug dealing going on which isn’t great but many just sit in their cars quietly and enjoy the view.

Good choice for swimming and bonus for parking and bathrooms!","Loved this small beach. Went there to see Blue Angels air show during Seattle Seafair 2022. Place was not too crowded. If the parking lot is full, there is enough parking around the streets.","Looks alright, have an area for swimming and there is lifeguard service which are good. The not good is a few people feeding the ducks and attracting them stay and poop in the swim area (could be un-hygiene).","It was quiet when I went, likely because the city is not servicing the beach with lifeguards. It was somewhat clean, some trash around but better than other city parks. Good beach to start an open water swim or launch kayaks and SUP."]

name: Richmond Beach Saltwater Park
reviews: ["One of the most beautiful beaches on the sound! Dog friendly with lots of areas to explore. Stunning views. I don't know how I waited so long to come here!","Such a fun park/beach. Lots of beautiful shoreline for kids of all ages to explore and play.\nDogs running around in the water.","If you are a beachcomber, this is one of the best beaches for sea glass north of Seattle! Plus it’s a dog friendly beach, so there are lots of pups running around!\nIncredible views for sunset, too. I don’t know what it is about this beach, but it’s just stunning to watch the sun set under the mountains from here.","I personally love this park and the different access points, families enjoying the beach, people walking their dogs, and views of the Sound. I’ve never used the restrooms or playground so I can’t attest to their cleanliness, but I enjoy walking the beach and the mix of industrial train tracks and rugged PNW views.","This beach park is a great place to visit. Once you arrive at the beach, the scenery is incredible. You can see the Olympic Mountains from the beach on a clear day. I don't know how you couldn't love this place. It's a nice hike down to the beach. Be prepared for a bit of a walk downhill to get to the beach, especially at the top of the lot. There are lots of free parking. The park is always pretty populated, and don't expect to be too alone here. They have good restrooms and garbage cans and a kids playground. Dogs are required to be leashed at all times."]

name: Carkeek Park
address: 950 NW Carkeek Park Rd, Seattle, WA 98177, USA
reviews:
["The name is deceptive. It is not a park. It is an effort in conservation within a city. It is wonderful. We could do with a little more directions and boards.
People visiting It by taxi should take the taxi up to the railway line point.
The railway line is quite picturesque.
Sea view with mountains in the background is marvellous.","A compact alternative to Discovery Park with a stretch of rock beach, rain forest, beaver dam, and kid-friendly educational trail markers explaining the salmon that run upstream through the park in summer and early fall. There's also a playground with a fun Salmon Slide and other apparatus. It was a little muddy and slipper but there's no avoiding that sometimes.","Great place! Quite big and has lots of space for dogs and kiddos to play around. If you want to go to the beach side, I would recommend not taking a stroller because there’s only stairs —or at least what I saw.","Too much traffic here wrecks nature and the natural quiet down inside the gully. What splendor, peaceful hike, or bike into the park now contends with constant streams of cars and suvs.

If saving coho from toxic 6-ppd matters, ought not Pipers Creek highlight where they're missing. Here mostly chum salmon teem, the others gone. Keep car tire particulate far from where runoff flows into the Creek. Hike or e-bike instead into Carkeek.","I love Carkeek Park and used to do trail running here all the time back in my 30s. Now in my 50s it's still a park I enjoy taking my dogs on long walks on the varied trails throughout the park. Entrance from the south has a smaller lot and you go more immediately into wooded trails. If you have kids to pack with you, you'll want to go in on the east entrance on the northern side of the park as some parking is right next to playground/picnic areas."]

name: Calkins Point Sand Beach
reviews: ["After the dog park we come lay in the sun and admire the view. Theres picnic tables and grass with a beach area.  Great for lunch and a walk. There’s a short trail through wetlands","Nice beach. Except the dog owner's that bring their dog to the beach and have them go in the water. They take their dog leash off and let the dog go in the water where people swim at.  The sign says you can't take the leash off too. I had to take my kids out and leave. This park has its own dog section too with water. Not sure why the dog owners do this. Took a picture of an owner who did this. Took the picture alittle bit late before he had the dog off leash but he was one of them.","Peaceful Lakeside area with small beach 🏖️.","Very nice small beach with a Seattle view. There are some benches where you can sit and enjoy the sunset. Clean and quiet.","No sand whatsoever, but a nice small beach with pea gravel. 5 stars if there weren’t so many dogs and their annoying handlers screaming at them. Sand would be great too. Did I mention crying toddlers? Bring noise-cancelling headphones for any peace of mind. This place is actually 3.51 stars."]

name: Me-Kwa-Mooks Park
address: 4430 Beach Dr SW, Seattle, WA 98116, USA
reviews: ["This park was an unexpected gem. The trails are a bit hidden but there’s a narrow path between the shrubs in the back right corner. Once you get through them, there’s a surprisingly beautiful, steep winding trail. Definitely wear thick pants and shoes as there’s nettle along the trails but I’m so glad I found this park.","As it show, different view to Seattle and cruises, sound and birds.\nYou know why people love Seattle which has so many fantastic natural views, spectacle !\nClosed to Alki beach ~","Wanted to try this park out, but the big dog off a leash on a main trail in pictures has me saying absolutely not. 🙁","Quite with plenty of shade. Tide pools all over the place with filled with sea-life for the little ones to explore.","We climbed out of the park at the top of the bluff which I would not recommend to those not able to rappel.  A few more ropes are needed."]

name: Carkeek Park Picnic Area
address: NW Carkeek Park Rd, Seattle, WA 98177, USA
reviews: ["Often crowded on nice days","Carkeek Park Picnic Area is a good area for picnics with good picnic tables.","Gorgeous park, so much wild life","A quiet and wonderful place. The occasional passing train breaks the silence, but even that is lovely."]

name: Picnic Party Seattle
address: 309 S Cloverdale St Unit D30, Seattle, WA 98108, USA
reviews: ["Disappointed overall, everything was pretty except the set up wasn’t done on time, the speaker didn’t have any battery and the Polaroid didn’t work. Also the battery operated tea lights didn’t turn on. I paid extra for flowers and didn’t get them. No one contacted me afterwards to apologize for these items not working. Definitely not worth the over $500 I paid. Definitely not hiring this service again.","Hannah at Picnic Party is absolutely incredible. The whole process is so easy, customizable, and friendly. The picnic we had was absolutely beautiful. Thank you team for making such a special day for our group!","This picnic was everything I could have ever wanted. Hannah was wonderful to work with and the picnic was absolutely perfect! My friends and I had such a wonderful time and they couldn't stop talking about how amazing the set up was. I will absolutely book again for a stress free event!","We had such an incredible experience with Picnic Party Seattle! Hannah and her team were so communicative, creative and kind… everything was perfect and the park was an incredible location for this picnic. My friend celebrated her bachelorette and we chose a picnic to be calm but fun, and she had the best time. Hannah was incredible - everything from scheduling to designing the picnic was amazing. Everyone should do this !!! It was soooo cute and she even provided a speaker, extra little things to make the experience that much better. This is the coolest way to celebrate friends and family.","We had a gorgeous picnic!!!! Britney was an amazing help at planning our experience from afar, as we had our picnic while on a trip from Tennessee. She helped me find a CRAZY GOOD restaurant, I called in the order, and she had it picked up and ready for us with the ADORABLE date night setup! I added the games and flowers options, plus the personalized message board. We played bananagrams plus some cute little date night conversation starter games. It was well worth the price just for Britney’s help alone! She also found us the cutest tiny little beach park with a truly gorgeous sunset view. We will absolutely be doing this again next time we are in Seattle!!"]

name: Washington Park Arboretum
address: 2300 Arboretum Dr E, Seattle, WA 98112, USA
reviews:
["Highly recommend spending some time at the arboretum. It’s gorgeous and so so peaceful. Take a moment to relax and take in all the smells, definitely one my favorite places during my short visit to Seattle.","An oasis of serenity and one of the most accessible and elegantly soothing natural spaces in the city. I have walked through this place multiple times throughout my calmer weekends in Seattle, and every time, it feels new, changing with the seasons and the weather. There are lush groves of rhododendrons and towering copses of redwood trees that reach into the sky. There are relatively easy and simple trails that lead around placid ponds filled with lily pads, and there are steeper paths leading into mysterious groves filled with lush arrangements of trees and creatively ecological art installations. I do not come here as often as I should, but I am never disappointed when I do, and every trip here feels unique.","Went here with the dogs on my day off Tuesday and parked down by the Japanese Garden parking lot. It was relatively empty so finding a spot was no problem. It was actually my first time ever coming here and I thoroughly enjoyed my visit. It was a hot day so it was nice and cool walking amongst all the foliage. My dogs had a  blast smelling all the new smells and walking along the different paths. This is a must visit spot if you're a local like me or a visiting tourist!","It is a botanical garden not to be missed. Walking inside is a great experience. It is very well maintained and has even a unique art sculpture on trees.
There is a winter garden as well.","​I had a great time at this large park on the east side of South Lake Union. It's home to a beautiful Japanese garden and has multiple trails winding through lush greenery. I especially appreciated how clean and well-maintained the paths were, with no muddy sections to worry about.
​Following the trails to the far north side, you can reach the bay and pass directly under a large bridge. Turning left after the bridge leads to a wooden boardwalk trail that takes you out of the park on the west side. Since I took the bus, this was the perfect way for me to exit.
​The park is full of unique flowers and plants with interesting shapes that you don't often see elsewhere, and I took plenty of photos. Just be mindful of one area where a sign warns of nearby wasps."]

name: Center for Urban Horticulture
reviews:
["I had the pleasure of photographing a wedding at the Center for Urban Horticulture this past spring. It is such a beautiful location for an intimate/small wedding. The arboretum is a super cool location for the ceremony and if the weather allows the Goodfellow Grove is a great location for ceremony or even a reception.","There's a short trail around the garden, and then a longer trail that goes out by the water. Very peaceful.

There was a decent amount of parking for a weekday afternoon. There are also bathrooms.","I attended a beginner backyard beekeeping course here. It was such a pleasure to sit under the trees during breaks and explore the trails after class. I saw so many beautiful flowers and had a delightful time soaking in the nature!","BEWARE OF !!DEADLY!! NIGHTSHADE

My bf and I were touring the gardens. We found a plant with beautiful, luscious berries. It looked familiar and then I found a tag on the tree that said “serviceberry”. Since I recently looked at buying some serviceberry plants I figured that was where I recognized them from.

We each ate one berry. Initially sweet the berries quickly turned to an absolutely awful taste that I can’t accurately describe. We immediately spit them out and luckily I had water, which we used to rinse our mouthes. No swallowing. We made it another 100ft and the headache kicked in.

A quick google search confirmed my suspicions that what we had eaten was not serviceberry and another search that it was likely deadly nightshade. We went straight to the staff. They seemed completely apathetic and were wildly unhelpful.

It’s ten hours later and I still have a headache. AS LITTLE AS THREE BERRIES CAN KILL!!! And they were labeled as serviceberries!!!!

I had some previous experiences here that were great, but someone could die from their indifference. Please be careful!!!","One of my couples hosted a wedding here this past June and it was such a fun space. The staff were so kind the day of and super helpful! The tree grove is such a unique area to host dinner. Loved the greenhouse for dancing! - Prologue Events"]

name: Highline SeaTac Botanical Garden
reviews: ["Great nine acres of garden to stroll around in. Can also bring your well manoried dog.\nWinter time less flower blooming going on.\nThey have garden area you can rent to grow your owne garden.","This is SUCH a cute garden!! Very relaxing to walk around and well maintained! It's pretty small, I think I explored the whole thing and took photos in under a half hour, but it's so peaceful to walk around and would be a great place to chat with a friend or relax. There's even a little library stand inside and several benches to sit. Loved the Japanese garden and the Rose garden area even though not a lot was in bloom now (mid-Sep). Still highly recommend! It's free and there's plenty of parking (2 level parking area, just go down the hill)!","Beautiful, free, smells delightful. Japanese garden painstakingly transplanted to make room for a runway. Helps me remember that beautiful things don't happen by accident or stay beautiful without attention.","We had time before our flight so stopped to check out the gardens. The roses were beautiful. We loved walking through the different sections. The dahlias were in full bloom. If you get to SeaTac early it is worth the ten minute drive to spend a half hour or more... And get a walk in before your flight.","I really wanted to like this garden more, but besides the planes flying over every 1-2 minutes and the relatively sparse features,  I felt like this was challenging to enjoy.  Nonetheless, for what it is, the garden would be a welcome spot for travelers seeking a moment of repose before a flight or a casual visitor that doesn't plan to stay too long.  While small, this does have some key features like a small pond, a modest rose garden, and a tiny Japanese garden (that was not active when I was there but lookef like it would be pretty if it was fully decked out). Given its convenient proximity to the Seattle airport, I'd give this another shot."]

name: Carl S. English Jr. Botanical Garden
reviews: ["So fun to visit the locks. And salmon ladder. Great walk thru the gardens. My parents owned the Lockspot during the World Fair in 62. The Govt locks was our back yard. Free admission.","Spent Labor Day weekend with my family at the Ballard Locks and Carl S. English Jr. Botanical Garden in Seattle, such an amazing experience! The locks were fascinating to watch with all the boats passing through, and the salmon ladder was a highlight. The botanical garden was peaceful and beautifully maintained, making it a perfect spot for a relaxing stroll. A wonderful place for families to enjoy both nature and history.","It was a pleasant surprise to find a beautiful botanical garden. We enjoyed the weather, the animals, the trees, plants, and flowers, as well as the sound of the damp water currents; the seagulls and ducks made a lot of happy noises while searching for and finding their meals. The garden was colorful and fragrant, peaceful and refreshing. It will be worth a visit if you ask me.","Carl S English Jr Botanical Garden is a lovely garden to visit with plethora of rich flora, plenty space to sit, walk around, watch sunsets,  go for a run or take your pet for a walk.. If you visit during the fish migratory season you would be able to spot them on the fish watching ladder. And.. if you are in luck, you could find the ballard locks in action, which are fascinating to watch.","It’s a lovely spot for a walk on a beautiful sunny day. The garden is right next to the Ballard Locks, which has an underground salmon observatory. I’ve never seen any salmon passing through, but the locks themselves are an engineering wonder worth visiting."]

name: Pacific Connections Gardens at Washington Park Arboretum
address: 2300 Arboretum Dr E, Seattle, WA 98112, USA
reviews: ["Beautiful circle walkway, spring blossoms with huge trees backdrop. The structure (looks like a pavilion) carries unique designs. Several benches for you to sit down and savor the quietness.","So much plant diversity from around the world! This is a great place for a pleasant stroll, jog, or bike ride; although walking works best, as it let's you explore more by meandering through the many side paths. Even on a beautiful Sunday morning there was plenty of free parking. On the way in there is a building with information, water, and bathrooms.","Wonderful forested park with plentiful trails, both a paved one that circumnavigates the entire park and several gravel trails that criss cross the area. Don’t miss the totem pole just up the trail from the visitor center. Plenty of parking around, as well as ebikes waiting to be used.","My favorite place to go running.  Many excellent running trails for both walkers and runners. The plants are lush and there is something blooming all year round. The smells as you walk though the  Arboretum pleasures the senses. This is a must see if your from out of town, even if it is raining.  Just do like Northwesterners do put on a raincoat and go. Cheers!","Spring in Seattle is my favorite season.  The Arboretum is a great place to stroll, look at nature.  It is very quiet and there are always something to see. I was lucky this year to pop in the Arboretum during Rhododendron season.  It was exquisite."]

name: Kubota Garden
reviews:
["The garden is stunning and beautiful. It's so much bigger than the Japanese garden in the Arboretum in Seattle and very well designed. The fall colors were in full show today and we were very glad we went. Even managed to get a nice shot of the iconic Japanese maple. Parking was slightly challenging but patience helped and by the time we left around 1:30 pm a lot more slots had opened up. Definitely worth a visit and please take the time to donate to ensure the organization can keep the garden accessible to the public.","It's a lovely park. If I lived closer, I'd visit more often. Not too big, but feels both spacious and intimate. So many beautiful areas to appreciate and photograph.","Such a wonderful way to spend a few hours. Hard to believe this is a free (donations encouraged) City run park, bigger and with more trails than even the Japanese Garden within the Washington Park Arboretum. While there aren't too many flowers blooming in the fall, the changing leaves make for spectacular vista. So many ponds and bridges and little creek crossings make this place feel like your own personal hidden garden. Take some snacks and find one of the many picnic spots to relax for awhile. Highly recommend visiting!","If I were a local here, I would definitely come every weekend. There’s so much to do, and the walks surrounded by greenery and fresh air are amazing. I can’t even imagine how beautiful it must be in the springtime.","wanted to go to the Japanese garden this weekend but didn't score free tickets
so came here instead
didn't disappoint! always love bringing guests here, they're surprised with how long it takes to walk thru

highly recommend"]

name: Bellevue Botanical Garden
reviews:
["Escape in Bellevue
​This garden is a true hidden gem! It’s rare to find such a high-quality experience with free admission, and the Bellevue Botanical Garden delivers on every level.
​It’s a Must-Visit ​Winter Magic, Even in the cold, the garden is alive. I was amazed to see so many small flowers blooming despite the winter chill. It’s a testament to how well the grounds are curated.
​Impeccable Maintenance, The trees are stunning and look perfectly "naturally maintained." You get the feeling of being in deep nature without it feeling overgrown or messy.
​Seamless Paths,The trails and sidewalks are beautifully integrated into the landscape. They feel like a part of the natural path rather than a distraction from it, making for an easy and accessible walk.
​Don’t skip the gift shop! It is so cozy and filled with beautiful little finds that are surprisingly affordable with a staff member is incredibly welcoming , her amazing customer service really adds to the overall peaceful and friendly vibe of the place.","Visited this gem on a crisp winter morning! 🌳☁️ The mix of cloudy skies and sunshine added to the charm. Loved seeing friends, families, and kids soaking in the beauty. The park has so much to offer - stunning waterfalls 🌊, scenic nature walks 🌿, and trails to explore 🥾. Don't miss the Ravine Bridge for a breathtaking view! 😍 Facilities-wise, there are plenty of benches to rest, picnic areas for a snack 🍔, and restrooms available too 🚻. A perfect spot to unwind and connect with nature. Highly recommend! 🌟 4.5/5 stars","Nice place to walk around. It wasn't in season so there were not a lot of blooms. If you have spare time and want fresh air it's good. The suspension bridge is neat.","Bellevue Botanical Garden was a wonderful place to visit with my family, especially during the holiday season. The Christmas light plant displays were beautiful and really well done—creative, colorful, and thoughtfully arranged throughout the garden. Walking the paths felt festive without being overwhelming, and it was enjoyable for all ages. The garden itself is peaceful and well maintained, and the lights added a special touch that made the experience feel magical. It was a great way to spend quality time together, take photos, and enjoy the holiday atmosphere. We all left impressed and would definitely recommend it as a family-friendly outing, especially during the Christmas season.","Such a beautiful and peaceful escape right in the heart of Bellevue!
The gardens are perfectly maintained with vibrant flowers, lush greenery, and lovely walking trails. It’s a great place to unwind, take photos, or enjoy a quiet stroll surrounded by nature. The suspension bridge and seasonal blooms are must-sees! Free entry and parking make it even better. Highly recommend for families, couples, or anyone needing a nature break. 🌿🌸"]

name: Streissguth Gardens
reviews: ["Really pleasant network of paths on a steep slope. Lots of foliage and tight corners with a couple benches scattered around. A true neighborhood hidden gem.","Went January 12th, and even in winter it's a lovely city park. There is a big tree down in the upper area, but there are enough paths to get around. I plan on coming back in more seasons","Discovered a “secret” bench here in April and absolutely fell in love with the place. In spring, it feels like you’re stumbling upon a treasure. The paths up near 10th, off the stairs, are probably best explored by the sure-footed in sturdy footwear, however.","Nice family-maintained hillside garden, requires climbing steps and walking along narrow and sometimes steep paths. Out of the way, but worth the trip.","Tiny hillside switchbacked network of garden paths. Pretty overgrown in May, but in a Secret Garden kind of way and very pretty.\nLoud traffic from all angles.\nParking tight on the dead end street, I parked at the top of the hill and walked down."]

name: Seattle Japanese Garden
address: 1075 E Lk Washington Blvd, Seattle, WA 98112, USA
reviews:
["Beautiful spot, especially in fall!

The Seattle Japanese Garden is absolutely stunning this time of year, the fall colors are vibrant, with crimson and gold leaves reflecting beautifully in the large central pond. The garden is peaceful and very easy to walk around, making it perfect for a short, relaxing visit. The pond is full of graceful koi fish, and the surrounding trails are just as lovely ,
easy to access and great for a quiet stroll.

Parking is decent-sized but tends to fill up quickly, so it’s smart to have a few nearby options saved. General admission is around $10, and it’s totally worth it for the calm, beauty, and serenity you get. You can walk through the main garden in about 10–15 minutes, but it’s the kind of place you’ll want to linger.

A must-visit spot for anyone in Seattle, especially in autumn","Bite-sized, carefully curated Japanese garden with delightful spring and fall color. ABSOLUTELY NO DOGS ALLOWED.

Plentiful benches. ADA path goes halfway, the other half of a loop trail has stairs and rock stepping stones. This is a great place to go with your aging parents, impatient children, or out of town guests.

Beautiful views from all directions. Even on a busy day (ie fall) you can always find a quiet corner to sit in or get a photo. Parking any where in the arboretum in the fall is tricky, you may have to wait for a spot to open up. Try going a little later in the day or in the morning for best options.

Parking is free. There is an entrance fee of around $10 for adults, with discounts for seniors and children you pay at the front booth, and sometimes there’s a bit of a line.

Bathrooms are clean and accessible.

Make sure you check the art gallery near the ticket house.

If you’d like a longer walk, you prefer a wilder park, or you’d like to bring your dog, try the Kubota Gardens in Seattle near Beacon Hill.","Such a beautiful place to go visit!! Unfortunately not much in bloom when we went, but nonetheless watching the koi and seeing such beautiful places to sit were very nice!","Visited the Garden on a sunny but chilly fall day. Some trees had already turned deep red while others were still changing. It’s a small, well-kept space where you can wander with a pamphlet to learn the meaning behind each element in the garden, or just take a short, peaceful walk surrounded by maple trees. A calm, reflective spot to enjoy the season.","I think the Seattle Japanese Garden beautifully captures the essence and aesthetic of a traditional Japanese garden. I can't justify giving it 5 stars because it's a very small garden (the Portland Japanese Garden is 4X the size of this one), but I don't want to travel to Portland. FYI restrooms are not gender-neutral and only there's only one location at the entry, but since the garden is small it's not that long of a walk to access them.

We visited the garden during regular hours, and we also attended a Full Moon Viewing event, with dancing and koto music, in September 2025. I recommend attending one of these if you can get tickets. Nice chill vibe with (mostly) quiet and respectful attendees."]

name: Volunteer Park Conservatory
address: 1400 E Galer St, Seattle, WA 98112, USA
reviews:
["Wonderful to visit even in winter. Fantastic in the spring when all the trees bloom! However the conservatory ticketing machine isn't the most intuitive to use, even with instructions taped on it. I had to help out two separate parties since there was no one manning the machine or the gift shop.","A true gem of an experience! You don't have to enjoy plants to enjoy this setting and if you do enjoy plants you're really in for a treat! The biodiversity really brings back the awe of nature.","Great place to visit and take a stroll in the park. Recommended going in the colder weather as it is very warm inside, feels nice when the outside temperature is chilly. $6 for entry is fair.","This conservatory has beautiful and unique plants.  Exhibits include seasonal flowering plants, succulents, citrus trees and other plants that require a warmer greenhouse environment.

Admission is inexpensive ($6) and paid through a kiosk.

Parking can be limited.","What a wonderful Seattle gem.

I always enjoy my visits here, they are short and sweet and I am always over the moon to be surrounded by their beautiful plants.

This is a Victorian styled building located at Volunteer Park. It is filled to the brim with many varieties of orchids, ferns, pitcher plants, bromeliads, air plants and more. I fell in love with Staghorn ferns after seeing them here for the first time years ago.
I also quite enjoy their cactus garden.

They do have a sweet little gift shop with reasonably priced live plants, I managed to leave with only two of them today.

We came specifically for the conservatory today but stay and enjoy all the other amazing things at Volunteer Park."]

name: Vista Gardens
reviews:
["Vista Gardens is a gem and a feast for the senses. We attempt to go for every season so that new nooks can be explored and the landscape can be a reminder of nature alive. It is a haven to immerse yourself in the quiet plantings and to enjoy the vista!
I love to visit gardens that is someone's vision and his/her personal stamp on nature. Vista gardens never disappoints!","We very much enjoyed ourselves at the Vista Gardens. Recommended!
The peaceful vibe there is good for the « Soul » and the view at the top of the garden is quite wonderful. Not to be missed!
Obviously the family who established the garden  all those years ago knew what they were doing leaving a botanical local legacy for later generations to enjoy.
Larry Hicks
Bremerton resident","I came by myself last week and the guy that came up to greet me was really nice. I believe he’s the owner, really sweet and welcome man. Lovely place to take pictures with an awesome view! I loved it when I visited it. I had cute pictures that I was really happy with ☺️","We had a great self- guided tour of the gardens in mid summer. So much to see and a lovely place to hang out for a while.  Interesting to see so many plants, many of them doing well without built in irrigation. I like the emphasis on sustainable gardening. A large collection of evergreens. It is a garden on a steep hillside, so need to be prepared to navigate that. My hiking sticks were helpful. A chat with the Director both before and after our tour was helpful to get oriented, and to ask our questions.","Garden was amazing.   can't wait to go back another season.  does have a lot of stairs on the trails.  worth the hike to top to see incredible view."]

name: Japanese Maples
address: J. A. Witt Winter Garden, Azalea Way, Seattle, WA 98112, USA
reviews: ["The place is filled with trees, making it shady and perfect for relaxing. As for the Japanese garden, there are many maple trees, but the greenery is rather overgrown.","Very beautiful place.  Cost 10$ but its worth it.","Beautiful maples!","Epic! Incredible place to relax, have a walk, or take pictures! It does involve walking, so bring shoes that will accommodate gravel and a bit of slippery grass/mud. The area is generally well maintained, but there are a lot off people visiting it daily. Parking is at a premium, but the end result is worth it.","Had a great experience at somewhere I was just driving by. The staff were super friendly and let me pay on my way out after some card reader issues. The garden is so well kept and its very peaceful."]

name: Arboretum Foundation
address: 2300 Arboretum Dr E, Seattle, WA 98112, USA
reviews: ["The Graham visitor center has a great gift shop, with books, local art and fantastic greeting cards. Stop by on your next visit to the park.","Beautiful place to go for a walk and take some pictures. It does get crowded on weekends and good sunny days and you will have to drive around to get a parking spot. Lot of flowers in bloom right now and a good place to get portrait shots","I absolutely loved the Washington Park Arboretum. The walk was beautiful & the azalea’s were in full bloom. It was a highlight to my trip in Seattle.","The flowers were so pretty!","We toure part of the arboreutum grounds with a guide. We used the tram by... appointment.  You need to make apointment. Tram   comes  there( to gift shop area )to load  the people.   Arbouretum trees and shrubs  planted from all around the world."]

name: Seattle Chinese Garden
reviews:
["I just live walking through the oaths at this place. Whether in daylight or by evening, the paths with their current lighting, provided by the Astra Lumina event organizers, creates a magical place to stroll through. By day, the Chinese Garden paths offer a tranquil beauty and contemplative space to re-center, collect one's thoughts amd feelings, and come back into connection and alignment with their being. Truly an urban gem!","This was our 2nd time going to Astra Lumina and it did not disappoint! It’s beautiful and this year they added some enhancements including a cute concessions area to get beverages (beer, spiked cider and hot chocolate, and nonalcoholic drinks) and snacks (full-size bags of popcorn, Pringles, Cup-O-Noodles, nuts and trail mix). They also had a cute “hall of lights” at the exit as well as a cute photo opp area. The whole experience was so much fun.

We went on Christmas night with friends and it was a perfect activity for families and friends. The paths are wide and flat so easy enough for strollers and wheelchairs to navigate, too. It is dark but that enhances the beauty of the light displays. You can easily see all 9 installations in 30 min. or if you’re like us, you can take your time and leisurely spend 1.5 hours walking through all the exhibits. They don’t allow backtracking (although no one really enforces it) so, we took our time. I highly recommend going to this immersive exhibit! Also, it’s at the Seattle Chinese Garden so, it’s nice to be in the gardens 🤓","You have to go see this place!! It was breath taking!!!! I love that fact that they have benches that you can sit cuddled and watch the different lights!!! They also post how long each light show is. We were here for 2hrs and didn't even realize it!! One of the best dates nights ever!!!!

I never had coco and liquor before that was nice.","Beautiful garden. Nice trails. Christmas time light show. North area of South Seattle Community College of parking lot.","Beautiful garden!! The fall colors look good, and the speakers etc for Astra lumina are covered with moss to look more natural.

Reb in the office was very friendly and helpful 😁"]

name: Heronswood Garden
reviews:
["Cool drizzly day but lots of winter color scenes to enjoy. Many many evergreen shrubs and trees and the Renaissance Garden is filled with evergreen ferns. Ducks on the pond. Staff are warm and friendly, and share knowledge about various plants.","🪷 Beautiful botanical garden with defined walkways so you can wander around at your leisure. It was a very peaceful and calming experience. They also have a cute gift shop with coffee and tea, and an educational center so you can learn about some of the varieties of botanicals that they have in the gardens.

🅿️ Parking - there's parking in front of the main building, it's mostly gravel. I went on a weekday so there were plenty of spaces available.

🚻 There's a nice facility at the main building.

🎟️ $15. Well worth it.","A beautiful place to visit! The gardens were well kept and diverse. I was impressed by the rock garden filled with cacti and yucca. A day visit was only $15 and a membership is $60. The staff were kind, helpful, and informative. The gift shop was also full of good finds!","Lovely day to be on the Kitsap Peninsula! Probably my favorite garden I’ve found in the PNW. So many beautiful specimens. I would like to visit during the winter months too!","DJing for a wedding a Heronswood was lovely! The square garden setting is so beautiful and smelled like a florist shop! Some things to note: The south corners of the garden have the best shade during the summer afternoons. Getting things level on the flagstones isnt terrible. The tent area is lovely and there's a power outlet in the corner. I would prefer to be set up as close to the dance floor as possible and not shoot sound over tables, but it worked out OK. Load in/out is was pretty simple with a cart, and not too far. Lighting inside the tent was very lovely and not too bright. My uplights started to pop around 8pm (early August). The coordinator with the venue was awesome, and I would love to work here again!"]

name: Parsons Gardens
address: 650 W Highland Dr, Seattle, WA 98119, USA
reviews:
["Parsons Gardens offered the perfect spot for our wedding ceremony! We loved the romantic willow tree for the setting. The garden seems small but is actually quite large and fit 75 people with lots of extra space. There are nice winding paths around the main meadow that offer lots of locations for photos.

The weather was absolutely perfect in July and the beautiful flowers were still in bloom. The vibrant garden made some epic photos, and we were able to spend less on flowers.

We initially booked from 4-7pm, but once it was the month of our date we were able to also book 3-4pm, which was very helpful for extra set up time. There were also easy and obvious spots for the buses to drop people off.

Over highly recommend!!","A very quiet, peaceful garden tucked away near the intersection in the Queen Anne area. I heard birds chirping melodically in the background, and the trees offered just enough shade to make it feel like you’re being gently hugged by nature. It seems to be a regular spot for local residents to walk their dogs, adding to its calm, neighborhood charm. I’d definitely come back here for a cute little picnic.","Beautiful surprise in the city….. found it by accident and it was so worth wandering through it","Wonderful place a wedding ceremony!! Booking was easy through the Seattle Parks website and park was well maintained. Only thing was the portable toilet that was supposed to be there for our guests had a lock on it and the park worker had no keys and no one to contact. Thankful this was a quick ceremony so it was alright.","The garden looks pretty ordinary to me. It's quiet but doesn't give you the sense of seclusion since it's rather small. I guess you have to visit at the right time of the year (and of the day) to see the best of it because some other people's pictures looks great. For me, I'd rather take the break across the street with a view of the bay."]

name: Pike Place Urban Garden
reviews: ["Beautiful place to be with your family","A lovely little gem tucked behind Pike Place Fish Market. Worth checking out! Very few crowds","Thanks to people who take care of this garden. We enjoyed it so much. Beautiful and peaceful to get away a little from the crowd.","Great place to relax for a bit. We brought our ellenos greek yogurt to enjoy in peace. It was also a good place for our toddler to walk around.","Away from the hustle-bustle of the market. Here you can have an amazing view of the pier, and the sea."]

name: Graham Visitors Center
address: 2300 Arboretum Dr E, Seattle, WA 98112, USA
reviews: ["Visited for a long walk through the arboretum starting at the Visitors center. A good amount of parking on a dry Saturday afternoon. We went towards the water to cross the boardwalk section, highly recommend!","Visited the Arboretum starting with the Visitor Center on November 3, the fall colors created a paradise so close to home! There is a lot of volunteer love, feeling of peace coupled with creative designs by humans and nature herself! Unbelievable that this place is in the heart of the city!","The Arboretum is one of Seattle’s treasures. The visitor center is friendly, with a good collection of books and interesting crafts. The best thing about it is that the sales benefit the Arboretum Foundation, which maintains and preserves this beautiful place","A nice Visitors Center. The people were extremely helpful while we visited the Arboretum let us store our bags 💼 there and charge our phones as we were spending the day exploring Seattle","Today I enjoyed a guided tour around the Arboretum beginning and ending at the visitor center. There’s an information, desk, restrooms, and a small gift shop. The restroom for out of order, and the water fountain was out of order today but the building was mostly heated to warm up from a cold, wet walk."]
